title: PM Modi to hold discussions with Myanmar President U Min Aung Hlaing in New Delhi tomorrow
summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi will hold discussions with Myanmar President U Min Aung Hlaing in New Delhi tomorrow. The talks will be held on further strengthening the historical and civilisational ties between the two countries. The entire gamut of relations b...
body: Prime Minister Narendra Modi will hold discussions with Myanmar President U Min Aung Hlaing in New Delhi tomorrow. The talks will be held on further strengthening the historical and civilisational ties between the two countries. The entire gamut of relations between Myanmar and India, including security and connectivity, will come up for discussion during the talks. National Security Advisor Ajit Doval called on the visiting dignitary in Delhi this evening. The Myanmar President arrived in Gaya, Bihar, yesterday on a five-day visit to India. He is being accompanied by a high-level delegation comprising several Cabinet Ministers, senior officials and business leaders. This is his first visit to India in his current capacity. The Myanmar President will also travel to Mumbai on Tuesday for business and industry interactions and site visits. Our correspondent reports that Myanmar lies at the confluence of India’s Neighbourhood First, Act East and MAHASAGAR policies. The official visit of President U Min Aung Hlaing to India is expected to further strengthen and deepen the multifaceted relations between the two countries.

title: Women’s cricket: England wins second T20I in Bristol, Three-match series tied 1-1
summary: In Women’s cricket, hosts England defeated India by 26 runs in the second T20 International in Bristol last night. The three-match series is now tied at 1-1. Opting to bat first, England posted 168 for 5 in their allotted 20 overs. For the hosts, Freya Kemp pl...
body: In Women’s cricket, hosts England defeated India by 26 runs in the second T20 International in Bristol last night. The three-match series is now tied at 1-1. Opting to bat first, England posted 168 for 5 in their allotted 20 overs. For the hosts, Freya Kemp played a blistering knock of 39 runs off just 13 balls. For India, left-arm orthodox spinner Shree Charani picked up three wickets. In reply, India could manage only 142 for 9 in their 20 overs. For the visitors, Yastika Bhatia top-scored with 33 runs off 36 balls before retiring hurt. For England, skipper Charlotte Dean was the pick of the bowlers, claiming two wickets at an economy rate of just five runs per over. England’s Freya Kemp was adjudged Player of the Match for her match-winning innings. The third and final T20I of the series will be played on Tuesday in Taunton.

title: Bangladesh: Rohingya crisis at critical juncture as funding shrinks, dependency deepens, says UNHCR
summary: The Rohingya refugee response in Bangladesh has entered what the UNHCR describes as a “fragile phase”, marked by declining international funding, deteriorating camp conditions and growing protection concerns, nearly a decade after the mass exodus from Myanmar....
body: The Rohingya refugee response in Bangladesh has entered what the UNHCR describes as a “fragile phase”, marked by declining international funding, deteriorating camp conditions and growing protection concerns, nearly a decade after the mass exodus from Myanmar. Presenting the 2026 Joint Response Plan, the United Nations and its humanitarian partners have appealed for over 710 million US dollars to support nearly 1.56 million Rohingya refugees and members of host communities. UNHCR Representative in Bangladesh, Ivo Freijsen, said continued instability in Myanmar, coupled with deteriorating conditions in the camps, has made the humanitarian situation increasingly vulnerable. Nearly 1.3 million stateless Rohingyas continue to live in Bangladesh with limited prospects of returning to Myanmar. Humanitarian agencies say livelihood opportunities inside the camps remain severely restricted. According to UNHCR data, only 23 percent of refugee households earned income through cash-for-work programmes last year, while 35 percent had no source of income and depended entirely on humanitarian assistance. Women, children, elderly persons, people with disabilities and around 150 thousand new arrivals since early 2024 are among those most affected by funding shortages. Many of the new arrivals continue to live in overcrowded camps without adequate shelter. The UN has reiterated the need for voluntary, safe and dignified repatriation of Rohingyas to Myanmar, but says progress remains limited.

title: WHO chief calls for reconsideration of travel bans as DR Congo battles Ebola outbreak
summary: World Health Organisation Director-General Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus has urged countries that have imposed travel bans or closed borders in response to the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo and Uganda to reconsider such measures, saying they ...
body: World Health Organisation Director-General Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus has urged countries that have imposed travel bans or closed borders in response to the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo and Uganda to reconsider such measures, saying they can hinder response efforts. Speaking in Bunia in eastern Congo, the epicentre of the outbreak, he said such restrictions can make outbreak control more difficult and undermine transparency and trust, which are essential for saving lives. Tedros said his visit to Bunia is aimed at engaging directly with affected communities and strengthening support for response efforts in the hardest-hit areas. Health authorities in the DRC have reported over 900 suspected Ebola cases and more than 220 suspected deaths, while neighbouring Uganda has confirmed nine cases, including new infections in the capital Kampala. Officials say the outbreak involves the Bundibugyo strain of Ebola, for which there is currently no approved vaccine or specific treatment, although patients can recover with timely and quality medical care.

title: Badminton: India’s Satwiksairaj-Chirag win Singapore Open 2026
summary: In Badminton, Indian men’s double pair of Satwiksairaj Rankireddy and Chirag Shetty won the finals of the Singapore Open tournament today. The Indian pair defeated Indonesian pair of Muhammad Shohibul Fikri and Fajar Alfian 18-21, 21-17, 21-16 in the final mat...
body: In Badminton, Indian men’s double pair of Satwiksairaj Rankireddy and Chirag Shetty won the finals of the Singapore Open tournament today. The Indian pair defeated Indonesian pair of Muhammad Shohibul Fikri and Fajar Alfian 18-21, 21-17, 21-16 in the final match. This win helped the duo to end their two-year title drought. Earlier, the Indian pair defeated world number one South Korea’s duo Seo Seung-jae and Kim Won-ho, who were on a 34-match winning streak, in the semi-final match.

title: President Trump says US is close to reaching nuclear agreement with iran; Warns of military action if talks fail
summary: US President Donald Trump said the United States is close to reaching a nuclear agreement with Iran but warned that military action remains an option if negotiations fail. In an interview with Fox News at the White House, Trump said his administration’s priori...
body: US President Donald Trump said the United States is close to reaching a nuclear agreement with Iran but warned that military action remains an option if negotiations fail. In an interview with Fox News at the White House, Trump said his administration’s priority is ensuring that Iran never obtains a nuclear weapon. Trump claimed Iran had agreed to language barring it from developing or acquiring a nuclear weapon. He said negotiators had expanded the terms under discussion after he raised concerns about Iran potentially obtaining a weapon from another source. President Trump called the talks tough but said progress is happening. He explained that he prefers to reach a peaceful agreement, but he warned that the United States will still take action if its demands are not met Trump argued that Iran was negotiating from a weakened position following US military operations. He also claimed that a successful agreement would allow the Strait of Hormuz to reopen immediately. The latest remarks come as the administration continues efforts to secure a long-term agreement with Tehran following months of heightened tensions in the West Asia.

title: Sri Lanka raises fuel prices across all major categories
summary: Sri Lanka has raised fuel prices across all major categories, with the new rates taking effect from midnight last night. The move comes days after the International Monetary Fund approved the release of about 695 million US dollars under the island nation’s on...
body: Sri Lanka has raised fuel prices across all major categories, with the new rates taking effect from midnight last night. The move comes days after the International Monetary Fund approved the release of about 695 million US dollars under the island nation’s ongoing reform programme. State-owned Ceylon Petroleum Corporation increased the price of 92-octane petrol by 24 Sri Lankan rupees to 434 rupees per litre and 95-octane petrol by 25 rupees to 495 rupees per litre. Auto diesel rose by 15 rupees to 407 rupees per litre, while super diesel increased by 20 rupees to 478 rupees per litre. Kerosene prices were also raised by 20 rupees to 285 rupees per litre. Fuel retailers Lanka IOC and Sinopec Energy Lanka subsequently revised their prices in line with the increase. The price revision follows the IMF’s latest programme review and reflects Sri Lanka’s continued efforts to maintain cost-reflective energy pricing, a key component of the economic reforms undertaken as the country recovers from its 2022 financial crisis.

title: IPL 2026 final: Gujarat Titans and RCB all set for epic title clash today
summary: In IPL Cricket, Gujarat Titans will be up against defending champions Royal Challengers Bengaluru in the final of the 2026 season at Narendra Modi Stadium in Ahmedabad today. The match is scheduled to begin at 7:30 PM. Akashvani correspondent reports that Guja...
body: In IPL Cricket, Gujarat Titans will be up against defending champions Royal Challengers Bengaluru in the final of the 2026 season at Narendra Modi Stadium in Ahmedabad today. The match is scheduled to begin at 7:30 PM. Akashvani correspondent reports that Gujarat Titans and Royal Challengers Bengaluru will look to make the most of their strengths in the final. Gujarat Titans have beaten RCB at this venue earlier this season, but Bengaluru will draw confidence from having won their maiden IPL title at the same ground last year. RCB have enjoyed an impressive campaign and enter the final after a three-day break. Their batting has been one of the strongest in the tournament, with Virat Kohli leading the charge. In the bowling department, Bhuvneshwar Kumar and Josh Hazlewood have been among their key performers with the new ball. Meanwhile, Gujarat Titans, led by Shubman Gill, secured a top-two finish in the league stage and bounced back from a Qualifier 1 defeat to book their place in the final. The team will once again rely on key batters Gill, Sai Sudharsan and Jos Buttler, while all-rounder Jason Holder has strengthened their bowling attack with important wickets in the second half of the season. Both teams are aiming for their second IPL title. Gujarat won their maiden crown in 2022, while RCB are looking to successfully defend the trophy they won last season.

title: India & Nepal lay foundation stone for hospital building in Manang
summary: India and Nepal today jointly laid the foundation stone for a hospital building in Nason Rural Municipality of Manang District in Nepal’s Gandaki Province. The project is being constructed with the Government of India’s assistance of around 56 million Nepali R...
body: India and Nepal today jointly laid the foundation stone for a hospital building in Nason Rural Municipality of Manang District in Nepal’s Gandaki Province. The project is being constructed with the Government of India’s assistance of around 56 million Nepali Rupees. The foundation stone was laid by Deputy Chief of Mission at the Indian Embassy in Kathmandu, Dr Rakesh Pandey and the Chairman of Nason Rural Municipality, Dhan Bahadur Gurung. Local stakeholders welcomed India’s support and said the facility would improve access to healthcare services in the area. The project will be implemented under the High Impact Community Development Project (HICDP) programme through Nason Rural Municipality. As close neighbours, India and Nepal share close and wide-ranging cooperation across multiple sectors. The implementation of HICDP reflects India’s continued support for Nepal’s development efforts and infrastructure creation in priority sectors.

title: UEFA Champions League: Paris Saint-Germain beat Arsenal 4-3 on penalty shootout in final
summary: In Football, Paris Saint-Germain retained the UEFA Champions League title after defeating Arsenal 4-3 in a penalty shootout in the final in Budapest last night. The match ended 1-1 after extra time. English champions Arsenal took an early lead through Kai Have...
body: In Football, Paris Saint-Germain retained the UEFA Champions League title after defeating Arsenal 4-3 in a penalty shootout in the final in Budapest last night. The match ended 1-1 after extra time. English champions Arsenal took an early lead through Kai Havertz in the sixth minute, but Ballon d’Or winner Ousmane Dembélé equalised from the penalty spot in the 65th minute to force extra time. With the scores still level after 120 minutes, the title was decided on penalties. Arsenal defender Gabriel Magalhaes missed the decisive spot-kick, handing PSG their second successive European crown. The victory makes PSG only the second club in the modern era to successfully defend the Champions League title after Real Madrid. PSG coach Luis Enrique also joined an elite group of managers to win the European Cup or Champions League at least three times. The UEFA Champions League is Europe’s premier club football competition, featuring the top teams from domestic leagues across the continent.

title: WHO announces recovery of five patients from rare Ebola strain
summary: The head of the World Health Organisation (WHO) has announced that five patients have recovered from a rare strain of Ebola during his visit to Bunia in eastern Democratic Republic of Congo, the epicentre of the ongoing outbreak. WHO Director-General Tedros Ad...
body: The head of the World Health Organisation (WHO) has announced that five patients have recovered from a rare strain of Ebola during his visit to Bunia in eastern Democratic Republic of Congo, the epicentre of the ongoing outbreak. WHO Director-General Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus made the announcement while inaugurating a new Ebola treatment centre in Bunia, the capital of Ituri Province. He also emphasised the critical role of community involvement in controlling the spread of the disease. The recoveries mark the first documented cases of confirmed Bundibugyo Ebola patients surviving during the current outbreak. According to the WHO, the latest official figures show 906 suspected cases and 223 suspected deaths linked to the outbreak. In neighbouring Uganda, authorities have confirmed nine cases and one death. Earlier, Doctors Without Borders (MSF) warned that the virus continues to spread faster than response efforts, despite improvements in healthcare coordination and the arrival of additional aid. The organisation called for an immediate expansion of testing, quicker deployment of health workers, and uninterrupted access to essential medical supplies.

title: France: 219 injured in clashes between police and football fans
summary: In France, a total of 219 people have been injured due to clashes between police and football fans who were celebrating the victory of Paris Saint-Germain against Arsenal in the Champions League. Thousands of officers had been deployed to control the situation...
body: In France, a total of 219 people have been injured due to clashes between police and football fans who were celebrating the victory of Paris Saint-Germain against Arsenal in the Champions League. Thousands of officers had been deployed to control the situation there as transportation services are affected in the capital, Paris. French Interior Minister Laurent Nuñez said eight people were in a serious condition, while 780 people were arrested due to violence, and more than 450 were in custody. One person was found dead on the Paris ring road, which rioters tried to block overnight. The Interior Minister said security forces would respond firmly.

title: Myanmar: Over 45 people killed in a blast at a building storing explosives in Namhkam town
summary: In Myanmar, more than 45 people were killed in a blast at a building in the northeastern part of the country that was reportedly storing explosives used for mining. According to local media reports, around 70 others were injured in the explosion, which occurre...
body: In Myanmar, more than 45 people were killed in a blast at a building in the northeastern part of the country that was reportedly storing explosives used for mining. According to local media reports, around 70 others were injured in the explosion, which occurred around noon in the village of Kaungtup in Namhkam township. The area, located about 3 km south of the Chinese border, is controlled by the Ta’ang National Liberation Army, an ethnic armed group engaged in conflict with Myanmar’s central government. A rescue worker who reached the site said that 46 bodies, including those of six children, were recovered by evening and taken for cremation. Rescue operation underway.

title: IAEA chief expresses concern over reported drone strike at Zaporizhzhia Nuclear Plant
summary: The International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) Director General Rafael Grossi has expressed concern after a reported drone strike on a turbine building at the Zaporizhzhia Nuclear Power Plant in Ukraine yesterday. The IAEA said it was informed by the plant that...
body: The International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) Director General Rafael Grossi has expressed concern after a reported drone strike on a turbine building at the Zaporizhzhia Nuclear Power Plant in Ukraine yesterday. The IAEA said it was informed by the plant that a drone struck the turbine building earlier in the day, reportedly leaving a hole in one of its walls. The agency said its team on site has requested access to the area to assess the damage. Grossi warned that any attacks on or near nuclear facilities pose serious risks, reiterating that such sites must not be targeted under any circumstances. Russia’s State Atomic Energy Corporation, Rosatom said a Ukrainian drone struck the turbine island of Unit 6 yesterday, causing damage to a structure but not affecting key equipment. The plant also reported no casualties or major damage. The Zaporizhzhia plant, one of Europe’s largest nuclear power facilities, has been under Russian control since March 2022. The IAEA has repeatedly warned about the risks of military activity around the site and called for the protection of nuclear infrastructure.

title: Union Ministers Mandaviya and Scindia congratulate Satwiksairaj-Chirag on Singapore Open title win
summary: Youth Affairs and Sports Minister Dr Mansukh Mandaviya and Union Minister Jyotiraditya Scindia congratulated Satwiksairaj Rankireddy and Chirag Shetty for winning the Singapore Open Super 750 title. In a social media post, Mansukh Mandaviya said that the win e...
body: Youth Affairs and Sports Minister Dr Mansukh Mandaviya and Union Minister Jyotiraditya Scindia congratulated Satwiksairaj Rankireddy and Chirag Shetty for winning the Singapore Open Super 750 title. In a social media post, Mansukh Mandaviya said that the win ends a two-year wait for the title and is yet another glorious chapter to India’s badminton journey. Dr Mandaviya called this a historic triumph for Indian Badminton. In a social media post, Jyotiraditya Scindia lauded the duo for fighting back from a game down and ending a two-year wait for a title. He said the duo once again demonstrated the resilience, belief and class that make them among the very best in the world.

title: Shubman Gill’s century powers Gujarat Titans into IPL final
summary: In cricket, Gujarat Titans stormed into the final of IPL 2026 with a commanding seven-wicket victory over Rajasthan Royals in Qualifier Two at New Chandigarh. Chasing a formidable target of 215 runs, the 2022 champions produced a batting masterclass, reaching ...
body: In cricket, Gujarat Titans stormed into the final of IPL 2026 with a commanding seven-wicket victory over Rajasthan Royals in Qualifier Two at New Chandigarh. Chasing a formidable target of 215 runs, the 2022 champions produced a batting masterclass, reaching 219 for three in just 18.4 overs. Skipper Shubman Gill led from the front with a scintillating 104 off 53 balls, while Sai Sudharsan contributed 58 runs. Shubman Gill was adjudged Player of the Match. Earlier, after being put in to bat, Rajasthan Royals posted an imposing 214 for six in stipulated 20 overs. Young opener Vaibhav Sooryavanshi starred with a brilliant 96, narrowly missing out on a century again. Ravindra Jadeja remained unbeaten on 45. For Gujarat Titans, Kagiso Rabada and Jason Holder scalped two wickets each. With this victory, Gujarat Titans have set up a title clash against defending champions Royal Challengers Bengaluru. The summit clash will be played in Ahmedabad tomorrow, with the match scheduled to begin at 7:30 PM.

title: India climbs to second spot with eight medals at Asian U-20 Athletics Championships
summary: India continued its impressive performance at the Asian Under-20 Athletics Championships in Hong Kong, taking its medal tally to eight, including four gold, two silver and two bronze medals, and climbing to second place in the standings behind China. On the se...
body: India continued its impressive performance at the Asian Under-20 Athletics Championships in Hong Kong, taking its medal tally to eight, including four gold, two silver and two bronze medals, and climbing to second place in the standings behind China. On the second day of competition yesterday, Indian athletes won seven medals, including four golds. The standout performance came from 19-year-old Pooja Singh, who clinched gold in the women’s high jump with a leap of 1.93 metres. In the process, she broke the 14-year-old senior national record of 1.92 metres, set by Sahana Kumari in 2012. India’s three other gold medals came through Nitin Gupta, who won the men’s 5,000-metre race walk, Amanat Kamboj, who topped the women’s discus throw, and Rahul Jakhar, who bagged gold in the men’s decathlon. Sadhana Devi won silver in the women’s triple jump, while Upkar secured silver in the men’s decathlon. Neeru Pathak added a bronze medal in the women’s 400 metres. India had opened its medal account on Thursday when Nipam claimed bronze in the women’s 100 metres. A 48-member Indian contingent, comprising 30 men and 18 women, is participating in the championships, which will conclude tomorrow.

title: DFS Secretary reviews performance of Public Sector Banks in New Delhi
summary: Secretary, Department of Financial Services, M. Nagaraju, chaired a review meeting in New Delhi yesterday to assess the performance and progress of Public Sector Banks across key operational, financial and strategic priorities during the financial year 2025-26...
body: Secretary, Department of Financial Services, M. Nagaraju, chaired a review meeting in New Delhi yesterday to assess the performance and progress of Public Sector Banks across key operational, financial and strategic priorities during the financial year 2025-26. The Finance Ministry said that the performance of Public Sector Banks across key areas, including business growth, profitability, asset quality, implementation of government schemes, financial inclusion, digital ecosystem, MSME credit flow, cyber resilience and operational risk management, was comprehensively assessed. It highlighted that Public Sector Banks demonstrated strong financial and operational performance during the financial year 2025-26. Progress under major financial inclusion initiatives, including Pradhan Mantri Jan Dhan Yojana, social security schemes, Pradhan Mantri Mudra Yojana, PM Vishwakarma and digital lending initiatives, was also reviewed. Deliberations covered strengthening digital banking ecosystems, enhancing cyber security frameworks and improving access to credit for MSMEs and other productive sectors of the economy. The discussions also stressed the need for Public Sector Banks to adopt prudent expenditure and austerity measures at all levels while maintaining resilience amid evolving global uncertainties. Secretary, Department of Financial Services, M. Nagaraju highlighted the importance of maintaining robust grievance redressal systems, strong governance standards and operational preparedness. He said these measures would ensure that the banking system remains resilient, trusted and aligned with the vision of Viksit Bharat 2047. The revamped website of the Department of Financial Services was also launched during the meeting. The portal offers enhanced accessibility, seamless navigation and improved dissemination of information in more than 23 regional languages.

title: Government launches portal for search of unclaimed financial assets
summary: The Department of Financial Services has launched the Common Landing Portal for Unclaimed Financial Assets to facilitate easier access to information on unclaimed financial assets. The portal has been developed in collaboration with the Public Sector Bank Alli...
body: The Department of Financial Services has launched the Common Landing Portal for Unclaimed Financial Assets to facilitate easier access to information on unclaimed financial assets. The portal has been developed in collaboration with the Public Sector Bank Alliance (PSBA), a centralised umbrella organisation jointly promoted by 12 public sector banks in the country. The Finance Ministry said that the portal, www.unclaimedassetsportal.in, serves as a unified platform providing access to search facilities for unclaimed bank deposits, insurance claims, shares, dividends and mutual funds across the financial ecosystem. The Common Landing Portal marks an important step towards building a more accessible, transparent and citizen-centric financial ecosystem. Secretary, Department of Financial Services, M. Nagaraju, said that the portal will enhance citizen convenience by bringing together access to multiple search facilities on a single platform and strengthen ongoing efforts to reconnect individuals with their rightful financial assets.

title: Russia recalls ambassador from Armenia over growing EU ties ahead of elections
summary: Russia has recalled its ambassador to Armenia, Sergei Kopyrkin, to Moscow for consultations amid growing concerns over Yerevan’s efforts to strengthen ties with the European Union ahead of the June 7 elections. In a statement released today, Russia’s Foreign M...
body: Russia has recalled its ambassador to Armenia, Sergei Kopyrkin, to Moscow for consultations amid growing concerns over Yerevan’s efforts to strengthen ties with the European Union ahead of the June 7 elections. In a statement released today, Russia’s Foreign Ministry said Ambassador Kopyrkin had been recalled in connection with steps taken by the Armenian leadership toward rapprochement with the European Union. Although Armenia remains a formal ally of Russia, it has deepening ties with Western countries in recent ​years despite its economic dependence on Moscow. The move comes a day after a Russian-led economic union of former Soviet republics said that it would consider suspending Armenia for seeking ​EU membership and called on Yerevan to hold a referendum.

title: Union Minister Piyush Goyal highlights India’s strong growth story with over 50 business, industry leaders at Roundtable in New York
summary: Union Commerce and Industry Minister Piyush Goyal engaged with over 50 business and industry leaders at the Roundtable. The roundtable was hosted by the Consulate General of India in New York in collaboration with US-India Strategic Partnership Forum. In a soc...
body: Union Commerce and Industry Minister Piyush Goyal engaged with over 50 business and industry leaders at the Roundtable. The roundtable was hosted by the Consulate General of India in New York in collaboration with US-India Strategic Partnership Forum. In a social media post, Mr Goyal said that during the roundtable he highlighted India’s strong growth story, reform-driven business environment, and expanding opportunities for global investors under the leadership of Prime Minister Narendra Modi. He further discussed ways to deepen India-US trade, investment, innovation, and supply-chain partnerships for shared prosperity.

title: 14-year-old Indian-American student wins National Spelling Bee competition
summary: Shrey Parikh, a 14-year-old Indian-American student from California today won the Scripps National Spelling Bee after he spelt 32 of the 35 words correctly in 90 seconds. Parikh, an 8th-grade student, beat 7th grader 12-year-old Ishaan Gupta. 12-year-old Sarv ...
body: Shrey Parikh, a 14-year-old Indian-American student from California today won the Scripps National Spelling Bee after he spelt 32 of the 35 words correctly in 90 seconds. Parikh, an 8th-grade student, beat 7th grader 12-year-old Ishaan Gupta. 12-year-old Sarv Dharavane finished third. Parikh also beat the previous spell-off record set in 2024 by Bruhat Soma, who spelled 29 out of 30 words correctly in the spell-off. Parikh, who was also a finalist in the 2024 edition of the competition, takes home a slew of prizes, including 50,000 US Dollars, a commemorative medal and the Scripps Cup among others. The 3-day competition began Monday at D.A.R. Constitution Hall with 247 contestants from all 50 states and the District of Columbia, Guam, Puerto Rico, the US Virgin Islands, Defence Department schools in Europe and five other countries including the Bahamas, Canada, Ghana, Nigeria and the United Arab Emirates. The Spelling Bee competition was first held in 1925. Balu Natarajan became the first Indian origin student to win the competition in 1985. Indian origin students have dominated the competition and won titles over the past four decades. Of the nine finalists this year, five were of Indian origin.

title: Badminton: Indian shuttlers make way to semifinals of doubles & Mixed doubles
summary: India’s men’s doubles pair, Satwiksairaj Rankireddy and Chirag Shetty advanced to the semifinals of the Singapore Open Super 750 badminton tournament today. Rankireddy and Chirag beat the Malaysian pair of Aaron Tai and Kang Khai Xing 19-21, 21-17, 21-13. Thei...
body: India’s men’s doubles pair, Satwiksairaj Rankireddy and Chirag Shetty advanced to the semifinals of the Singapore Open Super 750 badminton tournament today. Rankireddy and Chirag beat the Malaysian pair of Aaron Tai and Kang Khai Xing 19-21, 21-17, 21-13. Their next clash will be against the world number one pair of Seo Seung-jae and Kim Won-ho of South Korea tomorrow. In the mixed doubles, Dhruv Kapila and Tanisha Crasto also managed to enter the semifinals, beating Chen Tang Jie and Toh Ee Wei of Malaysia. In the men’s singles quarterfinal, Lakshya Sen, lost the quarterfinal 19-21, 21-15, 15-21 to Japan’s Koki Watanabe. Veteran shuttler PV Sindhu’s campaign came to an end in the quarterfinals after a hard-fought defeat against world No. 1 An Se Young of South Korea.

title: Air India extends suspension of its flights to Israel till July end
summary: Air India has extended the suspension of its flights to Israel till July end, amid continued geopolitical uncertainties in West Asia. The airlines had earlier suspended flight operations on the Tel Aviv-Delhi route till the end of June. A senior Air India offi...
body: Air India has extended the suspension of its flights to Israel till July end, amid continued geopolitical uncertainties in West Asia. The airlines had earlier suspended flight operations on the Tel Aviv-Delhi route till the end of June. A senior Air India official said that flights have been further suspended, keeping in mind the geopolitical situation in the region. In the wake of the West Asia conflict, airlines have been facing multiple headwinds, including costlier fuel and airspace curbs, which pushed up their operational costs. Despite the ceasefire between the US and Iran holding on for some time, the uncertainties surrounding the situation in West Asia have pushed the majority of airlines to stop operating on the Tel Aviv route. The suspension of Air India flights has caused major worries among more than 40,000 Indians living in Israel who wish to travel to India for personal or professional reasons, or even to escape the uncertainties in the region. The limited options also mean heightened costs, which is a major concern among Indian workers.

title: Oil prices down by 1.5%
summary: Global crude oil prices dropped by around one and a half per cent today. Brent crude was trading 1.7 per cent lower at 92 US dollars and 14 cents per barrel in intra-day trade. West Texas Intermediate crude was also trading 1.4 per cent lower at 87 US dollars ...
body: Global crude oil prices dropped by around one and a half per cent today. Brent crude was trading 1.7 per cent lower at 92 US dollars and 14 cents per barrel in intra-day trade. West Texas Intermediate crude was also trading 1.4 per cent lower at 87 US dollars and 64 cents per barrel when reports last came in.

title: Rajasthan Royals to take on Gujarat Titans in all-important Qualifier 2 in New Chandigarh today
summary: In IPL Cricket, Gujarat Titans (GT) and Rajasthan Royals (RR) will meet in the all-important Qualifier 2 at the Maharaja Yadavindra Singh International Cricket Stadium, New Chandigarh, this evening. The match will start at 7:30 PM. The winner will take on Roya...
body: In IPL Cricket, Gujarat Titans (GT) and Rajasthan Royals (RR) will meet in the all-important Qualifier 2 at the Maharaja Yadavindra Singh International Cricket Stadium, New Chandigarh, this evening. The match will start at 7:30 PM. The winner will take on Royal Challengers Bengaluru in the IPL 2026 final on Sunday at Narendra Modi Stadium in Ahmedabad. Gujarat Titans lost to defending champions Royal Challengers Bengaluru (RCB) in Qualifier 1, while Rajasthan Royals made it by defeating Sunrisers Hyderabad in Eliminator 1. Gujarat will be looking for ways to stop Rajasthan’s opener Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, who had smashed a 28-ball 97 against Sunrisers Hyderabad and will be a big threat to the 2022 winners. Rajasthan had dramatically made the playoffs after beating the Mumbai Indians in their final league phase match at the Wankhede. While the RR, winner of the inaugural edition in 2008, are aiming to make it to the final, GT are determined to reclaim the championship glory they first achieved during their debut season in 2022.

title: HM Amit Shah holds meeting to review preparations for CWG and Olympics to be hosted in Ahmedabad
summary: Union Home Minister Amit Shah held a meeting yesterday to review preparations for the Commonwealth Games (CWG) and proposed Olympics to be hosted in Ahmedabad. Union Sports Minister Mansukh Mandaviya, Gujarat Chief Minister Bhupendra Patel, Deputy Chief Minist...
body: Union Home Minister Amit Shah held a meeting yesterday to review preparations for the Commonwealth Games (CWG) and proposed Olympics to be hosted in Ahmedabad. Union Sports Minister Mansukh Mandaviya, Gujarat Chief Minister Bhupendra Patel, Deputy Chief Minister Harsh Sanghavi and senior officials were present at the meeting. In a social media post, Mr Shah said the Modi government is expanding world-class sports infrastructure and state-of-the-art training facilities in India. Keeping in mind the proposed world-class sporting events in Ahmedabad, sports facilities are being developed in accordance with global standards. In line with this, review meetings were held in Ahmedabad regarding the progress of sports infrastructure and related projects, he said. Ahmedabad would be hosting the 2030 Commonwealth Games and has officially submitted a bid for the 2036 Olympics.

title: Air travel declines globally by 3,4% amid West Asia conflict
summary: Global air passenger demand fell 3.4 per cent year-on-year in April 2026, marking a sharp slowdown, according to the International Air Transport Association (IATA). The decline was largely attributed to the impact of the ongoing war in the Middle East. Total d...
body: Global air passenger demand fell 3.4 per cent year-on-year in April 2026, marking a sharp slowdown, according to the International Air Transport Association (IATA). The decline was largely attributed to the impact of the ongoing war in the Middle East. Total demand, measured in revenue passenger kilometers, declined 3.4 per cent compared to April 2025, while total capacity, measured in available seat kilometers dropped 2.9 per cent. Global load factor stood at 83.1 per cent, down 0.4 percentage points from the year-ago period. IATA said the downturn was largely caused by a steep collapse in travel demand for Middle Eastern carriers. Middle Eastern airlines recorded the steepest decline among all regions, with passenger demand plunging 48.1 per cent year-on-year in April. However, excluding Middle Eastern airlines, international demand grew 1.9 per cent.

title: SC permits wrestler Vinesh Phogat to participate in selection trials of Asian Games 2026
summary: The Supreme Court today permitted wrestler Vinesh Phogat to participate in the selection trials to be held tomorrow and 31st of this month for the Asian Games 2026. A bench of Justices P S Narasimha and Alok Aradhe passed the order while hearing a plea filed b...
body: The Supreme Court today permitted wrestler Vinesh Phogat to participate in the selection trials to be held tomorrow and 31st of this month for the Asian Games 2026. A bench of Justices P S Narasimha and Alok Aradhe passed the order while hearing a plea filed by the Wrestling Federation of India (WFI) challenging the Delhi High Court order allowing Phogat to participate in the selection trials. A division bench of the high court said, the WFI’s selection policy was exclusionary for the lack of discretion to consider an iconic player like her, who is returning from a maternity break. The high court had said the law must ensure that motherhood does not become a ground to exclude female athletes like Phogat. In her petition before the high court, Phogat has challenged the WFI’s selection policy and circular, which have limited eligibility for participation in the Asian Games trials only to the medal winners of certain tournaments. Phogat has asserted that the qualification window chosen by the WFI substantially overlapped with her notified sabbatical on account of pregnancy and post-partum recovery.

title: RBI balance sheet swells due to surge in domestic & foreign investments
summary: The Reserve Bank’s balance sheet swelled to 91.97 lakh crore rupee at the end of March 2026, up 20.6 per cent year-on-year, due to a rise in domestic investments as well as gold and foreign investments, according to the central bank’s annual report released to...
body: The Reserve Bank’s balance sheet swelled to 91.97 lakh crore rupee at the end of March 2026, up 20.6 per cent year-on-year, due to a rise in domestic investments as well as gold and foreign investments, according to the central bank’s annual report released today. The balance sheet size increased by 15 lakh 71 thousand 699 crore rupees from around 76 lakh 25 thousand 422 crore as on March 31, 2025, to 91 lakh 97 thousand 121 crore rupees as on March 31, 2026. The increase on the assets side was due to a rise in domestic investments, gold and foreign investments by 44.9 per cent, 63.8 per cent and 7.9 per cent, respectively. On the liabilities side, revaluation accounts, notes issued, deposits and other liabilities increased by 63.4 per cent, 11.8 per cent, 11.6 per cent and 21.1 per cent, respectively, the report said. Domestic assets constituted 29.1 per cent, while foreign currency assets, gold (including gold deposits and gold held in India), and loans and advances to financial institutions outside India constituted 70.9 per cent of total assets as on March 31, 2026, as against 25.7 per cent and 74.3 per cent, respectively, as on March 31, 2025. As on March 31, 2026, the report said the total gold held by the Reserve Bank was 880.52 metric tonnes, compared to 879.58 metric tonnes as on March 31, 2025, an increase of 0.94 metric tonnes during the year. It said that of the 880.52 metric tonnes, 312.32 metric tonnes of gold are held as an asset of the Issue Department, compared to 311.38 metric tonnes as on March 31, 2025. The remaining 568.20 metric tonnes, unchanged from March 31, 2025, are treated as an asset of the Banking Department, the RBI said. It added that the value of gold, including gold deposits, held as an asset of the Banking Department increased by 63.6 per cent from 4 lakh 31 lakh 625 crore as on March 31, 2025, to 7 lakh 6 thousand 162 crore as on March 31, 2026. The increase was attributed to a rise in gold prices and the depreciation of the Indian rupee vis-à-vis the US dollar.

title: PM Surya Ghar scheme achieves major milestone as 40 lakh households embrace rooftop solar
summary: India has marked a historic milestone in its clean energy journey. In a social media post, Union New and Renewable Energy Minister Pralhad Joshi said that 40 lakh households have now been empowered with rooftop solar under PM Surya Ghar scheme. He added that m...
body: India has marked a historic milestone in its clean energy journey. In a social media post, Union New and Renewable Energy Minister Pralhad Joshi said that 40 lakh households have now been empowered with rooftop solar under PM Surya Ghar scheme. He added that more than a renewable energy initiative, this transformative scheme is turning citizens from power consumers to power producers. The scheme is also enabling families to reduce electricity expenses, benefit from government subsidies and move towards zero electricity bills. The Minister highlighted that with every solar-powered home, the country moves closer towards energy security, environmental sustainability and the vision of a self-reliant Viksit Bharat.

title: India emerged as stronger economy under PM Modi leadership, and textile sector achieving rapid growth: Giriraj Singh
summary: Union Textiles Minister Giriraj Singh today said India has emerged as a stronger economy under the leadership of Prime Minister Narendra Modi, and the textile sector is achieving rapid growth through reforms and policy support. During his two-day visit to Andh...
body: Union Textiles Minister Giriraj Singh today said India has emerged as a stronger economy under the leadership of Prime Minister Narendra Modi, and the textile sector is achieving rapid growth through reforms and policy support. During his two-day visit to Andhra Pradesh, the Minister visited Mohan Spintex Company at Mallavalli Industrial Park in Krishna district. He said India’s textile market is currently valued at over 15 lakh crore rupees and the government is working to increase it beyond 25 lakh crore rupees by 2030. Mr Singh said the textile industry is expanding rapidly and stressed the need to provide wider global market access to Indian artisans and craftsmen. He said the Centre is preparing a future action plan with the Andhra Pradesh Government to accelerate textile sector growth in the state. The Minister said the Production Linked Incentive scheme is helping boost production, promote Indian brands and attract foreign investment into the textile sector. He also assured efforts for higher fund allocation to Andhra Pradesh. Referring to Prime Minister Narendra Modi completing 12 years in office, Mr Singh said India has gained greater global recognition and witnessed major progress in economic growth, digital transformation, infrastructure, national security and social welfare. He said the achievements of the past 12 years have laid a strong foundation for building a Viksit Bharat by 2047.

title: India, Canada reiterate commitment to conclude negotiations on CEPA by end of this year
summary: India and Canada have reiterated their commitment to conclude negotiations on the Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement (CEPA) by the end of this year. In the India-Canada Joint Statement on the Trade and Investment Forum, Commerce and Industry Minister...
body: India and Canada have reiterated their commitment to conclude negotiations on the Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement (CEPA) by the end of this year. In the India-Canada Joint Statement on the Trade and Investment Forum, Commerce and Industry Minister Piyush Goyal and International Trade Minister of Canada Maninder Sidhu reiterated their commitment to advancing an ambitious and mutually beneficial CEPA Agreement. Both Ministers reaffirmed their shared commitment to strengthening bilateral trade and investment ties to deliver tangible outcomes that support economic growth and increased commercial opportunities for businesses in both countries. The Ministers launched the Canada-India Trade and Investment Forum as a key platform that brings together Canadian and Indian business leaders and fosters new commercial partnerships and increased business engagement. Building on the momentum generated through recent ministerial engagement, Minister Sidhu underscored opportunities to deepen cooperation across complementary sectors such as clean energy, critical minerals, agri‑food, advanced manufacturing, digital technologies, and skills development. Mr Sidhu confirmed that Canada will lead a Team Canada Trade Mission to India later this year, reflecting the strong interest of Canadian businesses in expanding their presence in the Indian market and the shared ambition to deepen commercial partnerships.

title: PM Modi congratulates Indian wrestlers for outstanding performance at U23 Asian Championships 2026
summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi today congratulated Indian wrestlers for their outstanding performance at the 2026 U23 Asian Championships held in Da Nang, Vietnam. In a social media post, Mr Modi said that the Men’s Freestyle and Women’s Wrestling teams won the ...
body: Prime Minister Narendra Modi today congratulated Indian wrestlers for their outstanding performance at the 2026 U23 Asian Championships held in Da Nang, Vietnam. In a social media post, Mr Modi said that the Men’s Freestyle and Women’s Wrestling teams won the Team Titles at the Championships. He said that the Men’s freestyle wrestling team secured a record of nine medals, including four gold medals, registering the country’s highest ever overall medal haul in the history of the championships. The Prime Minister added that the women’s wrestling contingent also delivered a remarkable performance by winning ten medals, including six gold medals. He also highlighted that the Greco-Roman team recorded its best-ever overall medal tally at the championship with eight medals.

title: R Praggnanandhaa defeats Magnus Carlsen in round three of Norway Chess
summary: In Norway Chess, R Praggnanandhaa defeated the Norwegian world No. 1 Magnus Carlsen, gripping the contest to claim three full points in round three of the tournament yesterday. In contrast, world champion D Gukesh continued to endure a difficult run, suffering...
body: In Norway Chess, R Praggnanandhaa defeated the Norwegian world No. 1 Magnus Carlsen, gripping the contest to claim three full points in round three of the tournament yesterday. In contrast, world champion D Gukesh continued to endure a difficult run, suffering another setback after losing to Alireza Firouzja in the final game following a hard-fought classical draw. The victory was another major statement from Praggnanandhaa, who arrived in Oslo on the eve of the Norway Chess after a string of impressive performances at the Superbet Chess Classic Romania in Bucharest. The win took the Indian to 4.5 points and lifted him to the sole second place in the standings at the Norway Chess. He now trails only Firouzja, who leads the tournament with 7.5 points. Meanwhile, Gukesh remains on 3.5 points and continues to struggle for momentum, with his difficult run in the Norway Chess showing little sign of easing. The world champion is currently placed fourth in the six-player Open field.

title: WMO says Global average temperatures to continue at or near record levels in five years
summary: The World Meteorological Organisation (WMO) today said that Global average temperatures are likely to continue at or near record levels in the next five years, with Arctic temperature anomalies expected to continue to be higher than the global mean. The conclu...
body: The World Meteorological Organisation (WMO) today said that Global average temperatures are likely to continue at or near record levels in the next five years, with Arctic temperature anomalies expected to continue to be higher than the global mean. The conclusion was drawn from the WMO report ‘Global Annual to Decadal Climate Update 2026-2035’, produced by Britain’s Met Office and the WMO Lead Centre for Annual to Decadal Climate Prediction. According to the report, annual global mean near-surface temperatures during the period from 2026 to 2030 will range between 1.3 and 1.9 degrees Celsius above the 1850-1900 average. It is likely that one year coming between 2026 and 2030 will surpass 2024 as the warmest year on record. It predicted that the average global temperature for 2026-2030 will likely exceed 1.5 degrees Celsius above the 1850-1900 average, while no single year during the period is likely to exceed 2.0 degrees Celsius. The predicted average temperature in the central tropical Pacific indicates a tendency towards El Niño conditions in the next five years, particularly in 2027 and 2028. Leon Hermanson, lead author of the report, said there is an El Niño predicted for the end of 2026, which increases the chances of the following year, 2027, being the next record-breaking year.

title: Two-time Paralympic champion Sumit Antil breaks world record in Men’s Javelin Throw F64 in Bengaluru
summary: Two-time Paralympic champion Sumit Antil shattered his own world record in the Men’s Javelin Throw F64 category at 8th Indian Open International Para Athletics Championships 2026 in Bengaluru yesterday, producing a massive throw of 74.82 metres. Representing H...
body: Two-time Paralympic champion Sumit Antil shattered his own world record in the Men’s Javelin Throw F64 category at 8th Indian Open International Para Athletics Championships 2026 in Bengaluru yesterday, producing a massive throw of 74.82 metres. Representing Haryana, Antil delivered a sensational gold medal-winning performance to surpass his previous world record of 73.29m, which he had set at the 2022 Asian Para Games in Hangzhou. Antil comfortably secured the top spot ahead of Maharashtra’s Sandip Sargar, who claimed silver with a throw of 62.88m. Rajasthan’s Sandeep completed the podium with bronze after registering 61.83m. The second day of the championships witnessed several standout performances across track and field events. Paralympic medallist Praveen Kumar cleared 2.00m to clinch gold in the Men’s High Jump T64 event, while fellow Indian para-athlete Varun Singh Bhati secured silver with a clearance of 1.84m. In the Women’s 400m T20 race, Tokyo Paralympic gold medallist Deepthi Jeevanji impressed with a winning time of 57.84 seconds. Maharashtra’s Dilip Gavit also produced a commanding run in the Men’s 400m T47 category, sprinting to gold in 47.64 seconds.

title: PV Sindhu defeats Japan’s Riko Gunji to enter Singapore Open quarterfinals
summary: India enjoyed an impressive day at the Singapore Open today, with Lakshya Sen, PV Sindhu, the men’s doubles pair of Chirag Shetty and Satwiksairaj Rankireddy, and the mixed doubles duo of Dhruv Kapila and Tanisha Crasto all advancing to the quarterfinals. Sind...
body: India enjoyed an impressive day at the Singapore Open today, with Lakshya Sen, PV Sindhu, the men’s doubles pair of Chirag Shetty and Satwiksairaj Rankireddy, and the mixed doubles duo of Dhruv Kapila and Tanisha Crasto all advancing to the quarterfinals. Sindhu defeated Japan’s Riko Gunji in straight games, 21-9, 21-12, in the women’s singles Round of 16. With this victory, Sindhu moves into the quarterfinals, where she is set for a high-profile clash against South Korea’s An Se Young. Lakshya advanced to the quarterfinals after Thailand’s world No. 2 Kunlavut Vitidsarn retired hurt just moments into their Round of 16 clash today. The much-anticipated contest ended abruptly after only two rallies, with Lakshya leading 2-0 in the opening game. Satwiksairaj Rankireddy and Chirag Shetty have also joined India’s strong quarterfinal lineup after defeating Lee Jhe-Huei and Yang Po-Hsuan 21-15, 11-21, 21-18 in a thrilling three-game Round of 16 clash.

title: Indian contingent all set to play in Under-18 Asia Cup Hockey 2026 in Japan
summary: Indian contingent is all set to play in Under-18 Asia Cup Hockey 2026 beginning in Kakamigahara, Japan, tomorrow to June 6th. India have been assigned to Pool A in the men’s competition, alongside hosts Japan, Korea, Chinese Taipei, and Kazakhstan. They will s...
body: Indian contingent is all set to play in Under-18 Asia Cup Hockey 2026 beginning in Kakamigahara, Japan, tomorrow to June 6th. India have been assigned to Pool A in the men’s competition, alongside hosts Japan, Korea, Chinese Taipei, and Kazakhstan. They will start their campaign against Kazakhstan on May 29, followed by matches against Japan on May 31, Korea on June 1, and Chinese Taipei on June 3. Meanwhile, Pool B includes Malaysia, Pakistan, Bangladesh, and China. The Indian U-18 girls’ team, drawn in Pool A, will begin their campaign against Malaysia on May 30th, followed by matches against Korea on May 31st and Singapore on June 2. Pool B includes China, Japan, Chinese Taipei and Bangladesh. The top two teams from each pool will advance to the semi-finals on June 5th, with the final scheduled for June 6.

title: Rajasthan Royals defeat Sunrisers Hyderabad in IPL eliminator match in New Chandigarh
summary: In IPL Cricket, Rajasthan Royals defeated Sunrisers Hyderabad by 47 runs in the eliminator match in New Chandigarh, last night. Rajasthan Royals set the target of 244 runs against Sunrisers Hyderabad. In reply, Sunrisers Hyderabad could score only 196 runs in ...
body: In IPL Cricket, Rajasthan Royals defeated Sunrisers Hyderabad by 47 runs in the eliminator match in New Chandigarh, last night. Rajasthan Royals set the target of 244 runs against Sunrisers Hyderabad. In reply, Sunrisers Hyderabad could score only 196 runs in 19.2 overs. Put into bat, Rajasthan Royals posted 243 runs for the loss of 8 wickets in stipulated 20 overs. Earlier, Sunrisers Hyderabad won the toss and elected to field. Vaibhav Sooryavanshi has been adjudged as the PLAYER OF THE MATCH. Tomorrow, Rajasthan Royals will take on Gujarat Titans in Qualifier 2, for a place in the final. The final match will be played on Sunday at Narendra Modi Stadium in Ahmedabad. Earlier, Royal Challengers Bengaluru stormed into the final beating Gujarat Titans by 92 runs in the Qualifier one match at Dharamshala on Tuesday.

title: India to host 2027 Asian Relays and 2028 Asian Indoor Championships
summary: The Indian cities of Chandigarh and Bhubaneswar were today awarded hosting rights of next year’s Asian Relays and the 2028 Asian Indoor Championships by the continent’s athletics governing body during its executive council meeting in Hong Kong. The Asian Athle...
body: The Indian cities of Chandigarh and Bhubaneswar were today awarded hosting rights of next year’s Asian Relays and the 2028 Asian Indoor Championships by the continent’s athletics governing body during its executive council meeting in Hong Kong. The Asian Athletics Association’s (AAA) council meeting was held ahead of the Asian U20 Championships from May 28 to 31. World Athletics Vice-President Adille Sumariwalla, who attended the AAA council meeting in Hong Kong, said that India will host the Asian Relays in 2027 to be held in Chandigarh and Bhubaneswar will host the Asian Indoor Championships in 2028. The Athletics Federation of India (AFI) had last year submitted the bids to host both the events.

title: Badminton: Indian shuttlers H.S. Prannoy and Lakshya Sen advance to second round of Men’s Singles
summary: In Badminton, Indian shuttler H.S. Prannoy stunned fifth-ranked Jonatan Christie of Indonesia with a 10-21, 21-12, 21-18 first-round victory in Men’s Singles at the Singapore Open today. Lakshya Sen also advanced to the second round beating China’s Lu Guangzu ...
body: In Badminton, Indian shuttler H.S. Prannoy stunned fifth-ranked Jonatan Christie of Indonesia with a 10-21, 21-12, 21-18 first-round victory in Men’s Singles at the Singapore Open today. Lakshya Sen also advanced to the second round beating China’s Lu Guangzu 21-17, 21-15. However, in another Round of 32 match, Kidambi Srikanth lost to Singapore’s Loh Kean Yew 20-22, 21-19, 17-21. Later in the day, Lakshya Sen will take on China’s Lu Guangzu. In the Mixed Doubles, the pair of Dhruv Kapila and Tanisha Crasto defeated the Malaysian duo of Wong Tien Ci and Lim Chiew Sien 21-14, 20-22, 21-13 in the first round. In Women’s Singles, Malvika Bansod exited in the opening round of the tournament after losing to Chinese Taipei’s Lin Hsiang-Ti 21-11, 19-21, 12-21.

title: ISSF WC: Esha Singh breaks World Record in Women’s 25-meter pistol event, clinching gold
summary: Indian shooter Esha Singh today broke the world record in the Women’s 25 metre pistol event at the ISSF World Cup in Munich. 21-year-old Esha earned 43 points from a possible 50 in the final to surpass the previous record of 42 points held by South Korea’s Kim...
body: Indian shooter Esha Singh today broke the world record in the Women’s 25 metre pistol event at the ISSF World Cup in Munich. 21-year-old Esha earned 43 points from a possible 50 in the final to surpass the previous record of 42 points held by South Korea’s Kim Yeji and claimed the gold medal. German shooter Doreen Vennekamp finished second with 38 points, while former world champion Miroslava Mincheva of Bulgaria claimed third place with 31 points.

title: Asian markets display mixed response, European indices trading with gains
summary: Most Asian stock market indices displayed mixed trend today. China’s Shanghai Composite Index slipped more than 1.2 per cent, and Hong Kong’s Hang Seng Index dropped almost 1.1 per cent. Conversely, South Korea’s Kospi index extended its upward move, rising mo...
body: Most Asian stock market indices displayed mixed trend today. China’s Shanghai Composite Index slipped more than 1.2 per cent, and Hong Kong’s Hang Seng Index dropped almost 1.1 per cent. Conversely, South Korea’s Kospi index extended its upward move, rising more than 2.2 per cent while Japan’s Nikkei 225 ended on a flat note, giving up gains after hitting a fresh record high. Meanwhile, Singapore’s stock market remained closed today for Hari Raya Haji. Major European indices were trading with gains today. France CAC 40 rose 0.8 per cent, while Germany’s DAX as well as London’s FTSE 100 were trading more than 0.1 per cent up, when reports last came in.

title: Five-time Olympian shooter Raja Randhir Singh passes away
summary: A renowned shooter, five-time Olympian and a former Honorary Secretary General of the Indian Olympic Association, Raja Randhir Singh passed away at his residence, today, following prolonged illness. He was 79. A veteran sports administrator and cousin of forme...
body: A renowned shooter, five-time Olympian and a former Honorary Secretary General of the Indian Olympic Association, Raja Randhir Singh passed away at his residence, today, following prolonged illness. He was 79. A veteran sports administrator and cousin of former Punjab Chief Minister, now a senior BJP leader, Capt. Amarinder Singh, Randhir Singh belonged to the Royal family of Patiala and was from the fourth generation of the distinguished sports family. His uncle, Maharaja Yadavindra Singh, represented India in Test cricket, while his father, Raja Bhalindra Singh, served as a member of the Indian Olympic Committee from 1947 to 1992. Asian Games gold medalist, former Arjuna awardee, he held several key positions in Indian and international sports administration. He also served as Secretary General of the Olympic Council of Asia and was a member of the International Olympic Committee before becoming an honorary member. He also served on various IOC commissions and represented the IOC on the World Anti-Doping Agency (WADA) Foundation Board. In addition, he held positions with the Sports Authority of India, the Association of National Olympic Committees (ANOC) and the Afro-Asian Games Council, among several other sporting bodies. The National Rifle Association of India (NRAI) has mourned the demise of legendary Shooter and India’s most respected Sports Administrator, Raja Randhir Singh.

title: RCB crush GT by 92 runs to reach IPL final
summary: In IPL Cricket, Royal Challengers Bengaluru stormed into the final beating Gujarat Titans by 92 runs in the Qualifier one match at Dharamshala last night. Chasing a mammoth target of 255 runs, Gujarat Titans were all out for 162 with three balls to spare. Rahu...
body: In IPL Cricket, Royal Challengers Bengaluru stormed into the final beating Gujarat Titans by 92 runs in the Qualifier one match at Dharamshala last night. Chasing a mammoth target of 255 runs, Gujarat Titans were all out for 162 with three balls to spare. Rahul Tewatia was the top scorer with 68 runs. For Royal Challengers Bengaluru, Jacob Duffy took 3 wickets while Bhuvneshwar Kumar, Rasikh Salam Dar and Krunal Pandya took two wickets each. Earlier, put into bat first, Royal Challengers Bengaluru posted a massive 254 runs losing five wickets in the stipulated 20 overs. Rajat Patidar played a captain’s knock scoring unbeaten 93 runs off just 33 balls. Virat Kohli and Krunal Pandya added 43 runs each. For Gujarat Titans, Kagiso Rabada and Jason Holder took two wickets each. Rajat Patidar was named the Player of the Match. The eliminator will be played today between Sunrisers Hyderabad and Rajasthan Royals in New Chandigarh, Mohali at 7:30 PM. The winner will take on Gujarat Titans in Qualifier 2 on Friday for a place in the final, which will be played on 31 of this month at Narendra Modi Stadium in Ahmedabad.

title: Hindustan Petroleum Limited dismisses claims of shortage of fuel in parts of the country
summary: Hindustan Petroleum Corporation Limited (HPCL) has dismissed claims regarding reports of fuel shortages in parts of the country, including at retail outlets in the Hazaribagh area of Jharkhand. The company has also reassured customers and pilgrims travelling o...
body: Hindustan Petroleum Corporation Limited (HPCL) has dismissed claims regarding reports of fuel shortages in parts of the country, including at retail outlets in the Hazaribagh area of Jharkhand. The company has also reassured customers and pilgrims travelling on the Char Dham Yatra route that fuel supplies at HPCL retail outlets in Chamoli district remain normal and uninterrupted, while supplies across Gujarat are also stable and adequately supported. In a series of social media posts, HPCL said that reports regarding alleged fuel shortages at retail outlets in Bengaluru are misleading and do not reflect the actual supply position across the city. It stated that there is no overall product shortage situation at HPCL outlets. HPCL further said that the situation is being closely monitored by its regional and zonal teams, and multiple measures have already been initiated to ensure uninterrupted operations and seamless fuel availability. The company said supplies are being dispatched regularly as per dealer demand and the overall situation remains fully manageable and under control. HPCL has urged customers not to indulge in panic buying or rely on rumours and unverified social media reports.

title: IAF Announces Entry for Technical Branch through GATE Scores
summary: The Indian Air Force (IAF) has announced a scheme for induction into the Technical Branch (Officers Cadre), under which eligible candidates may apply based on their Graduate Aptitude Test in Engineering (GATE) scores. The Ministry of Defence said that candidat...
body: The Indian Air Force (IAF) has announced a scheme for induction into the Technical Branch (Officers Cadre), under which eligible candidates may apply based on their Graduate Aptitude Test in Engineering (GATE) scores. The Ministry of Defence said that candidates possessing a valid GATE score will be eligible for direct shortlisting for testing at the Air Force Selection Boards (AFSBs). Selection will be based on merit, effectively exempting these candidates from the preliminary written examination, such as the Air Force Common Admission Test (AFCAT). The Ministry added that the entry-level educational qualifications for the GATE Score scheme will remain consistent with the existing induction criteria for the Technical Branch via AFCAT.

title: India thrash Maldives 11-nil in opening Group B match of SAFF Women’s Football Championship
summary: In football, India thrashed the Maldives 11-nil in their opening Group B match of the SAFF Women’s Championship in Margao, Goa, yesterday. Aveka Singh starred for the hosts with four goals, while Priyangka Naorem and Karishma scored a brace each. Pyari Xaxa, D...
body: In football, India thrashed the Maldives 11-nil in their opening Group B match of the SAFF Women’s Championship in Margao, Goa, yesterday. Aveka Singh starred for the hosts with four goals, while Priyangka Naorem and Karishma scored a brace each. Pyari Xaxa, Dangmei Grace, and Sangita Basfore also found the net to complete the dominant victory. Ranked 69th in the world, India’s women’s team asserted control from the outset against the 167th-ranked Maldives, rarely allowing their opponents a foothold in the match. The five-time SAFF Women’s Championship winners India will face reigning champions Bangladesh in their next match on Sunday.

title: IPL 2026: Defending champions Royal Challengers Bengaluru to take on Gujarat Titans in Qualifier One today
summary: In IPL playoff cricket, defending champions Royal Challengers Bengaluru will take on Gujarat Titans in Qualifier One in Dharamshala today. The match is scheduled to begin at 7:30 PM. The winner of the match will advance directly to the IPL 2026 final, while th...
body: In IPL playoff cricket, defending champions Royal Challengers Bengaluru will take on Gujarat Titans in Qualifier One in Dharamshala today. The match is scheduled to begin at 7:30 PM. The winner of the match will advance directly to the IPL 2026 final, while the losing side will get another opportunity in Qualifier Two. Tomorrow, Sunrisers Hyderabad will face Rajasthan Royals in the Eliminator in New Chandigarh, Mohali, at 7:30 PM. The losing team will be knocked out of the tournament, while the winner will move on to Qualifier Two. Royal Challengers Bengaluru were the first team to qualify for the playoffs after their victory over Punjab Kings. They finished top of the table with 18 points from 14 matches, securing the number one position on Net Run Rate ahead of Gujarat Titans and Sunrisers Hyderabad, who also ended with 18 points. Rajasthan Royals secured the fourth and final playoff spot with 16 points.

title: Yogasana to be pushed for inclusion in future Olympics, says Sports Minister Mansukh Mandaviya
summary: Youth Affairs and Sports Minister Mansukh Mandaviya today said that Yogasana would be pushed for inclusion in the future Olympic Games roster, keeping in mind India’s bid to host the 2036 Games. Addressing a press conference in New Delhi, Mr Mandaviya said tha...
body: Youth Affairs and Sports Minister Mansukh Mandaviya today said that Yogasana would be pushed for inclusion in the future Olympic Games roster, keeping in mind India’s bid to host the 2036 Games. Addressing a press conference in New Delhi, Mr Mandaviya said that Ahmedabad will host the first World Yogasana Championship from 4th to 8th of next month, marking an important step in positioning the country’s traditional wellness practice as a global sporting discipline. The Minister said Yogasana reflects the country’s soft power and has emerged as a medium for promoting physical fitness, mental well-being and healthy living across the world. Mr Mandaviya said yoga, which originated in India thousands of years ago, is now being widely accepted globally and has become an integral part of daily life in several countries. He said that around 75 countries have registered for the championship and over 5 hundred participants from across the world are expected to take part in the event. He added that the growing international acceptance of yoga presents opportunities for wellness, employment and global outreach. The Minister said Yogasana combines fitness, discipline and mental balance while also creating livelihood opportunities for yoga trainers and wellness professionals. He emphasized that India should take the lead in promoting Yogasana internationally. During the event, the Minister also launched the official mascot, jersey and championship film of the First World Yogasana Championship 2026. The launches highlighted the government’s efforts to promote Yogasana as a global sporting discipline and strengthen awareness about the upcoming international event.

title: SEBI explores tokenisation of corporate bonds using blockchain-based technology to improve transparency
summary: Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) Chairman Tuhin Kanta Pandey today said corporate bonds are not risk-free, even as the market regulator plans major reforms to deepen India’s debt market and improve investor participation. Addressing the CareEdge D...
body: Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) Chairman Tuhin Kanta Pandey today said corporate bonds are not risk-free, even as the market regulator plans major reforms to deepen India’s debt market and improve investor participation. Addressing the CareEdge Debt Market Summit in Mumbai, the SEBI chief said the regulator is exploring a pilot project for tokenisation of corporate bonds using blockchain-based technology to improve settlement efficiency, transparency and accessibility. He said India’s corporate bond market has expanded significantly, with outstanding corporate bonds rising from over 17 lakh crore rupees in finacial year 2015 to over 59 lakh crore rupees in financial year 2026. Debt issuances in this fiscal mobilised over 9 lakh crore rupees, nearly double the amount raised through equity markets. However, Mr Pandey noted that challenges remain, including low retail participation, limited liquidity and concentration of issuers in a few sectors and ratings categories. He said bonds carry credit, interest-rate and liquidity risks and stressed the need for investor education and transparency. The SEBI chairman said the regulator is working with the Reserve Bank of India and market participants to strengthen market-making mechanisms and improve secondary market liquidity. He added that SEBI is also considering a separate regulatory framework for debt brokers and reviewing compliance requirements for debt-only listed entities. Mr Pandey said SEBI will conduct outreach programmes to encourage more companies, especially SMEs, to raise funds through bond markets. The regulator is also reviewing the municipal bond framework to increase retail participation and help finance urban infrastructure projects.

title: Norway Chess 2026: R Praggnanandhaa & D Gukesh win their opening matches
summary: The Norway Chess 2026 started with big action in Oslo. Indian players R Praggnanandhaa and D Gukesh both won their opening matches in Armageddon after draws in classical games. Praggnanandhaa beat Wesley So in the tiebreak. Gukesh also won against Vincent Keym...
body: The Norway Chess 2026 started with big action in Oslo. Indian players R Praggnanandhaa and D Gukesh both won their opening matches in Armageddon after draws in classical games. Praggnanandhaa beat Wesley So in the tiebreak. Gukesh also won against Vincent Keymer. Keymer was close to winning in the classical game, but Gukesh defended well and saved the game. In the Women’s tournament, reigning Women’s World Champion Ju Wenjun and Divya Deshmukh also ended in a draw after a tense battle in which Ju Wenjun was pressing for a win. Divya delivered a strong performance in the Armageddon decider, winning the tiebreak game. Another major result came from Bibisara Assaubayeva, who defeated Indian No. 1 Koneru Humpy in classical chess.

title: Asian Under-23 Wrestling: Mohit Kumar and Chander Mohan clinch gold medals in Men’s Freestyle event
summary: Indian wrestlers continued their impressive performance at the Asian Under-23 Wrestling Championships in Da Nang, Vietnam today, securing two gold, two silver, and one bronze medal in the men’s freestyle category. In the 65 kilogram category, Mohit Kumar clinc...
body: Indian wrestlers continued their impressive performance at the Asian Under-23 Wrestling Championships in Da Nang, Vietnam today, securing two gold, two silver, and one bronze medal in the men’s freestyle category. In the 65 kilogram category, Mohit Kumar clinched the gold medal with a dominant 12-4 victory over Kyrgyzstan’s Oskonbai A. Abdisamatov. Chandermohan also struck gold in the 79 kilogram final after edging past Kazakhstan’s Yerkhan Abil in a thrilling 7-6 contest. Meanwhile, Punit Kumar secured a silver medal in the 92 kilogram category after going down 12-1 to Kyrgyzstan’s Ulukbek Soorombekov in the final. In the 125 kilogram division, Lacky also settled for silver following a 10-0 defeat against Kazakhstan’s Rizabek Aitmukhan in the title clash. In the 86 kilogram category, Sachin Mor bagged the bronze medal by defeating Uzbekistan’s Boburbek Ruzimboev 4-1 in the bronze medal match.

title: Khadi achieves record sale of 1.87 thousand cr rupees in FY 2025-26
summary: The Khadi and Village Industries Commission (KVIC) has achieved a historic milestone in the financial year 2025-26, recording sales of one lakh 87 thousand crore rupees. Addressing a press conference in New Delhi today, KVIC Chairman Manoj Kumar said that this...
body: The Khadi and Village Industries Commission (KVIC) has achieved a historic milestone in the financial year 2025-26, recording sales of one lakh 87 thousand crore rupees. Addressing a press conference in New Delhi today, KVIC Chairman Manoj Kumar said that this milestone has been achieved due to the hard work of millions of rural artisans. He underlined that compared to the year 2013-14, a remarkable growth of 501 percent in sales, 380 percent in production, and 56 percent in employment generation has been recorded over the last 12 years. Mr Kumar said that 12 years ago the turnover of Khadi industry stood at only 30 to 32 thousand crore rupees. He said, KVIC is providing employment to more than two crore people of which 80 per cent are women. The Chairman said that KVIC will soon launch e-commerce platform to further boost the business of khadi products. He added that Khadi is no longer merely a traditional product, but has evolved into a vibrant symbol of the New India self-reliant, indigenous pride, and rural prosperity.

title: Norway Chess 2026 begins in Oslo today with Round one matches
summary: The Norway Chess 2026 kicks off in Oslo, Norway, today with Round one matches. The tournament officially begins at 8:30 PM IST. For the first time in its 13-year history, the super-tournament has moved from Stavanger to the capital city of Oslo, hosting its ma...
body: The Norway Chess 2026 kicks off in Oslo, Norway, today with Round one matches. The tournament officially begins at 8:30 PM IST. For the first time in its 13-year history, the super-tournament has moved from Stavanger to the capital city of Oslo, hosting its matches at the iconic Deichman Bjørvika public library. This will be the 14th edition of Norway Chess and the third edition of Norway Chess Women. The prestigious six-player Open and Women’s categories feature top grandmasters, including Magnus Carlsen, D. Gukesh, and R. Praggnanandhaa.

title: Delhi Capitals defeat Kolkata Knight Riders by 40 runs in IPL at Eden Gardens
summary: In IPL Cricket, Delhi Capitals defeated Kolkata Knight Riders (KKR) by 40 runs at Eden Gardens in Kolkata last night. Delhi Capitals set a target of 204 runs for Kolkata Knight Riders (KKR). In reply, KKR scored only 163 runs in 18.4 overs. Put into bat, Delhi...
body: In IPL Cricket, Delhi Capitals defeated Kolkata Knight Riders (KKR) by 40 runs at Eden Gardens in Kolkata last night. Delhi Capitals set a target of 204 runs for Kolkata Knight Riders (KKR). In reply, KKR scored only 163 runs in 18.4 overs. Put into bat, Delhi Capitals posted 203 runs for the loss of 5 wickets in stipulated 20 overs. Earlier, Kolkata Knight Riders won the toss and opted to bowl. Kuldeep Yadav was declared Player of the Match. Earlier in the day, in another fixture, Rajasthan Royals defeated the host Mumbai Indians by 30 runs at Wankhede Stadium. Put into bat, Rajasthan Royals posted 205 runs for the loss of eight wickets in stipulated 20 overs. In reply, chasing a target of 206 runs, Mumbai Indians managed to score 175 runs for the loss of nine wickets in their allotted 20 overs.

title: India creates history with five medals at International Association of Ultrarunners 24-Hour Asia and Oceania Championships
summary: India created history at the International Association of Ultrarunners 24-Hour Asia and Oceania Championships held in Japan. India won a total of five medals and dominated the men’s category. Amar Singh Devanda won the gold medal after covering an impressive 2...
body: India created history at the International Association of Ultrarunners 24-Hour Asia and Oceania Championships held in Japan. India won a total of five medals and dominated the men’s category. Amar Singh Devanda won the gold medal after covering an impressive 282 point 881 kilometres in 24 hours. Geeno Antony claimed silver with 272 point 894 kilometres, while the bronze also went to India. In the women’s category, the Indian team secured the bronze medal with a combined distance of 667 point 722 kilometres. Tenzin Dolma was the top Indian performer, finishing fourth individually. The championship was held in Hirosaki City, Japan, with 81 athletes from nine countries participating in the endurance event.
