body: Prime Minister Narendra Modi shared an article written by Union Minister Pralhad Joshi and Director General of the International Solar Alliance Ashish Khanna, which highlights the emergence of solar power as a transformative force. In a social media post, the Prime Minister noted that India’s model of harnessing clean energy for agriculture offers a scalable model for Africa, strengthening shared prosperity, empowering communities and promising food security. He added that it is truly a matter of pride for every citizen, especially the farmers of the country.

title: Government debunks fake letter circulating on social media about “Operation Sindoor 2.0”
summary: The Government has debunked a letter circulating on social media, which claimed that a letter issued by Defence Secretary Rajesh Kumar Singh is proposing preparation for Operation Sindoor 2.0. The Fact Check Unit of the Press Information Bureau (PIB) informed ...
body: The Government has debunked a letter circulating on social media, which claimed that a letter issued by Defence Secretary Rajesh Kumar Singh is proposing preparation for Operation Sindoor 2.0. The Fact Check Unit of the Press Information Bureau (PIB) informed that the letter is fake. The PIB further added that neither the Defence Secretary nor the Defence Ministry has issued any such letter. PIB also urged the citizens to beware of fake news circulating online and always depend on reliable official sources for verified information.

title: PM Modi highlights transformation in India’s defence capabilities over the last decade
summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi has highlighted that India’s defence capabilities have witnessed significant transformation over the last decade, guided by the vision of self-reliance and powered by innovation, technology and indigenous manufacturing. Sharing a t...
body: Prime Minister Narendra Modi has highlighted that India’s defence capabilities have witnessed significant transformation over the last decade, guided by the vision of self-reliance and powered by innovation, technology and indigenous manufacturing. Sharing a thread of MyGov on his social media account, Mr Modi explained how the Country has strengthened its defence capabilities across air, land and sea; advanced indigenous technologies; and built a stronger foundation for self-reliance and national security. He said that this gives a glimpse of the strides India has made in the defence sector over the last 12 years.

title: One person killed after business jet crashes and catches fire on highway in Laredo, Texas
summary: In Texas, one person was killed after a business jet carrying six people crashed and caught fire on a highway in Laredo last night, said authorities. The aircraft, a Cessna Citation Latitude operated by NetJets, crashed on Loop 20 shortly, according to police ...
body: In Texas, one person was killed after a business jet carrying six people crashed and caught fire on a highway in Laredo last night, said authorities. The aircraft, a Cessna Citation Latitude operated by NetJets, crashed on Loop 20 shortly, according to police investigator Jose Baeza. Authorities did not immediately say whether the person who died was onboard the plane or on the ground. No injuries on the ground were reported, though five police officers were hospitalised for smoke inhalation. The cause of the crash was not immediately known.

title: PM Modi to arrive in Paris this evening during official visit to France
summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi will arrive in Paris this evening as part of his visit to France. Members of the Indian diaspora are eagerly awaiting his arrival, and preparations are underway to welcome him. Tomorrow morning, Prime Minister Modi is scheduled to ...
body: Prime Minister Narendra Modi will arrive in Paris this evening as part of his visit to France. Members of the Indian diaspora are eagerly awaiting his arrival, and preparations are underway to welcome him. Tomorrow morning, Prime Minister Modi is scheduled to hold meetings with leading Chief Executive Officers, with discussions expected to continue till the afternoon. Later, he will participate in the VivaTech Summit, one of Europe’s leading technology and innovation events, where he is expected to deliver an address. In the evening, the Prime Minister will attend a community event and interact with members of the Indian diaspora. The programme will also feature cultural performances highlighting India’s rich heritage and further strengthening the people-to-people ties between India and France.

title: Agricultural exports of India expand to nearly 500 products, says APEDA Chairman
summary: Chairman of the Agricultural and Processed Food Products Export Development Authority, Abhishek Dev, highlighted that India’s agricultural export basket has expanded from around 280 products a decade ago to nearly 500 products today, despite facing competition...
body: Chairman of the Agricultural and Processed Food Products Export Development Authority, Abhishek Dev, highlighted that India’s agricultural export basket has expanded from around 280 products a decade ago to nearly 500 products today, despite facing competition from major global exporters such as the United States, Brazil, China, and the European Union. Mr Dev informed us that the country’s agricultural exports reached 53 billion dollars last year and consistently ranked among the world’s top ten agricultural exporting countries. He noted that the country has significantly diversified its agricultural exports beyond traditional products such as rice and spices to include pulses, cocoa products, pineapples, grapes, and pomegranates, which are now being shipped to markets across dozens of countries.

title: Union Ministers Shivraj Singh Chouhan and Bhupender Yadav chair review on crop residue burning
summary: Union Agriculture and Farmers’ Welfare Minister Shivraj Singh Chouhan and Environment, Forest and Climate Change Minister Bhupender Yadav chaired a high-level inter-ministerial meeting in New Delhi to review stubble management measures and discuss long-term so...
body: Union Agriculture and Farmers’ Welfare Minister Shivraj Singh Chouhan and Environment, Forest and Climate Change Minister Bhupender Yadav chaired a high-level inter-ministerial meeting in New Delhi to review stubble management measures and discuss long-term solutions to crop residue burning. During the meeting, held yesterday, Mr Chouhan highlighted the role of active monitoring by the Stubble Protection Force across 70 tehsils in 14 districts of the National Capital Region. He said that stubble burning not only pollutes the environment but also affects the health of Mother Earth, destroying beneficial insects and reducing soil fertility. Mr Chouhan added that states have set ambitious targets for the current year, including the distribution of more than 46 thousand Crop Residue Management machines, the establishment of 910 Custom Hiring Centres, and the development of 141 stubble supply chain projects. He informed that a provision of over 544 crore rupees has been made under the Crop Residue Management scheme for the year 2026-27, out of which the first instalment of more than 272 crore has already been released. Further, both Ministers reiterated the government’s commitment to eliminating the problem of stubble burning through mechanisation, technical innovation, industrial use of stubble, public awareness and coordinated efforts of all stakeholders.

title: Union Minister Piyush Goyal calls on Prince Albert II of Monaco to discuss bilateral cooperation
summary: Union Commerce and Industry Minister Piyush Goyal called on Prince Albert II of Monaco. In a social media post, Mr Goyal said that during their meeting, they discussed expanding the India-Monaco partnership in sectors such as sustainability, the blue economy, ...
body: Union Commerce and Industry Minister Piyush Goyal called on Prince Albert II of Monaco. In a social media post, Mr Goyal said that during their meeting, they discussed expanding the India-Monaco partnership in sectors such as sustainability, the blue economy, innovation, and emerging technologies. He also briefed Prince Albert II on India’s flagship Bharat Innovates 2026 event, which concluded yesterday in Nice, France, showcasing India’s vibrant technology and innovation ecosystem and strengthening global collaboration. Mr Goyal expressed confidence that India-Monaco relations will continue to scale new heights in the years ahead.

title: Japan reports trade deficit of $2.34 billion in May after four months of surplus
summary: Japan witnessed a trade deficit of 2.34 billion US dollars in May, which is its first deficit in four months, government data showed today. The deficit in May was down 42.8% from a year earlier. Meanwhile, exports rose 17%, driven by strong demand for semicond...
body: Japan witnessed a trade deficit of 2.34 billion US dollars in May, which is its first deficit in four months, government data showed today. The deficit in May was down 42.8% from a year earlier. Meanwhile, exports rose 17%, driven by strong demand for semiconductors, electronic components and motor vehicles. Imports increased 12.5%, led by purchases of communication devices, according to the Finance Ministry. Japan’s crude oil imports fell 57.3% to 4.73 million kilolitres as conflict in the Middle East disrupted transportation through the Strait of Hormuz.

title: 8th BRICS Anti-Drug Working Group Meeting held virtually under India’s BRICS Presidency
summary: The 8th BRICS Anti-Drug Working Group Meeting was convened virtually under India’s BRICS Presidency. The Narcotics Control Bureau (NCB) informed us that delegates from India, China, Ethiopia, Indonesia, Russia, South Africa and the UAE took part in the meeting...
body: The 8th BRICS Anti-Drug Working Group Meeting was convened virtually under India’s BRICS Presidency. The Narcotics Control Bureau (NCB) informed us that delegates from India, China, Ethiopia, Indonesia, Russia, South Africa and the UAE took part in the meeting. During the meeting, the delegates exchanged national drug situation reports and deliberated on emerging drug threats, trafficking trends, operational challenges and best practices in drug control. NCB said the meeting underscored the shared commitment of BRICS nations to strengthen international cooperation, enhance information exchange, build capacities and advance coordinated efforts to counter illicit drug trafficking and drug abuse.

title: Sri Lanka unveils new five-year export strategy targeting $36 billion exports by 2030
summary: Seeking to strengthen economic recovery after the 2022 financial crisis, Sri Lanka has unveiled a new five-year export strategy targeting 36 billion US dollars in export earnings by 2030. The National Export Development Plan, NEDP, for 2026 to 2030 has been ap...
body: Seeking to strengthen economic recovery after the 2022 financial crisis, Sri Lanka has unveiled a new five-year export strategy targeting 36 billion US dollars in export earnings by 2030. The National Export Development Plan, NEDP, for 2026 to 2030 has been approved by the Cabinet. The plan aims to transform the island nation into an export-driven economy and establish it as a competitive logistics and knowledge hub. Launching the strategy, Sri Lankan Industries and Entrepreneurship Development Minister Sunil Handunnetti said sustained policy consistency and transparency would be essential to achieving the country’s export ambitions. The framework identifies eight priority sectors, including digital products and services, auto components, electrical and electronic components, processed food and beverages, spices, rubber, as well as marine-based and mineral-based industries. Chairman of the Export Development Board, Mangala Wijesinghe, said the plan would improve trade logistics, accelerate customs digitisation and help small and medium-sized enterprises gain access to global markets. He said these measures would support Sri Lanka’s long-term economic transformation and enhance the country’s competitiveness in international trade.

title: India and Thailand agree to deepen defence cooperation in manufacturing, research and innovation during 10th Defence Dialogue
summary: India and Thailand agreed to deepen collaboration in manufacturing, research, innovation and capability development during the 10th Defence Dialogue. The 10th Thailand-India Defence Dialogue was co-chaired in Bangkok yesterday by Thailand’s Deputy Permanent Se...
body: India and Thailand agreed to deepen collaboration in manufacturing, research, innovation and capability development during the 10th Defence Dialogue. The 10th Thailand-India Defence Dialogue was co-chaired in Bangkok yesterday by Thailand’s Deputy Permanent Secretary for Defence Admiral Nuttapol Diewvanich and Joint Secretary of the Ministry of Defence Satyajit Mohanty from the Indian side. The meeting reviewed the full spectrum of bilateral defence cooperation and exchanged views on regional and global security issues of mutual interest. The two sides discussed the evolving security environment in the Indo-Pacific region and exchanged perspectives on regional developments and reaffirmed the importance of strengthening cooperation to promote peace, stability and prosperity in the region. Discussions also covered the ongoing military-to-military engagements, capacity-building initiatives, training exchanges, maritime cooperation and other areas of mutual interest. They also exchanged views on cooperation under regional and multilateral defence frameworks, including Association of Southeast Asian Nations (ASEAN)-led mechanisms. Further, they reaffirmed their commitment to strengthening practical cooperation and addressing shared security challenges through dialogue and collaboration.

title: Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla pays tribute to Maharana Pratap on birth anniversary
summary: The Lok Sabha Speaker paid tributes to the valiant hero Maharana Pratap on his birth anniversary today. In a social media post, Mr Birla said that the life of Maharana Pratap is an immortal saga of unwavering self-respect, self-esteem, freedom, and national de...
body: The Lok Sabha Speaker paid tributes to the valiant hero Maharana Pratap on his birth anniversary today. In a social media post, Mr Birla said that the life of Maharana Pratap is an immortal saga of unwavering self-respect, self-esteem, freedom, and national devotion. He added that even in the most adverse circumstances, Maharana Pratap never compromised on his principles, culture, and the honour of the motherland. The Lok Sabha speaker underlined that from the battlefield of Haldighati to the impenetrable hills of Dever and Aravali, the life of Maharana Pratap stands as a unique example of sacrifice, patience, and indomitable willpower. He added that his valour is not merely the heritage of Mewar or Rajasthan but an inspirational treasure for the entire Bharatvarsha. Mr Birla urged everyone to take a resolve to imbibe Maharana Pratap’s ideals of patriotism, duty, and self-respect in their lives.

title: Security tightened across J&K ahead of annual Amarnath Yatra
summary: In Jammu and Kashmir, authorities have tightened security across the Union Territory ahead of the annual Amarnath Yatra, with multi-layered arrangements, mock drills and coordinated exercises being carried out to ensure the smooth conduct of the pilgrimage. Se...
body: In Jammu and Kashmir, authorities have tightened security across the Union Territory ahead of the annual Amarnath Yatra, with multi-layered arrangements, mock drills and coordinated exercises being carried out to ensure the smooth conduct of the pilgrimage. Security forces, police and civil administration have activated a comprehensive security grid covering transit routes, base camps, railway infrastructure, lodgement centres and other critical installations linked to the pilgrimage, scheduled to begin early next month. The police have strengthened security measures along the traditional Pahalgam route. Specialised teams are carrying out anti-sabotage checks, area domination exercises and sanitisation drives from Khanabal to Pahalgam, covering transit points, parking areas, accommodation centres and vulnerable locations. The operations are being supported by bomb disposal squads, sniffer dog units and technical surveillance teams. Security personnel are also conducting inspections of vehicles, including abandoned and unattended vehicles, while surveillance has been enhanced in forest areas and along key stretches of the pilgrimage route. Authorities have also introduced QR code-based verification for service providers associated with the yatra, including pony operators and other workers, to strengthen identification and monitoring mechanisms. As part of broader preparedness efforts, security forces conducted a mock drill at the Chichi Mata Temple in Samba district to assess response capabilities in the event of a terror attack or other emergency situation. The exercise involved coordinated action by multiple agencies and focused on evacuation procedures, emergency response and crowd management. Security arrangements have also been reviewed at railway stations, bridges, culverts and other critical infrastructure in several districts. Regular searches and area sanitisation drives are being undertaken as part of preventive measures. To strengthen administrative coordination during the pilgrimage, the Jammu and Kashmir Government has temporarily deputed 63 officers from various departments. The officers will assist in managing logistics, pilgrim facilities, inter-departmental coordination and other operational requirements during the yatra. Authorities have appealed to the public to remain vigilant and immediately report any suspicious activity or unattended objects to security agencies as preparations gather pace for the annual pilgrimage.

title: HM Amit Shah pays tribute to the third Sarsanghchalak of the RSS, Balasaheb Deoras on death anniversary
summary: Union Home Minister Amit Shah has paid tributes to the third Sarsanghchalak of the Rashtriya Swayamsevak Sangh, Balasaheb Deoras, on his death anniversary today. In a social media post, Mr Shah said that Balasaheb Deoras dedicated his life to national service,...
body: Union Home Minister Amit Shah has paid tributes to the third Sarsanghchalak of the Rashtriya Swayamsevak Sangh, Balasaheb Deoras, on his death anniversary today. In a social media post, Mr Shah said that Balasaheb Deoras dedicated his life to national service, social harmony, and the character building of the youth and considered untouchability to be the greatest curse. He added that Balasaheb Deoras was a leader who shaped the Sangh even during the difficult days of the Emergency and motivated the youth to safeguard democracy. The Home Minister noted that he will continue to be an inspiration for everyone.

title: Brent crude oil prices fall 5.4% to 78.66 dollars per barrel amid global market easing
summary: The price for a barrel of Brent crude fell 5.4 per cent to 78.66 dollars. With this, the oil prices dipped below 80 dollars per barrel for the first time since March this year. The dip comes amid optimism continuing that a tentative U.S.-Iran deal on their war...
body: The price for a barrel of Brent crude fell 5.4 per cent to 78.66 dollars. With this, the oil prices dipped below 80 dollars per barrel for the first time since March this year. The dip comes amid optimism continuing that a tentative U.S.-Iran deal on their war will reopen the Strait of Hormuz at the end of the week and get the global flow of oil going again. In the wake of US and Israeli strikes on Iran beginning on the 28th of February, crude oil prices skyrocketed, climbing up to nearly 120 dollars per barrel. However, prices have fallen precipitously since the opening of futures trading for the week on Sunday evening after President Trump announced Washington and Tehran had agreed to the terms of a memorandum of understanding to end the war that has roiled the global energy market.

title: CEC Gyanesh Kumar congratulates IIIIDEM team on completion of 15 years of service in electoral training and management
summary: Chief Election Commissioner (CEC) Gyanesh Kumar today congratulated the India International Institute of Democracy and Election Management (IIIIDEM) team for completing 15 years in service of electoral training and management. Mr Kumar said that during the Lok...
body: Chief Election Commissioner (CEC) Gyanesh Kumar today congratulated the India International Institute of Democracy and Election Management (IIIIDEM) team for completing 15 years in service of electoral training and management. Mr Kumar said that during the Lok Sabha elections, the institute shoulders the responsibility of imparting training nearly one crore 80 lakh officials. He was speaking during the conference on capacity building and strategic roadmap organised by IIIDEM in New Delhi. During the two day event which began yesterday, over 450 participants including National and State Level Master Trainers too part to discuss emerging priorities and chart the future direction of electoral capacity building. Election Commission said that since 2011, IIIDEM has conducted one thousand 885 training programmes reaching more than one lakh 20 thousand participants across national and international platforms. The commission also highlighted that IIIDEM’s international training footprint reaches 142 countries.

title: President Droupadi Murmu will embark on 5-day visit to Madhya Pradesh & Odisha from June 18
summary: President Droupadi Murmu will embark on a five-day visit to Madhya Pradesh and Odisha beginning tomorrow. The President’s Secretariat said that on the first day of her visit, President Murmu will attend a programme on the empowerment of tribal society through ...
body: President Droupadi Murmu will embark on a five-day visit to Madhya Pradesh and Odisha beginning tomorrow. The President’s Secretariat said that on the first day of her visit, President Murmu will attend a programme on the empowerment of tribal society through spiritual awakening at Betul in Madhya Pradesh. On Friday, she will participate in the International Sickle Cell Day celebrations at Omkareshwar in Madhya Pradesh. Later the same day, the President will inaugurate a medical camp organised by AIIMS Bhubaneswar and dedicate a mega integrated rural piped water supply project for mining-affected villages under Kusumi block at Rairangpur in Odisha. On Saturday, President Murmu, along with Prime Minister Narendra Modi, will visit various facilities at Pahadpur, including School and Skill Centre at Pahadpur and interact with beneficiaries and children. Later, they will grace a public programme at Rairangpur. On 21st of this month, the President will participate in the International Day of Yoga celebrations at Jabalpur in Madhya Pradesh. Later in the day, she will preside over the 36th Convocation Ceremony of Rani Durgavati University. While on the final day of her visit, President Murmu will interact with members of the Sahariya tribe, cheetah trackers, tourist guides and the Kuno field team at Kuno National Park in Madhya Pradesh.

title: Govt Strengthens Women’s Safety Framework with Upgraded SHe-Box 2.0 Portal Under Mission Shakti Initiative
summary: The government has undertaken a series of structural and policy interventions to enhance women’s safety, dignity and participation across sectors. The Women and Child Development Ministry said that in this direction, Mission Shakti provides an integrated frame...
body: The government has undertaken a series of structural and policy interventions to enhance women’s safety, dignity and participation across sectors. The Women and Child Development Ministry said that in this direction, Mission Shakti provides an integrated framework for convergence of schemes and strengthening institutional mechanisms. The Ministry highlighted that within this framework, the Sexual Harassment electronic Box (SHe-Box) portal has been developed to operationalise the provisions of the Sexual Harassment of Women at Workplace (Prevention, Prohibition and Redressal) Act, 2013 (SH Act). To further strengthen the portal, the government launched the upgraded version SHe-Box 2.0 on 29th August 2024. The Ministry further said that SHe-Box 2.0 is a platform that provides single-window access for women across all organised, unorganised, public and private sectors to register and track complaints of sexual harassment at the workplace. The portal enables seamless coordination among stakeholders and strengthens the implementation framework of the SH Act by providing a more structured monitorable system. SHe-Box 2.0 provides a secure, confidential and user-friendly interface that enables women to report incidents without fear of stigma or retaliation. The portal is available in 22 languages, including Hindi and English, ensuring inclusivity and wider accessibility across diverse user groups.

title: Taiwanese President Lai Ching-te welcomes G7 leaders statement
summary: Taiwanese President Lai Ching-te today welcomed G7 leaders statement reaffirming their opposition to any unilateral attempts to change the status quo across the Taiwan Strait by force or coercion. Lai Ching-te stated that Taiwan will keep working with its part...
body: Taiwanese President Lai Ching-te today welcomed G7 leaders statement reaffirming their opposition to any unilateral attempts to change the status quo across the Taiwan Strait by force or coercion. Lai Ching-te stated that Taiwan will keep working with its partners to uphold regional peace and prosperity. In the statement on geopolitical issues, the G7 leaders reaffirmed opposition to any unilateral attempts to change the status quo, especially by force or coercion, in the East and South China Seas and across the Taiwan Strait. The statement comes as China has increased the frequency and scale of its military exercises around Taiwan in recent years.

title: Govt Debunks False Claims Linking E20 Petrol to ant attraction around vehicle fuel caps
summary: The Government has refuted several social media posts circulating online, claiming that ants are gathering around the fuel filler caps of vehicles using E20 petrol. The Fact Check Unit of the Press Information Bureau (PIB) has clarified that the claim is false...
body: The Government has refuted several social media posts circulating online, claiming that ants are gathering around the fuel filler caps of vehicles using E20 petrol. The Fact Check Unit of the Press Information Bureau (PIB) has clarified that the claim is false and said that there is no scientific basis for linking E20 fuel with ant attraction. It added that E20 fuel does not contain any identifiable attractant that would cause ants or other insects to congregate around vehicle fuel caps.

title: Yoga is not merely form of exercise but powerful science of holistic living, says AIIMS Media In-charge & Professor Dr Rima Dada,
summary: All India Institute of Medical Sciences, Media In-charge and Professor, Dr Rima Dada, said that yoga is not merely a form of exercise but a powerful science of holistic living. Talking exclusively to Akashvani news, she highlighted the transformative role of y...
body: All India Institute of Medical Sciences, Media In-charge and Professor, Dr Rima Dada, said that yoga is not merely a form of exercise but a powerful science of holistic living. Talking exclusively to Akashvani news, she highlighted the transformative role of yoga in addressing Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS), a lifestyle-related disorder that is increasingly affecting women across India. Emphasising the importance of yoga in the prevention and management of PCOS, Dr Dada said scientific studies have demonstrated that regular yoga practice helps regulate menstrual cycles, restore hormonal balance, improve insulin sensitivity, and support healthy weight management. She said that yoga, when combined with a balanced diet and regular physical activity, can significantly improve reproductive and metabolic health. Dr Dada added that yoga is a vital tool in combating the growing burden of PCOS.

title: Union Minister Dr Jitendra Singh releases book highlighting yoga’s role in reducing type 2 Diabetes
summary: Science and Technology Minister Dr Jitendra Singh today said that Yoga is no longer viewed merely as a traditional wellness practice but is increasingly emerging as an evidence-based preventive healthcare intervention with global relevance. He said this while ...
body: Science and Technology Minister Dr Jitendra Singh today said that Yoga is no longer viewed merely as a traditional wellness practice but is increasingly emerging as an evidence-based preventive healthcare intervention with global relevance. He said this while releasing a book titled Yoga and Prevention of Type 2 Diabetes: An Integrated Approach to Wellness, postulating 40 percent reduction of risk of Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus with Yoga, ahead of the International Day of Yoga. Referring to the theme of this year’s International Day of Yoga, Yoga for Healthy Ageing, he said that healthy ageing is not only about increasing life expectancy but also about ensuring a life free from chronic diseases and disability. The Minister highlighted that the book gains particular significance in light of the findings of the Indian Prevention of Diabetes Study, the first long-term randomized controlled trial demonstrating that Yoga can reduce the risk of progression from prediabetes to Type 2 Diabetes by nearly 40 percent. The book provides a comprehensive overview of the role of Yoga in the prevention and management of Type 2 Diabetes. It reviews the available scientific evidence, examines the physiological and metabolic mechanisms through which Yoga exerts its beneficial effects, and offers practical guidance on Yogic practices that may help reduce diabetes risk.

title: India will have 300 GW of installed non-fossil fuel capacity by end of this quarter: Union Minister Pralhad Joshi
summary: Union Minister for New and Renewable Energy, Pralhad Joshi today emphasised that India will have 300 gigawatts of installed non-fossil fuel capacity by the end of this quarter. Mr Joshi said this while addressing the Workshop on Strengthening the National Gree...
body: Union Minister for New and Renewable Energy, Pralhad Joshi today emphasised that India will have 300 gigawatts of installed non-fossil fuel capacity by the end of this quarter. Mr Joshi said this while addressing the Workshop on Strengthening the National Green Hydrogen Mission: through State Policies, Hubs and Infrastructure in New Delhi. During the address, Mr Joshi said that, under the leadership of Prime Minister Narendra Modi, India has undertaken one of the world’s fastest and largest clean energy transformations. He added that in the last 12 years India has become a global leader in the energy transition sector. Talking about the National Green Hydrogen Mission, the Minister said that the initiative is aimed at attracting an investment of 8 lakh crore rupees, generating 6 lakh jobs in the sector and reducing annual carbon emissions by 50 million tonnes. The Minister announced that the Green Hydrogen Mission has allocated 100 crore rupees for supporting Start Ups on green hydrogen technologies. He informed that the first list of nine startups is being approved with a total funding of 22 crore rupees. He also underscored the Government’s focus on innovation and standards, noting that a dedicated research and development roadmap worth over one hundred crore is supporting 21 approved projects. Mr Joshi also praised the major Indian private companies for their contribution in the sector. On the occasion, he also launched the Green Hydrogen Certification Portal of India. Developed by the Ministry of New and Renewable Energy, the portal will facilitate transparent certification and regulatory compliance under the Green Hydrogen Certification Scheme. The workshop was organised as part of the celebrations marking 12 Years of Decisive Achievement of the Government, highlighting the country’s progress in clean energy transition, renewable energy deployment, green hydrogen and industrial decarbonisation.

title: Agriculture Ministry says Kharif Crop area coverage crosses 84 Lakh hectares as of June 12
summary: The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare has informed that the progress of area coverage under Kharif Crops has recorded more than 84 lakh hectare area as of 12th of this month. The Ministry said that kharif crops have witnessed a slight decline in area...
body: The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare has informed that the progress of area coverage under Kharif Crops has recorded more than 84 lakh hectare area as of 12th of this month. The Ministry said that kharif crops have witnessed a slight decline in area coverage of 3.44 lakh hectare area compared to the corresponding period of last year. It added that the Sugarcane was cultivated in over 54 lakh hectare area, while Cotton was sown over more than 9 lakh hectare area. According to the Ministry, more than 6 lakh hectare area was covered with Jute and Mesta. Additionally, rice was sown over nearly 5 lakh hectare area, while pulses were cultivated in more than one lakh hectare area. The Ministry informed that Shri Anna and Coarse Cereals were grown on more than 4 lakh hectare areas. Meanwhile, the area cultivated with oilseeds was recorded with area coverage of more than 3 lakh hectares.

title: Delhi LG Taranjit Singh Sandhu chairs meeting to review implementation of POCSO Act across national capital
summary: Delhi Lieutenant Governor (LG), Taranjit Singh Sandhu, today chaired a meeting to review the implementation status of the Protection of Children from Sexual Offences (POCSO) Act across the national capital. On the occasion, Mr Sandhu directed a comprehensive a...
body: Delhi Lieutenant Governor (LG), Taranjit Singh Sandhu, today chaired a meeting to review the implementation status of the Protection of Children from Sexual Offences (POCSO) Act across the national capital. On the occasion, Mr Sandhu directed a comprehensive audit of schools across Delhi to assess adherence to the provisions and guidelines of the POCSO Act. The Education Department has also been instructed to enforce strict compliance and initiate firm action against institutions found lacking in implementation. The LG further directed Delhi Police to ensure robust and visible deployment around school premises and prominent student hubs, particularly during school dispersal hours, to proactively deter any untoward incidents and strengthen public confidence. On the occasion, Mr Sandhu emphasized a zero-tolerance approach towards eve-teasing, harassment, and molestation of women and children. He asserted that the safety, dignity, and well-being of every student remain non-negotiable. The LG said that government is committed to building a secure, fearless, and protective environment for every child in Delhi.

title: Norway open FIFA World Cup 2026 campaign with dominant 4–1 win over Iraq in Group I
summary: In Football, Norway returned to the FIFA World Cup in style with a 4-1 win over Iraq in their Group I opener at the Boston Stadium in the United States this morning. Norway’s Erling Haaland netted twice to make his World Cup debut special. The scoreline now pu...
body: In Football, Norway returned to the FIFA World Cup in style with a 4-1 win over Iraq in their Group I opener at the Boston Stadium in the United States this morning. Norway’s Erling Haaland netted twice to make his World Cup debut special. The scoreline now puts Norway at the top of Group I. Earlier, France defeated Senegal 3-1 in the opening game of Group I of the FIFA World Cup 2026 at New Jersey Stadium last night. Kylian Mbappe scored a brilliant brace to become France’s all-time leading scorer with 58 goals. He also surpassed Lionel Messi in the all-time World Cup scoring charts. In the game, Senegal matched France in a tightly contested first half, but the breakthrough came just after the hour mark when Michael Olise played a precise pass into Mbappe, who calmly finished to open the scoring. Paris Saint-Germain forward Bradley Barcola doubled the lead in the 79th minute, delicately lifting the ball over Edouard Mendy after coming off the bench, before Ibrahim Mbaye pulled one back for Senegal to reduce the deficit. The next target for Mbappe will be Miroslav Klose’s record 16 World Cup finals goals. The Frenchman currently has 14 in the global showpiece. Earlier, Iran secured a hard-fought 2-2 draw against New Zealand in their opening match at SoFi Stadium in Los Angeles. In another fixture, Uruguay and Saudi Arabia played out a 1-1 draw in their opening clash in Miami. Belgium and Egypt also shared the points with a 1-1 draw in their Group G opener at the Seattle Stadium.

title: 4th India-Netherlands Cyber Dialogue takes place in New Delhi
summary: The Fourth India-Netherlands Cyber Dialogue was held in New Delhi, in which the two sides exchanged views on the evolving cyber threat landscape, national strategies and policies, data protection, transnational cybercrime and law enforcement. The dialogue whic...
body: The Fourth India-Netherlands Cyber Dialogue was held in New Delhi, in which the two sides exchanged views on the evolving cyber threat landscape, national strategies and policies, data protection, transnational cybercrime and law enforcement. The dialogue which began yesterday, was co-chaired by Joint Secretary for Cyber Diplomacy at the Ministry of External Affairs, Amit A. Shukla and Ambassador-at-large for Cyber Affairs at the Ministry of Foreign Affairs of the Netherlands, Ernst Noorman. During the meeting, they discussed cooperation in multilateral fora, including the latest developments on these issues at the United Nations, including the UN Convention against Cybercrime and AI Governance. The meeting also focused on strengthening bilateral cooperation in cyber matters and continuing engagement on joint capacity-building and training initiatives. Both sides reviewed avenues for collaboration on the protection of critical information infrastructure and supply chain vulnerability. The meeting also took forward the discussions held in the field of cyber and digital technologies in line with the Roadmap of India-Netherlands Strategic Partnership- 2026-2030. The Roadmap was released during the visit of Prime Minister Narendra Modi to the Netherlands last month.

title: PM Modi meets US President on sidelines of G7 Summit in Evian; Issues of seafarers safety & Opening of Strait of Hormuz figure prominently
summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi today emphasised that keeping the Strait of Hormuz open is very important for global economy. During his bilateral meeting with US President Donald Trump on the sidelines of G7 Summit in Evian, Prime Minister Modi stressed on...
body: Prime Minister Narendra Modi today emphasised that keeping the Strait of Hormuz open is very important for global economy. During his bilateral meeting with US President Donald Trump on the sidelines of G7 Summit in Evian, Prime Minister Modi stressed on ensuring freedom of navigation. Mr Modi added that for maritime trade, lakhs of Indian seafarers are providing their services in different regions of the world. Highlighting seafarers’ contribution, Mr Modi said their safety is of paramount importance.He also commended President Trump for the progress in peace efforts in West Asia. Mr Modi hoped that lasting peace will return to the region. He expressed happiness that the trade talks between India and US are progressing smoothly. Earlier, on the margins of the meeting in Evian, Prime Minister Modi and German Chancellor Friedrich Merz today exchanged views on regional and global developments, including the situation in West Asia and the Russia-Ukraine conflict. The two leaders also reviewed the progress in bilateral engagements and expressed satisfaction at the renewed momentum in the India-Germany Strategic Partnership, with the successful visit of Mr Merz to India and the conclusion of the India-EU FTA negotiations earlier this year. As India and Germany celebrate the 75th anniversary of diplomatic ties in 2026, the two leaders reaffirmed their commitment to further strengthen cooperation across diverse sectors, including trade and investment, defence and security, green and sustainable development, technology, innovation, education and mobility. They welcomed the signing of the Defence Industrial Cooperation Roadmap and the operationalisation of a transit visa waiver for Indian nationals transiting through Germany. Mr Merz invited the Prime Minister for the 8th India-Germany Inter-Governmental Consultations (IGC) scheduled to be held later this year in Germany. Prime Minister Modi also held a meeting with President of the European Commission Ursula von der Leyen and President of the European Council António Costa on the sidelines of the Summit. The leaders exchanged views on regional and global issues of mutual interest and welcomed recent developments in West Asia. They reaffirmed their shared commitment to promoting a resilient multipolar global order that supports peace, stability, prosperity and sustainable development. The leaders noted the recent conclusion of negotiations on the India-European Union Free Trade Agreement, describing it as a historic achievement. Earlier in the day, Prime Minister Modi also addressed a crucial working session themed “Promoting balanced, inclusive and sustainable economic growth for the benefit of all” at the ongoing G7 Summit in Evian, France. In his address, the Prime Minister underscored the need for international financial institutions to develop mechanisms that impart economic resilience to developing countries. He also proposed connectivity projects linking Africa, Latin America and the Pacific Island countries. He also suggested the creation of International Mobilization Partnership for Accelerating Connectivity and Trade (IMPACT), in which G7’s capital, India’s talent and Global South’s ownership could be combined. During the session Mr Modi also highlighted that tangible growth leads to inclusion and well-being of the people and spoke about India’s growth story underpinned by “Sabka Sath, Sabka Vikas, Sabka Vishwas, Sabka Prayas”. The high-level session was attended by G7 member nations, partner countries, and key representatives from the International Monetary Fund and the OECD. PM Modi also participated in a working lunch focused on “Ensuring the safe, rapid and effective deployment of artificial intelligence. The meeting brought together G7 member countries, partner nations, and the CEOs of leading AI companies to discuss the future of digital innovation. title: India, France reaffirm commitment to Innovation, AI & Technology Cooperation at VivaTech 2026 in Paris
summary: India and France have reaffirmed their commitment to innovation, artificial intelligence and technology cooperation at the inauguration of the India Pavilion at VivaTech 2026 in Paris. Speaking at the event, Union Commerce and Industry Minister Piyush Goyal an...
body: India and France have reaffirmed their commitment to innovation, artificial intelligence and technology cooperation at the inauguration of the India Pavilion at VivaTech 2026 in Paris. Speaking at the event, Union Commerce and Industry Minister Piyush Goyal and French Minister for Artificial Intelligence and Digital Affairs Anne Le Hénanff highlighted the growing strategic partnership between the two countries. Addressing innovators and startups, Piyush Goyal said the India-France partnership has been strengthened further through the India-France Year of Innovation 2026 announced by Prime Minister Narendra Modi and French President Emmanuel Macron. He said India offers key advantages for the AI era, including a young population, a large pool of STEM graduates, strong digital infrastructure and democratic governance. The Minister underlined India’s vision of human-centric artificial intelligence through the concept of “MANAV”, emphasising ethical, accountable, inclusive and trustworthy AI. He said India is prepared to become a global hub for AI talent, data centres and advanced technologies. French Minister Anne Le Hénanff described India as a global innovation powerhouse and welcomed its role as the AI Country Partner at VivaTech 2026. She said India’s technological achievements and leadership in responsible AI reflect Prime Minister Modi’s vision of technology serving humanity. Highlighting the Franco-Indian Year of Innovation, she said collaboration between startups, universities and research institutions is creating new opportunities. Anne Le Hénanff said France and India share common goals of innovation, responsibility and action, and are working together to promote trustworthy AI, digital resilience and sustainable technological development. The India Pavilion is one of the largest national pavilions at VivaTech 2026 and showcases the country’s growing startup and innovation ecosystem. title: Railways approves multiple projects worth over Rs 1200 crore to improve connectivity & safety
summary: Indian Railways has approved a series of infrastructure projects worth over 1,200 crore rupees aimed at enhancing rail capacity, improving freight movement, strengthening safety systems and upgrading urban transport infrastructure across Gujarat, Maharashtra, ...
body: Indian Railways has approved a series of infrastructure projects worth over 1,200 crore rupees aimed at enhancing rail capacity, improving freight movement, strengthening safety systems and upgrading urban transport infrastructure across Gujarat, Maharashtra, West Bengal and Madhya Pradesh. The projects include the doubling of a key railway section in Gujarat, construction of a new freight chord line in Maharashtra, the modernisation of signalling systems on Eastern Railway and the rebuilding of a major road overbridge in Indore. Together, the initiatives are expected to improve operational efficiency, decongest critical rail corridors, enhance passenger convenience and support economic growth. In a boost to rail connectivity in the Kutch region, the doubling of the 49-km Adipur-Bhuj section on Western Railway has been approved at an estimated cost of 493 crore rupees. Indian Railways has also approved the construction of the 3.7-km Somtane-Chikhli Chord Line on Central Railway at a cost of 172 crore rupees in Maharashtra. The new line will establish a direct rail connection between the Karjat and Roha routes, allowing freight trains to bypass the heavily congested Panvel Junction. In a major modernisation initiative, Indian Railways has sanctioned a 405-crore-rupee signalling upgradation project on Eastern Railway. The project involves replacing ageing relay-based interlocking systems with advanced Electronic Interlocking technology at 32 locations, including 25 stations and seven intermediate block signalling points located on high-density and highly utilised railway routes. To improve urban mobility and rail-road connectivity in Madhya Pradesh, the Railways has approved the reconstruction of the Shastriya Road Overbridge in Indore Yard at a cost of 139 crore rupees.

title: NTA warns candidates and parents against fake NEET re-exam paper scams on social media
summary: The National Testing Agency (NTA) has urged parents and candidates to beware of scammers ahead of NEET examinations. In a social media post, NTA said that scammers on Telegram are running channels demanding money ranging from 14 thousand to 25 thousand rupees ...
body: The National Testing Agency (NTA) has urged parents and candidates to beware of scammers ahead of NEET examinations. In a social media post, NTA said that scammers on Telegram are running channels demanding money ranging from 14 thousand to 25 thousand rupees with claims of sharing the re-exam paper. It clarified that there is no leaked paper for the re-exam. It said that fake “proof” videos showing chats from before the exam are also being circulated. NTA also shared a video of its Director General Abhishek Singh, explaining the reason behind blocking the access to the app in the country. Talking to the media, Mr Singh said that NTA is committed to ensuring that no malpractice will happen and every question paper has been made in a complete secure manner. He said that the agency has added layers of security to ensure that nobody who is involved in setting up the question papers, translating them, printing them, moving them or storing them till the date of the examination is compromised in any manner.

title: Crop residue management reviewed to tackle air pollution in Delhi-NCR
summary: Environment, Forest and Climate Change Minister Bhupender Yadav today held a detailed review along with Agriculture and Farmer’s Welfare Minister Shivraj Singh Chouhan on crop residue management. The meeting also discussed prevention of stubble burning in the ...
body: Environment, Forest and Climate Change Minister Bhupender Yadav today held a detailed review along with Agriculture and Farmer’s Welfare Minister Shivraj Singh Chouhan on crop residue management. The meeting also discussed prevention of stubble burning in the context of Air Quality Management in Delhi-NCR and adjoining areas. In a social media post, the Minister said that the review was focused on ways and means to efficiently and effectively manage and monitor and crop residue in a bid to combat air pollution in the Delhi-NCR region.

title: Six states agree to sign MoU for Kishau Multi-Purpose Dam Project
summary: Home Minister Amit Shah today chaired a crucial meeting in New Delhi, during which consensus was reached among six states on the long-pending Kishau Multi-Purpose Dam Project aimed at the rejuvenation of the Yamuna River. Home Minister said in a statement, Him...
body: Home Minister Amit Shah today chaired a crucial meeting in New Delhi, during which consensus was reached among six states on the long-pending Kishau Multi-Purpose Dam Project aimed at the rejuvenation of the Yamuna River. Home Minister said in a statement, Himachal Pradesh, Uttarakhand, Delhi, Uttar Pradesh, Haryana and Rajasthan have agreed to sign a Memorandum of Understanding for the implementation of the project. Following the signing of the MoU, the Kishau project will be placed before the Union Cabinet for approval. Under the agreed funding pattern, the Central Government will provide 90 per cent assistance for the water component of the project, while the remaining 10 per cent will be shared by the six states. It was also decided that, in lieu of sharing the cost of the power component, the water allocated to Himachal Pradesh will be supplied to Delhi and Rajasthan. The Ministry said that this decision will prove to be a significant step towards a clean and pure Yamuna, as it will increase the flow of clean water in the river.

title: India well-positioned to lead global Pharma and MedTech innovation: Union Minister JP Nadda
summary: Union Minister for Chemicals and Fertilizers Jagat Prakash Nadda today said that India is well-positioned to emerge as a global hub for Pharma and MedTech innovation while ensuring affordable, accessible, and quality healthcare for all. In a social media post,...
body: Union Minister for Chemicals and Fertilizers Jagat Prakash Nadda today said that India is well-positioned to emerge as a global hub for Pharma and MedTech innovation while ensuring affordable, accessible, and quality healthcare for all. In a social media post, Mr Nadda said that he chaired the Consultative Committee Meeting of the Ministry of Chemicals and Fertilizers on Innovation in the Pharma and MedTech Sectors. The Minister said that during the meeting he deliberated on strengthening India’s Research and Development and innovation ecosystem, promoting innovation in pharmaceuticals, biologics, and medical devices, enhancing clinical research, and building a globally competitive healthcare industry.

title: India hosts two-day meeting of IORA Committee of Senior Officials in New Delhi
summary: The two-day meeting of the Committee of Senior Officials of the Indian Ocean Rim Association was hosted by India in New Delhi under India’s current Chairship of IORA. External Affairs Ministry said in a statement that the meeting reviewed progress across IORA’...
body: The two-day meeting of the Committee of Senior Officials of the Indian Ocean Rim Association was hosted by India in New Delhi under India’s current Chairship of IORA. External Affairs Ministry said in a statement that the meeting reviewed progress across IORA’s institutional matters, priority areas and advanced discussions on the next IORA Action Plan (2028-2032).

body: Switzerland’s foreign ministry today said a signing ceremony for a deal between the United States and Iran will take place on Friday at the Burgenstock resort near the city of Luzern. The ministry said it has been in close contact with both countries about the possible signing of the memorandum of understanding that US and Iranian officials announced over the weekend.

title: CSIR organizes Technology Transfer & Bharatiya Nirdeshak Document (BND) Release Function in New Delhi
summary: The Council of Scientific and Industrial Research (CSIR) today organized a Technology Transfer and Bharatiya Nirdeshak Document (BND) Release Function in New Delhi. It brought together scientists, industry partners, technology adopters and strategic stakeholde...
body: The Council of Scientific and Industrial Research (CSIR) today organized a Technology Transfer and Bharatiya Nirdeshak Document (BND) Release Function in New Delhi. It brought together scientists, industry partners, technology adopters and strategic stakeholders. The programme witnessed the transfer of seven technologies to industry, release of ten Bharatiya Nirdeshak Dravyas (BNDs), and handing over of critical components developed for quantum sensing applications to DRDO. These BNDs are expected to support laboratories, industry and regulatory agencies by providing reliable reference materials and measurement standards. Ministry of Science and Technology said that the transfer reflects the growing capabilities of Indian laboratories in developing advanced components required for next-generation strategic technologies. Speaking on the occasion, Director General, CSIR, Dr. N. Kalaiselvi noted that the number of technology transfers has been steadily increasing, reflecting the growing relevance of CSIR research to national priorities and industrial needs. Dr Kalaiselvi emphasized that technology transfer is not merely a transaction but the culmination of scientific research reaching society through industry partnerships. The DG, CSIR also reiterated CSIR’s commitment to accelerating innovation-led growth through stronger industry engagement, commercialization of research outcomes, and development of indigenous technologies aligned with the vision of Atmanirbhar Bharat. Director, CSIR- National Physical Laboratory, Prof. Venu Gopal Achanta highlighted the foundational role played by measurement science and metrology in scientific advancement, industrial development and national quality infrastructure. These BNDs are expected to support laboratories, industry and regulatory agencies by providing reliable reference materials and measurement standards. Ministry of Science and Technology said that the transfer reflects the growing capabilities of Indian laboratories in developing advanced components required for next-generation strategic technologies. Speaking on the occasion, Director General, CSIR, Dr. N. Kalaiselvi noted that the number of technology transfers has been steadily increasing, reflecting the growing relevance of CSIR research to national priorities and industrial needs. Dr Kalaiselvi emphasized that technology transfer is not merely a transaction but the culmination of scientific research reaching society through industry partnerships. The DG, CSIR also reiterated CSIR’s commitment to accelerating innovation-led growth through stronger industry engagement, commercialization of research outcomes, and development of indigenous technologies aligned with the vision of Atmanirbhar Bharat.
