Dr Kalaiselvi emphasized that technology transfer is not merely a transaction but the culmination of scientific research reaching society through industry partnerships. The DG, CSIR also reiterated CSIR’s commitment to accelerating innovation-led growth through stronger industry engagement, commercialization of research outcomes, and development of indigenous technologies aligned with the vision of Atmanirbhar Bharat. Director, CSIR- National Physical Laboratory, Prof. Venu Gopal Achanta highlighted the foundational role played by measurement science and metrology in scientific advancement, industrial development and national quality infrastructure.

title: FAI announces 24-member squad for the 26th Senior Asian Fencing Championships 2026
summary: The Fencing Association of India (FAI) has officially announced the Indian senior team for the 26th Senior Asian Fencing Championships 2026, which will be held in Delhi from June 19 to 24. Marking a historic milestone, India will host the prestigious continent...
body: The Fencing Association of India (FAI) has officially announced the Indian senior team for the 26th Senior Asian Fencing Championships 2026, which will be held in Delhi from June 19 to 24. Marking a historic milestone, India will host the prestigious continental event for the first time. Over 30 countries and more than 400 top athletes will participate in this high-stakes competition. India’s 24-member squad comprises 12 women and 12 men and will be led by C.A. Bhavani Devi, India’s first and only fencing Olympian.

title: US President Donald Trump warns Iran against acquiring nuclear weapons
summary: US President Donald Trump today warned Iran that “all hell will rain down” if the Islamic Republic seeks to acquire a nuclear weapon. Speaking during a high-stakes bilateral meeting with Emir of Qatar, Sheikh Tamim Bin Hamad Al-Thani, on the sidelines of the G...
body: US President Donald Trump today warned Iran that “all hell will rain down” if the Islamic Republic seeks to acquire a nuclear weapon. Speaking during a high-stakes bilateral meeting with Emir of Qatar, Sheikh Tamim Bin Hamad Al-Thani, on the sidelines of the G7 Summit in Evian-les-Bains, France, Mr. Trump said a proposed deal explicitly specifies that Tehran will not possess a nuclear weapon. Mr. Trump also vented at Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu over Tel Aviv’s continued strikes on Lebanon, particularly its recent strikes on Beirut, stating that the Israeli PM should be more responsible.

title: Agriculture Minister Shivraj Singh Chouhan reviews preparations for Kharif 2026 season
summary: Agriculture and Farmers’ Welfare Minister Shivraj Singh Chouhan today held a high-level agriculture review meeting in New Delhi and conducted a detailed review of preparations across the country for the Kharif 2026 season. Agriculture Ministry said in a statem...
body: Agriculture and Farmers’ Welfare Minister Shivraj Singh Chouhan today held a high-level agriculture review meeting in New Delhi and conducted a detailed review of preparations across the country for the Kharif 2026 season. Agriculture Ministry said in a statement that in the context of possible El Niño conditions, he placed special emphasis on increasing cotton production, achieving self-reliance in pulses and advance contingency plans for low-rainfall districts. He gave a clear message that protecting farmers’ interests is the government’s top priority under the guidance of Prime Minister Narendra Modi. The meeting focused on ensuring that all necessary steps are taken well in advance to safeguard agriculture and farmers from any adverse weather conditions that may arise during the season. Mr Chouhan gave clear directions that full preparations should be made in advance in those districts where there is a possibility of low rainfall or uneven rainfall. He said that such districts should be clearly identified and crop-wise contingency plans should be prepared in coordination with state governments so that farmers can be provided immediate alternatives, advice and support, that in any weather challenge situation. He strongly emphasised that special attention should be given to water conservation, moisture management, inter-cropping and alternative crop patterns.

title: PM Modi participates in high-level G7 working session in Evian
summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi is participating in a high-level working session at the ongoing G7 Summit in Evian, France, today. The session, themed “Forging New Partnerships and Rebuilding International Solidarity,” brought together leaders of the G7 nations, ...
body: Prime Minister Narendra Modi is participating in a high-level working session at the ongoing G7 Summit in Evian, France, today. The session, themed “Forging New Partnerships and Rebuilding International Solidarity,” brought together leaders of the G7 nations, partner countries, and senior representatives from the World Bank and the African Development Bank. The meeting began shortly after French President Emmanuel Macron formally welcomed PM Modi and other participating leaders. Following a group photograph, PM Modi shared the picture on social media, stating that India and the G7 countries would continue to work closely to promote global prosperity, sustainability and human well-being. Earlier in the day, PM Modi arrived in Evian on a two-day visit to attend the Summit, which commenced yesterday. In a social media post after his arrival, the Prime Minister said he looked forward to engaging with world leaders and exchanging views on key global issues. He added that India remains committed to advancing collective efforts towards a more sustainable and prosperous planet. The Summit assumes considerable strategic importance for India, with PM Modi expected to strongly articulate the concerns and priorities of the Global South. India’s regular participation in the G7 outreach process reflects its expanding role in addressing global challenges and contributing to international efforts relating to peace, security, sustainable development and economic growth. On the sidelines of the Summit, the Prime Minister is scheduled to hold bilateral meetings with Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney, United Kingdom Prime Minister Keir Starmer, and President of the United Arab Emirates Sheikh Mohamed bin Zayed Al Nahyan. Later this evening, he will attend a gala dinner to be hosted by President Macron. A key engagement during the Summit will be PM Modi’s meeting tomorrow with US President Donald Trump. The two leaders are expected to discuss ways to further strengthen bilateral cooperation across a range of sectors, besides exchanging views on important regional and global developments of mutual interest.

title: One killed, four injured in 6.3-magnitude earthquake in northwest China
summary: One person was killed and four others injured today after a 6.3-magnitude earthquake jolted Haixi Mongolian and Tibetan Autonomous Prefecture in northwest China. The quake struck at 5:06 PM (local time) at a depth of 10 km, said a report issued by the China Ea...
body: One person was killed and four others injured today after a 6.3-magnitude earthquake jolted Haixi Mongolian and Tibetan Autonomous Prefecture in northwest China. The quake struck at 5:06 PM (local time) at a depth of 10 km, said a report issued by the China Earthquake Networks Centre. The Qinghai provincial seismological bureau dispatched an on-site work team to the quake-hit area to assist local authorities with emergency response. A Level II provincial emergency response for earthquake disasters was issued.

title: IMD forecasts thunderstorms accompanied by lightning, gusty winds over North India for tomorrow
summary: The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued an orange colour warning for Heavy to Very Heavy Rainfall over Sub Himalayan West Bengal and Sikkim for tomorrow. Heavy rainfall is also expected over parts of North-East India, Tamil Nadu, Puducherry and Ka...
body: The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued an orange colour warning for Heavy to Very Heavy Rainfall over Sub Himalayan West Bengal and Sikkim for tomorrow. Heavy rainfall is also expected over parts of North-East India, Tamil Nadu, Puducherry and Karaikal during the next two days. IMD said that conditions are favourable for further advance of southwest monsoon into some more parts of Telangana, Odisha, Jharkhand and Bihar and some parts of Chhattisgarh during the next four to five days. The Met Department has forecast thunderstorms accompanied by lightning and gusty winds over Delhi, Haryana, Chandigarh, Punjab, Himachal Pradesh and Uttar Pradesh for tomorrow. Similar weather conditions are expected over Bihar, Jharkhand, Chhattisgarh, Madhya Pradesh, Coastal Andhra Pradesh, Odisha, and Andaman and Nicobar Islands. While Heat Wave conditions are expected over Telangana and Vidarbha, hot and humid weather conditions are expected over Coastal Andhra Pradesh, Gangetic West Bengal, Konkan, Goa and Odisha tomorrow. IMD said that a gradual rise in maximum temperatures over Maharashtra by about two degrees Celsius is expected during the next three days. For Delhi, IMD has predicted a partly cloudy sky tomorrow. According to the Weather Agency, the maximum temperature will be around 33 to 35 degrees Celsius in the national capital while the minimum temperature will hover around 26 to 28 degrees Celsius tomorrow.

title: USA: Life-size statue of Swami Vivekananda unveiled at Chicago Consulate
summary: A life-size statue of Swami Vivekananda was unveiled at the Consulate General of India in Chicago by India’s Ambassador to the US Vinay Mohan Kwatra. In a social media post, Mr. Kwatra said, he is proud to unveil a statue of Swami Vivekananda whose timeless me...
body: A life-size statue of Swami Vivekananda was unveiled at the Consulate General of India in Chicago by India’s Ambassador to the US Vinay Mohan Kwatra. In a social media post, Mr. Kwatra said, he is proud to unveil a statue of Swami Vivekananda whose timeless message of service and universal harmony lives on in all of us. Vivekananda, a prominent philosopher-thinker who spread the teachings of Vedanta and Yoga across the world, is best known in the US for his landmark speech at the World’s Parliament of Religions held in Chicago in 1893. In April this year, the first-ever life-size bronze statue of Swami Vivekananda was unveiled in Seattle, in Washington state. The monument was the first installation of Swami Vivekananda to be hosted by a city government anywhere in the US.

title: First Indian LNG tanker Disha safely crosses the Strait of Hormuz
summary: A Liquefied Natural Gas (LNG) carrier, Disha, safely transited through the Strait of Hormuz. It is the first Indian LNG tanker to cross the war-hit narrow shipping lane since the war in the Middle East broke out. The LNG carrier carrying 62 thousand 370 metric...
body: A Liquefied Natural Gas (LNG) carrier, Disha, safely transited through the Strait of Hormuz. It is the first Indian LNG tanker to cross the war-hit narrow shipping lane since the war in the Middle East broke out. The LNG carrier carrying 62 thousand 370 metric tons of LNG and managed by a Shipping Corporation of India-led consortium and chartered by Petronet LNG Ltd, is also one of the first commercial vessels to transit the Strait since the announcement of a preliminary ceasefire deal between the United States and Iran. Director in the Ministry of Ports, Shipping and Waterways, Upesh Kumar Sharma, said the vessel is expected to arrive at Dahej in Gujarat on 18th of this month. Mr. Sharma said 15 vessels, comprising 10 Indian-flagged ships and 5 foreign-flagged vessels , have safely crossed the strategic waterway so far. Mr. Sharma further informed, the Ministry of Ports, Shipping and Waterways, through the Directorate General of Shipping, remains in continuous coordination with the Ministry of External Affairs, Indian missions abroad, shipping companies and other stakeholders to ensure the safety of Indian seafarers and vessels operating in the region. The strategic waterway was effectively closed after the United States and Israel launched military strikes on Iran on 28th of February, triggering retaliatory action from Tehran and disrupting regional shipping.

title: Afghanistan: UNSC adopts resolution calling Taliban rulers to reverse crackdown on women
summary: The UN Security Council unanimously adopted a resolution calling on Afghanistan’s Taliban rulers to swiftly reverse their crackdown on women and to combat various groups inside Afghanistan. The resolution extends the UN political mission in Afghanistan until J...
body: The UN Security Council unanimously adopted a resolution calling on Afghanistan’s Taliban rulers to swiftly reverse their crackdown on women and to combat various groups inside Afghanistan. The resolution extends the UN political mission in Afghanistan until June 17 next year, and authorises the mission to support humanitarian aid deliveries and to promote national and local governance without any discrimination. The resolution’s adoption follows the arrest of 30 women in the western city of Herat this month for allegedly violating the Taliban’s strict dress code. The resolution authorises the UN mission to facilitate talks between the Taliban and regional countries and the wider international community.

title: Bharat Innovates will be a transformational platform for future: Union Minister Piyush Goyal
summary: The shared vision of Prime Minister Narendra Modi and French President Emmanuel Macron under the India-France Year of Innovation 2026 provided the foundation of Bharat Innovates that has become a transformational platform for the future. Union Commerce and Ind...
body: The shared vision of Prime Minister Narendra Modi and French President Emmanuel Macron under the India-France Year of Innovation 2026 provided the foundation of Bharat Innovates that has become a transformational platform for the future. Union Commerce and Industry Minister Piyush Goyal made these remarks in Nice, France, while addressing the closing ceremony of Bharat Innovates. The Minister expressed hope that the event will emerge as a trendsetter in the future. Mr Goyal said that the platform has provided innovators and founders with an opportunity to represent the voice of thousands of other innovators and start-ups by providing feedback regarding the sector. Talking about the solar energy sector in India, he highlighted that India’s solar mission increased from 20 gigawatt to 100 gigawatt before the targeted date.

title: BJP hails PM Modi receiving Slovakia’s highest State honour
summary: BJP has said that it is a matter of great pride for the nation that Prime Minister Narendra Modi has been conferred with Slovakia’s highest State honour, “The Order of the White Double Cross, First Class. Talking to media in New Delhi , BJP Spokesperson Sambi ...
body: BJP has said that it is a matter of great pride for the nation that Prime Minister Narendra Modi has been conferred with Slovakia’s highest State honour, “The Order of the White Double Cross, First Class. Talking to media in New Delhi , BJP Spokesperson Sambi Patra said that the Prime Minister has hismself said that this honour is not only for him but for all the people of the country. Mr Patra said, with this, Mr Modi has received 33 such highest civilian awards from various countries, which has raised India’s global stature. He said, it is a recognition of the welfare works done by the Prime Minister in the country.

title: President Trump and PM Modi’s meeting tomorrow on sidelines of G7 Summit in France
summary: US President Donald Trump has reached France for the Group of Seven, G-7 Summit after announcing an agreement with Iran. The agreement with Iran promises to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and pause a conflict that has rattled energy markets and unsettled allies. ...
body: US President Donald Trump has reached France for the Group of Seven, G-7 Summit after announcing an agreement with Iran. The agreement with Iran promises to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and pause a conflict that has rattled energy markets and unsettled allies. The agreement, announced yesterday, is expected to dominate discussions among G7 leaders, many of whom are seeking answers about a deal whose details remain largely unknown. Trump is expected to spend three days meeting world leaders in Evian-les-Bains, France. He is scheduled to meet Prime Minister Narendra Modi on Wednesday.

title: Government hikes export duties on diesel and Aviation Turbine Fuel
summary: The Government has increased the Special Additional Excise Duty (SAED) on the export of diesel and Aviation Turbine Fuel (ATF) in its latest fortnightly review. With effect from today, the export duty on diesel has been raised to 14 rupees per litre from 13 ru...
body: The Government has increased the Special Additional Excise Duty (SAED) on the export of diesel and Aviation Turbine Fuel (ATF) in its latest fortnightly review. With effect from today, the export duty on diesel has been raised to 14 rupees per litre from 13 rupees 5 paisa per litre, while the levy on ATF exports has been increased to 12 rupees 5 paisa per litre from 9 rupees 5 paisa per litre. The export duty on petrol remains unchanged at 1 rupee 5 paisa per litre. There is no change in the existing duty rates on petrol and diesel cleared for domestic consumption. The revised duties come amid continuing efforts by the Centre to ensure adequate domestic availability of petroleum products in view of the ongoing West Asia crisis. The Government had introduced export levies on petroleum products from March 27 to discourage overseas shipments and prioritize supplies for the domestic market.

title: Iran says the deal to end war with U.S. requires Israel to withdraw from Lebanon
summary: Iran today said that the deal to end the war with the United States would also require Israel to withdraw from Lebanon. Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi said Israel’s continued occupation of southern Lebanon would violate the Memorandum of Understanding...
body: Iran today said that the deal to end the war with the United States would also require Israel to withdraw from Lebanon. Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi said Israel’s continued occupation of southern Lebanon would violate the Memorandum of Understanding reached between the United States and Iran. The United States has not clarified whether Lebanon is part of the final agreement. Meanwhile, Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu yesterday described the agreement as US President Donald Trump’s decision, noting that Israel had its own priorities and would remain in a buffer zone in Lebanon as long as necessary.

title: 8 killed after B-52 bomber crashes at US Air Force base in Southern California
summary: A B-52 bomber crashed shortly after takeoff at a US Air Force base in Southern California’s Mojave Desert, killing all eight people aboard last night. The US military on a social platform stated that emergency crews were responding after the aircraft went down...
body: A B-52 bomber crashed shortly after takeoff at a US Air Force base in Southern California’s Mojave Desert, killing all eight people aboard last night. The US military on a social platform stated that emergency crews were responding after the aircraft went down around 11:20 am local time at Edwards Air Force Base. Aerial footage showed a plume of black smoke rising from the desert near the runway on the base. The airfield was closed, and all inbound aircraft were being diverted. Meanwhile, all non-commercial visitor passes for the base were suspended to allow the installation to focus entirely on emergency response operations. It is not immediately clear what caused the crash. The Boeing B-52 Stratofortress is a long-range bomber that entered service in 1955. It is designed to carry both conventional and nuclear weapons. Edwards Air Force Base is home to a large portion of the US Air Force’s aircraft test and development efforts.

title: NEET UG-Re-exams: Union Home Secretary reviews security & logistical readiness
summary: Union Home Secretary Govind Mohan has chaired a high-level review meeting on security and logistical readiness ahead of Neet UG re-examination. He reviewed the preparedness of the State Governments for the NEET(UG) Re-examination, 2026 with a special focus on ...
body: Union Home Secretary Govind Mohan has chaired a high-level review meeting on security and logistical readiness ahead of Neet UG re-examination. He reviewed the preparedness of the State Governments for the NEET(UG) Re-examination, 2026 with a special focus on student convenience, security arrangements, and the integrity of the examination process. The Union Home Secretary directed all State Governments, Union Territories and Central agencies to ensure the adherence of the security protocols. States and Union Territories were requested to facilitate smooth travel of students. Chairman, Railway Board assured full cooperation. During the meeting Secretary, Department of School Education and Literacy, Secretary, Department of Higher Education, Secretary, Department of Posts, DGs of CRPF, CISF, BCAS, and DG, NTA and senior officers from Ministry of Defence and Ministry of Health and Family Welfare were also present.

title: Delhi Govt organises GST domain training programme for officials
summary: The Delhi Government is organising a two-week Goods and Services Tax (GST) domain training programme for officers and employees associated with GST administration. The government said that this initiative is aimed at making tax administration more effective, t...
body: The Delhi Government is organising a two-week Goods and Services Tax (GST) domain training programme for officers and employees associated with GST administration. The government said that this initiative is aimed at making tax administration more effective, transparent, accountable and citizen-centric. Delhi Chief Minister Rekha Gupta said that tax administration today is no longer limited to revenue collection. She added that it has evolved into an integrated system involving technology, law, investigation, analysis and public service. The special training programme is being conducted from yesterday till 26th of this month.

title: Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal launches UPI at Nice Massena in France
summary: Commerce and Industry Minister Piyush Goyal has launched Unified Payments Interface (UPI) at Nice Massena in France. In a social media post, Mr Goyal said that by bringing India’s world-class digital payments platform to one of France’s premier retail destinat...
body: Commerce and Industry Minister Piyush Goyal has launched Unified Payments Interface (UPI) at Nice Massena in France. In a social media post, Mr Goyal said that by bringing India’s world-class digital payments platform to one of France’s premier retail destinations marks another significant step in UPI’s global expansion. He added that the initiative showcases India’s ability to deliver trusted, seamless, and interoperable digital solutions at scale. The Minister further noted that the launch is strengthening the economic and technological ties between India and France, reflecting the growing depth and ambition of the strategic partnership between the two countries.

title: Nine killed, 25 injured in train–bus collision in Zimbabwe
summary: Nine people were killed and 25 others injured after a train collided with a bus in Triangle, a sugar-producing town in southern Zimbabwe today. Police said the crash occurred at a railway crossing when a freight train collided with the bus. National Railways o...
body: Nine people were killed and 25 others injured after a train collided with a bus in Triangle, a sugar-producing town in southern Zimbabwe today. Police said the crash occurred at a railway crossing when a freight train collided with the bus. National Railways of Zimbabwe spokesperson Andrew Kanambura said the bus driver failed to stop and check for oncoming trains before crossing the tracks, violating railway safety regulations.

title: Op Sindoor conveys message India does not seek war, but terrorism will not be tolerated
summary: India has undergone a remarkable transformation, driven by the principles of Sabka Saath, Sabka Vikas, Sabka Vishwas and Sabka Prayas under the visionary leadership of Prime Minister Narendra Modi in the last 12 years. Last year, on the intervening night of 6t...
body: India has undergone a remarkable transformation, driven by the principles of Sabka Saath, Sabka Vikas, Sabka Vishwas and Sabka Prayas under the visionary leadership of Prime Minister Narendra Modi in the last 12 years. Last year, on the intervening night of 6th and 7th of May, Operation Sindoor sent a clear message to the world that India does not seek war, but terrorism will not be tolerated. Operation Sindoor drew a new red line, that if terrorism is a state policy, it will be met with a direct and forceful response. Targeting strongholds of Lashkar e Taiba, Jaish e Mohammed and Hizbul Mujahideen, India successfully destroyed nine major terrorist launch pads located in Pakistan and Pakistan-occupied Jammu and Kashmir. More than hundred terrorists were killed in this operation. On May 9th and 10th, India became the first nation to strike 11 airbases of a nuclear-armed state in a single operation. This resulted in the destruction of 20 percent of Pakistan Air Force’s infrastructure. There was widespread global support with numerous global leaders extending their support to India. This reflects India’s enhanced global standing and its command over the narrative. Meanwhile, to secure its land borders spanning over 15000 kilometres across seven neighbouring nations, India today conducts its effective foreign policy guided by the principle of Nation First, while simultaneously advancing towards self reliance in the defence sector. Government has accelerated the construction of border fencing along the India-Bangladesh border to enhance national security and fortify national surveillance. NDA government remains resolute and deeply committed to a policy of Zero Tolerance Against Terrorism.

title: Civil Services preliminary examination result declared
summary: The Union Public Commission Service (UPSC) has declared the result of civil services preliminary examination (CSE) for this year. UPSC informed that this year against more than one thousand vacancies, a total of more than 13 thousand candidates have been short...
body: The Union Public Commission Service (UPSC) has declared the result of civil services preliminary examination (CSE) for this year. UPSC informed that this year against more than one thousand vacancies, a total of more than 13 thousand candidates have been shortlisted for the civil services (Main) examination. UPSC also informed candidates that marks, cut off marks and answer key will be uploaded on the commission’s website only after the declaration of final result. The candidates can download the Prelims result on the official website of UPSC, upsc.gov.in. The commission said that last year, a total of over 14 thousand candidates were shortlisted for the civil services (Main) examination against one thousand eighty seven vacancies.

title: DDWS holds 9th District Collectors’ Peyjal Samvad
summary: The Department of Drinking Water and Sanitation (DDWS) today organised the 9th edition of the District Collectors’ Peyjal Samvad through video conferencing. It brought together senior officials, District Collectors, Deputy Commissioners, and Mission Directors ...
body: The Department of Drinking Water and Sanitation (DDWS) today organised the 9th edition of the District Collectors’ Peyjal Samvad through video conferencing. It brought together senior officials, District Collectors, Deputy Commissioners, and Mission Directors of State and Union Territories Jal Jeevan Mission (JJM), to deliberate on accelerating the implementation of JJM 2.0 and sharing of best practices. Secretary, DDWS, Ashok K.K. Meena chaired the Samvad. Speaking on the occasion, Mr Meena said that the focus of JJM 2.0 has now moved beyond infrastructure creation to ensuring reliable service delivery, sustainability, and community ownership of rural water supply systems. The Secretary underlined the significance of the annual Jal Seva Aankalan process in assessing the status of drinking water service delivery. He also urged districts to familiarise themselves with the assessment process and utilise its findings for improving service delivery. Emphasising community ownership, he called for the systematic commissioning and handing over of completed water supply schemes to Gram Panchayats through Jal Arpan ceremonies. He underlined that sharing the best practices by districts across the country would inspire improved performance and accelerate progress towards ensuring sustainable drinking water services for every rural household under the Jal Jeevan Mission.

title: Slovakia confers its highest State honour upon PM Modi
summary: President of Slovakia, Peter Pellegrini has conferred Slovakia’s highest State honour, “The Order of the White Double Cross, First Class” upon Prime Minister Narendra Modi. ​The Prime Minister expressed his heartfelt gratitude to the President, the Government,...
body: President of Slovakia, Peter Pellegrini has conferred Slovakia’s highest State honour, “The Order of the White Double Cross, First Class” upon Prime Minister Narendra Modi. ​The Prime Minister expressed his heartfelt gratitude to the President, the Government, and the people of Slovakia for the distinguished honour. Accepting the award, he noted that the honour was a tribute to the 1.4 billion people of India, and to the enduring bonds of friendship between India and Slovakia. ​Terming the award as a testament to the warmth, trust, and affection that unite the people of India and Slovakia, the Prime Minister hoped that the honour would inspire future generations in both countries to continue nurturing their special friendship.

title: NIA attaches Amritsar property in LeT-linked narco-terror case
summary: The National Investigation Agency (NIA) has attached a house in Amritsar, Punjab, allegedly linked to a Lashkar-e-Taiba (LeT) operative in a transnational narco-terror network case with footprints spread across multiple countries. The NIA said that the propert...
body: The National Investigation Agency (NIA) has attached a house in Amritsar, Punjab, allegedly linked to a Lashkar-e-Taiba (LeT) operative in a transnational narco-terror network case with footprints spread across multiple countries. The NIA said that the property was registered in the name of the father of accused Ankush Kapoor, a key operative in India of the narco-terror network spread across Italy, Australia, Iran, Thailand, UAE and Pakistan, among other nations. The Agency informed that investigations had revealed the alleged links of Ankush with a Dubai-based accused connected with the proscribed terrorist organisation LeT. It added that Ankush, who was arrested last year, was found to be a very important operative of the conspiracy relating to alleged smuggling, transportation, storage and distribution of narcotic substances, besides laundering the proceeds of terrorism to the accused in various countries. Investigations had further shown that the proceeds from the trafficking of narcotic substances were being used to finance and support terrorist activities in India through a complex and elaborate financial network. The anti-terror agency had earlier charged Ankush for his role in terror-funding activities, criminal conspiracy, and providing support to unlawful and terrorist networks, in addition to offences under the NDPS Act and IPC. NIA also said that a total of 26 accused have so far been chargesheeted in the case, in which the investigation is continuing.

title: West Asia crisis: US, Iran confirm signing of MoU aiming to end four months of conflict
summary: Washington, D.C.: This video screenshot, taken on April 26, shows U.S. President Trump speaking at a White House press conference in Washington on April 25. Speaking at a White House press conference on the evening of April 25, U.S. President Trump stated that...
body: Washington, D.C.: This video screenshot, taken on April 26, shows U.S. President Trump speaking at a White House press conference in Washington on April 25. Speaking at a White House press conference on the evening of April 25, U.S. President Trump stated that earlier that nightat the venue of the White House Correspondents' Association Dinnera gunman from California, carrying multiple weapons, attempted to force his way through security but was subsequently subdued. He characterized the suspect as a "lone wolf" but noted that further investigation was still required. (Xinhua via IANS) A major diplomatic breakthrough has raised hopes of a broader de-escalation in West Asia after the United States and Iran announced a framework agreement aimed at ending nearly four months of conflict. Officials from both countries confirmed that a memorandum of understanding has already been signed, with a formal signing ceremony scheduled to take place in Geneva, Switzerland, on Friday. The agreement is expected to pave the way for reopening the Strait of Hormuz, restoring maritime traffic and launching a 60-day period of negotiations on Iran’s nuclear programme and other unresolved issues. Oil prices eased following the announcement as markets responded positively to the prospect of reduced tensions and the resumption of energy shipments through one of the world’s most critical maritime corridors. US President Donald Trump said details of the agreement would be released after the formal signing ceremony, while Vice President JD Vance confirmed that the framework had been digitally signed and described it as a significant step towards ending the conflict. However, key provisions remain unclear as neither side has released the full text. Differences have already emerged over the future management of the Strait of Hormuz, with Washington insisting the waterway should remain open without tolls, while Iranian officials have suggested that future fees could still be considered. Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian welcomed the understanding as an important step towards ending the war but stressed that a final agreement has not yet been completed. The announcement of a US-Iran peace framework has triggered cautious optimism across West Asia, with regional governments welcoming efforts to end months of conflict. Oil markets reacted positively, with prices falling on expectations of improved energy supplies. Despite the breakthrough, major challenges remain. Regional tensions also remain high, particularly in Lebanon, where Israel has indicated it will maintain military positions in certain areas despite the broader diplomatic initiative. Iran has repeatedly stated that peace in Lebanon is an inseparable element of any lasting settlement, making the situation on the Israel-Lebanon front a potential test of the agreement’s durability. The coming days will determine whether the framework can evolve into a durable peace agreement for the region. However, key provisions remain unclear as neither side has released the full text. Differences have already emerged over the future management of the Strait of Hormuz, with Washington insisting the waterway should remain open without tolls, while Iranian officials have suggested that future fees could still be considered. Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian welcomed the understanding as an important step towards ending the war but stressed that a final agreement has not yet been completed. The announcement of a US-Iran peace framework has triggered cautious optimism across West Asia, with regional governments welcoming efforts to end months of conflict. Oil markets reacted positively, with prices falling on expectations of improved energy supplies. Despite the breakthrough, major challenges remain. Regional tensions also remain high, particularly in Lebanon, where Israel has indicated it will maintain military positions in certain areas despite the broader diplomatic initiative. Iran has repeatedly stated that peace in Lebanon is an inseparable element of any lasting settlement, making the situation on the Israel-Lebanon front a potential test of the agreement’s durability. The coming days will determine whether the framework can evolve into a durable peace agreement for the region.

title: ED conducts searches at 17 locations in YES Bank loan fraud case
summary: The Enforcement Directorate (ED) is conducting search operations at 17 locations across Mumbai, Khandala, and New Delhi in connection with the alleged fraudulent assignment of YES Bank loans during the financial years 2016-17 and 2017-18. The searches cover pr...
body: The Enforcement Directorate (ED) is conducting search operations at 17 locations across Mumbai, Khandala, and New Delhi in connection with the alleged fraudulent assignment of YES Bank loans during the financial years 2016-17 and 2017-18. The searches cover premises linked to Suraksha Asset Reconstruction Company Limited (SARCL), Suraksha Realty Limited, Khyati Realtors Private Limited, their promoters, directors and employees, as well as a former YES Bank employee. ED said that the search operations are being carried out to gather evidence in a money laundering case involving the alleged collusion between certain Asset Reconstruction Companies and YES Bank. The probe relates to circular transactions for the takeover of stressed assets, undervaluation of auctioned assets, inflated or fraudulent claims, and issues concerning creditor voting rights and transparency in the Corporate Insolvency Resolution Process.

title: 52nd G7 Summit begins in France with theme “Working Together to Address Major International Challenges”
summary: The 52nd G7 Summit began in Evian, France yesterday, with leaders of the world’s leading economies holding discussions on major international challenges. French President Emmanuel Macron hosted working dinner on the theme, “Working Together to Address Major In...
body: The 52nd G7 Summit began in Evian, France yesterday, with leaders of the world’s leading economies holding discussions on major international challenges. French President Emmanuel Macron hosted working dinner on the theme, “Working Together to Address Major International Challenges.” Earlier, Mr Macron formally welcomed the leaders of the G7 countries, including US President Donald Trump, and UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer. European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen and European Council President António Costa at the summit. During the three day summit, the leaders are expected to deliberate on reducing global imbalances, addressing major geopolitical crises, issues related to children, and strengthening the fight against organized crime and illicit flows. The G7 Summit is an international forum held annually for the leaders of the G7 member States of Canada, France, Germany, Italy, Japan, the UK, and the US.

title: Supreme Court directs Tripura SEC to hold village committee elections on September 27
summary: The Supreme Court today directed the Tripura State Election Commission to conduct elections to the village committees under the Tripura Tribal Areas Autonomous District Council in a single phase on September 27. The direction by a bench of justices Joymalya Ba...
body: The Supreme Court today directed the Tripura State Election Commission to conduct elections to the village committees under the Tripura Tribal Areas Autonomous District Council in a single phase on September 27. The direction by a bench of justices Joymalya Bagchi and Vipul M Pancholi came after Attorney General R Venkataramani, appearing for Tripura SEC, suggested conducting the entire election in a single phase. Solicitor General Tushar Mehta, appearing for the state government, agreed with Attorney General’s submission.

title: Sensex and Nifty extend rally for third consecutive session
summary: Extending their rally for a third consecutive session, domestic benchmark equity indices ended today’s trade with gains of more than half a per cent. The Sensex jumped 544 points or 0.7 per cent to close at 76,808, while the Nifty rose 135 points or 0.6 per ce...
body: Extending their rally for a third consecutive session, domestic benchmark equity indices ended today’s trade with gains of more than half a per cent. The Sensex jumped 544 points or 0.7 per cent to close at 76,808, while the Nifty rose 135 points or 0.6 per cent to settle at 23,989. In the broader market on the National Stock Exchange, the Midcap 100 as well as the Smallcap 100 indices gained 0.4 per cent. The further decline in global crude oil prices and a strengthening rupee boosted investor sentiment.

title: Sports Minister Mansukh Mandaviya to lead Fit India Cyclothon in Mumbai on June 17
summary: Union Minister for Youth Affairs and Sports Dr Mansukh Mandaviya will lead a special Fit India Cyclothon at Mumbai’s Worli Cycle Track tomorrow. The Ministry of Youth Affairs and Sports said that the FIT India Cyclothon is part of the broader Fit India Movemen...
body: Union Minister for Youth Affairs and Sports Dr Mansukh Mandaviya will lead a special Fit India Cyclothon at Mumbai’s Worli Cycle Track tomorrow. The Ministry of Youth Affairs and Sports said that the FIT India Cyclothon is part of the broader Fit India Movement, which continues to promote fitness as a way of life through community participation, cycling, yoga and other physical activities. Around 250 cyclists from different walks of life will pedal alongside the Minister against the backdrop of Mumbai’s scenic coastline. It will also serve as a curtain-raiser to the global celebrations of the 12th International Day of Yoga on the 21st of June.

title: India, Japan adopt Rules of Implementation for Joint Crediting Mechanism under Paris Agreement
summary: India and Japan have adopted the Rules of Implementation for the Joint Crediting Mechanism (JCM) under Article 6.2 of the Paris Agreement, for enhancing cooperation on climate action, greenhouse gas emission reduction and sustainable development initiatives. A...
body: India and Japan have adopted the Rules of Implementation for the Joint Crediting Mechanism (JCM) under Article 6.2 of the Paris Agreement, for enhancing cooperation on climate action, greenhouse gas emission reduction and sustainable development initiatives. According to the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change, the Rules of Implementation were adopted on 8th June this year following the Memorandum of Cooperation signed between the two countries last year for the Joint Crediting Mechanism. The mechanism provides a framework for collaboration on mitigation projects that reduce or remove greenhouse gas emissions while supporting sustainable development in India. It will also contribute to the achievement of the Nationally Determined Contributions (NDCs) of both India and Japan under the Paris Agreement. The Rules of Implementation lay down governance arrangements for the mechanism, including the establishment of a Joint Committee comprising representatives of both governments, transparent project approval procedures, third-party validation and verification processes, sustainable development safeguards and national registries for tracking the issuance and transfer of carbon credits. The Ministry said the Joint Crediting Mechanism reflects India’s commitment to climate action and is expected to promote investment, technology transfer and capacity-building for projects involving low-carbon technologies in the country. The initiative is aimed at supporting climate change mitigation efforts while advancing sustainable development goals.

title: Govt Debunks Fake Claim of UPSC CSE 2026 Preliminary Question Paper Leak
summary: The government has debunked several social media posts circulating online, claiming that UPSC Civil Services (Preliminary) Examination, 2026 question paper was leaked. The Fact Check Unit of the Press Information Bureau (PIB) said that the claim is fake. The U...
body: The government has debunked several social media posts circulating online, claiming that UPSC Civil Services (Preliminary) Examination, 2026 question paper was leaked. The Fact Check Unit of the Press Information Bureau (PIB) said that the claim is fake. The Unit added that UPSC examination question papers are developed by experts in the relevant subjects selected from across the country. PIB has also urged people to verify such information from official sources only and asked them to refrain from sharing such misleading and unverified claims.

title: Hockey Nations Cup: Women team storms into semifinal, defeating Japan 2-1
summary: In Women’s Hockey, India has stormed into the semi-finals of the FIH Hockey Nations Cup in Auckland, New Zealand. India defeated Japan 2-1 in their second Pool A match. Captain and Player of the Match Salima Tete and Lalremsiami netted the goals for India, whi...
body: In Women’s Hockey, India has stormed into the semi-finals of the FIH Hockey Nations Cup in Auckland, New Zealand. India defeated Japan 2-1 in their second Pool A match. Captain and Player of the Match Salima Tete and Lalremsiami netted the goals for India, while Hiramitsu Ai was the lone goalscorer for Japan. It was a memorable game for midfielder Jyoti, who marked her 100th senior international appearance. The win took unbeaten India to the top of Pool A with six points from wins against the USA and Japan. India will next face Uruguay in their last Pool A match of the tournament on Thursday, while Japan and the USA will face off for the second semi-final spot from Pool A. Meanwhile, in Men’s Hockey, India will face Germany in FIH Hockey Pro League in Rotterdam, Netherlands. Earlier, in the first match of the tour India suffered a narrow 2-3 loss against the Netherlands in Rotterdam on Sunday.

title: Indonesia: Earthquake measuring 6.7 hits Sulawesi island, causing scattered damage
summary: A 6.7 magnitude earthquake shook part of central Indonesia’s Sulawesi island today, causing scattered damage. The initial quake was centred 43 kilometres east-southeast of Palu, and the US Geological Survey said it was about 10 kilometres deep. Several aftersh...
body: A 6.7 magnitude earthquake shook part of central Indonesia’s Sulawesi island today, causing scattered damage. The initial quake was centred 43 kilometres east-southeast of Palu, and the US Geological Survey said it was about 10 kilometres deep. Several aftershocks followed, the strongest being of 5.2 magnitude. Authorities reported scattered damage to buildings. Indonesia’s Meteorology, Climatology, and Geophysical Agency said there was no danger of a tsunami but warned aftershocks could continue. Indonesia is crossed by several seismic faults, and earthquakes and volcanic activity are common

title: Banaras Connect in Bratislava: PM Modi appreciates creative interpretation of Varanasi by Slovak artists
summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi has said, Art and culture have the power to bring people closer together. This comes after PM Modi and Slovak President Peter Pellegrini witnessed an exhibition centred on Varanasi, including works by Slovak artists, at the Preside...
body: Prime Minister Narendra Modi has said, Art and culture have the power to bring people closer together. This comes after PM Modi and Slovak President Peter Pellegrini witnessed an exhibition centred on Varanasi, including works by Slovak artists, at the Presidential Palace in Bratislava, Slovakia, yesterday. Calling it a Banaras connect in Bratislava, PM Modi appreciated the creative interpretation of Varanasi by Slovak artists. The Ministry of External Affairs said that the exhibition was dedicated to the theme Varanasi City and featured artworks that beautifully captured the spiritual, cultural, and everyday life of one of India’s oldest living cities. The exhibition featured paintings and artworks by Slovak artists who had recently travelled to Varanasi and were inspired by its unique ambience and traditions.

title: IMD forecasts heavy rainfall in parts of NE and West Bengal
summary: The India Meteorological Department has forecast heavy to very heavy rainfall at isolated places over Arunachal Pradesh, Assam, Meghalaya, Sub Himalayan West Bengal and Sikkim till Sunday. IMD also predicted heavy rainfall over Tamil Nadu, Puducherry and Karai...
body: The India Meteorological Department has forecast heavy to very heavy rainfall at isolated places over Arunachal Pradesh, Assam, Meghalaya, Sub Himalayan West Bengal and Sikkim till Sunday. IMD also predicted heavy rainfall over Tamil Nadu, Puducherry and Karaikal till Friday, while Bihar, Kerala and Mahe will experience heavy rainfall for the day. The weather agency added that thunderstorms accompanied with lightning and gusty winds of speeds upto 50 kilometre per hour is expected over Delhi, Chandigarh, Haryana, Punjab Himachal Pradesh, Interior Karnataka, Jammu-Kashmir, Ladakh, Jharkhand, Madhya Pradesh and Maharashtra today. Meanwhile, IMD has issued an orange colour warning for dust storms at isolated places over West Rajasthan today. It also predicted heat wave conditions over parts of Vidarbha and Telangana till tomorrow. It added that hot and humid weather conditions are very likely at isolated places over Konkan, Goa, Madhya Maharashtra, Marathwada and Odisha till tomorrow.

title: Sensex, Nifty trading up during afternoon session
summary: Benchmark domestic equity indices were trading around half a per cent up in the afternoon session today. The Sensex rose 475 points to 76 thousand 740 while the Nifty advanced 115 points to 23 thousand 970, when reports last came in. On the sectoral front, Rea...
body: Benchmark domestic equity indices were trading around half a per cent up in the afternoon session today. The Sensex rose 475 points to 76 thousand 740 while the Nifty advanced 115 points to 23 thousand 970, when reports last came in. On the sectoral front, Realty, IT, and FMCG stocks gaining the most, while Metal, Hospitals, and healthcare stocks were bucking the trend

title: LS Speaker emphasises greater participation in democracy
summary: Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla has emphasized the importance of greater public participation in democracy. Interacting with participants of the Viksit Bharat Youth Parliament 2026 in New Delhi, Mr Birla said that despite being the world’s largest population with d...
body: Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla has emphasized the importance of greater public participation in democracy. Interacting with participants of the Viksit Bharat Youth Parliament 2026 in New Delhi, Mr Birla said that despite being the world’s largest population with diverse cultures and varied geographical conditions, the country has emerged as the world’s largest democracy after Independence, with increasing public participation. He said democracy has remained an integral part of country’s work culture and thought process. The Lok Sabha Speaker said that active and inclusive participation in democracy is essential for shaping the nation’s future and achieving the goal of a developed India. Lok Sabha Speaker said that India’s democracy is not merely a system of governance, but a vibrant tradition of dialogue, consensus, participation, and public involvement. He added that the Youth Parliament is a powerful platform for this democratic culture, which instills in the youth the values of leadership, a sense of duty, logical thinking, and placing the national interest above all.

title: Cough syrups’ sale in smaller villages permitted only through licensed pharmacies: Health Ministry
summary: The government has notified an amendment to the Drugs Rules, 1945, saying that the exemption given for the sale of cough syrups in small villages has been withdrawn. In a Gazette notification, the Ministry of Health and Family Welfare has said that the word “S...
body: The government has notified an amendment to the Drugs Rules, 1945, saying that the exemption given for the sale of cough syrups in small villages has been withdrawn. In a Gazette notification, the Ministry of Health and Family Welfare has said that the word “Syrup” from Schedule K of the drugs rules under the heading “Class of Drugs” has been omitted. The Ministry added that with the omission of the word “Syrup”, this exemption will no longer be available for cough syrups. Consequently, the sale and dispensing of cough syrups in smaller villages will now be required to take place only through duly licensed pharmacies in accordance with the provisions of the Drugs and Cosmetics Act, 1940 and the Rules framed under it. Earlier, the sale of cough syrups in villages with a population of less than one thousand without requiring compliance with certain retail sale licensing provisions was permitted. The Ministry added that the amendment was made to strengthen regulatory oversight of syrup formulations and to align the exemption framework with contemporary public health and safety requirements. It added that the measure is expected to promote responsible distribution and sale of cough syrups while ensuring greater compliance with regulatory standards across the country.

title: PM Modi to interact with leading CEOs in Paris
summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi will be arriving in Paris tomorrow evening as part of his visit to France. He is scheduled to hold meetings with leading Chief Executive Officers, with discussions expected to continue till the afternoon. Later, he will participate...
body: Prime Minister Narendra Modi will be arriving in Paris tomorrow evening as part of his visit to France. He is scheduled to hold meetings with leading Chief Executive Officers, with discussions expected to continue till the afternoon. Later, he will participate in the VivaTech Summit, one of Europe’s leading technology and innovation events, where he is expected to deliver an address. In the evening, Prime Minister Modi will attend a community event and interact with members of the Indian diaspora. The programme will also feature cultural performances showcasing India’s rich heritage and strengthening people-to-people ties between India and France.

title: India’s fiscal policy is evolving to support domestic growth
summary: India’s fiscal policy is evolving to support domestic growth and strengthen economic resilience amid rising global uncertainties triggered by the ongoing West Asia conflict. According to a report by S and P Global, the country’s risk management approach is tra...
body: India’s fiscal policy is evolving to support domestic growth and strengthen economic resilience amid rising global uncertainties triggered by the ongoing West Asia conflict. According to a report by S and P Global, the country’s risk management approach is transitioning from providing immediate economic buffers to building long-term strategic resilience. Despite the challenges, the report said India’s policy response reflects a broader emphasis on resilience and strategic autonomy. Fiscal measures, industrial policy adjustments and efforts to diversify energy sources are being deployed simultaneously.

title: SIT begins investigation of alleged misappropriation of donations and valuables at Ayodhya Temple
summary: The Special Investigation Team (SIT) constituted for the allegations of misappropriation of donations and valuables at the Ram Temple in Ayodhya begun its probe today. The SIT is headed by Divisional Commissioner of Lucknow Vijay Vishwas Pant with Inspector Ge...
body: The Special Investigation Team (SIT) constituted for the allegations of misappropriation of donations and valuables at the Ram Temple in Ayodhya begun its probe today. The SIT is headed by Divisional Commissioner of Lucknow Vijay Vishwas Pant with Inspector General of Police Kiran S and Special Secretary, Finance Department Neel Ratan as members. All members reached Ayodhya and started their investigation. They visited the Ram temple premises yesterday along with the commissioner and DM of Ayodhya. The SIT was constituted by the state government after allegations surfaced regarding irregularities in the handling of donations and valuables offered at the Ram Temple. Authorities have not yet made any findings public and the probe is at a preliminary stage. The committee was directed to submit a preliminary report in seven days and a final report within 15 days. One the other hand, Nripendra Mishra, Chairman of the Ram Temple Construction Committee is also in Ayodhya. Talking to media he said that there will not be any laxity in the probe. He said that whatever cooperation the SIT seeks, the district administration as well as Temple administration will provide.

title: Petroleum Ministry clarifies no proposal under consideration on Middle East-India deep water pipeline
summary: The Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas has clarified that no such proposal is currently under consideration by the Ministry on a proposed Middle East-India deep water pipeline. In a statement, the ministry said, it noticed a series of media reports suggesti...
body: The Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas has clarified that no such proposal is currently under consideration by the Ministry on a proposed Middle East-India deep water pipeline. In a statement, the ministry said, it noticed a series of media reports suggesting that the government is actively pursuing a deep-sea energy pipeline, sometimes referred to as the Middle East-India Deepwater Pipeline, connecting Gujarat to Oman and other Gulf countries. It added that there are no active discussions or negotiations with Oman or any other Gulf countries on this project at any level in the Ministry.

title: US markets soar over 3% amid announcement of US-Iran peace agreement
summary: Wall Street rallied yesterday following the announcement of US-Iran peace agreement. Among the major indices, the Nasdaq Composite Index soared over three per cent to close at 26 thousand 684, and the S&P 500 advanced more than 1.6 per cent to end at 7 thousan...
body: Wall Street rallied yesterday following the announcement of US-Iran peace agreement. Among the major indices, the Nasdaq Composite Index soared over three per cent to close at 26 thousand 684, and the S&P 500 advanced more than 1.6 per cent to end at 7 thousand 554. Meanwhile, the Dow Jones Industrial Average climbed 0.9 per cent to close at a record high of 51 thousand 671. Investors are now looking ahead to the Federal Reserve’s policy meeting, which is set to begin tonight under the new chairman Kevin Warsh.

title: PM Modi reiterates focus on next-gen infrastructure to realise vision of Viksit Bharat
summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi today said that the last decade has witnessed a record infrastructure upgrade across the country. Sharing a post of MyGov on his social media account, Mr Modi said that the government is committed to building next-generation infras...
body: Prime Minister Narendra Modi today said that the last decade has witnessed a record infrastructure upgrade across the country. Sharing a post of MyGov on his social media account, Mr Modi said that the government is committed to building next-generation infrastructure for the people of the country to realise the vision of a Viksit Bharat. The MyGov post highlighted the country’s infrastructure transformation over the last 12 years. It said that the infrastructure transformation has accelerated mobility, strengthened logistics, and unlocked new avenues of growth. The post highlighted that greater connectivity is not only reducing distances but also expanding opportunities and strengthening the foundations of a more prosperous India.

title: Bank of Japan raises interest rate to 1% amid weak Yen and higher prices
summary: The Bank of Japan has raised its benchmark interest rate to one percent today, citing challenges stemming from a weak Japanese yen and higher prices. Inflationary pressures due to the West Asia conflict, resulted in hike in oil prices, have hit Japan hard sinc...
body: The Bank of Japan has raised its benchmark interest rate to one percent today, citing challenges stemming from a weak Japanese yen and higher prices. Inflationary pressures due to the West Asia conflict, resulted in hike in oil prices, have hit Japan hard since it imports almost all its oil and gas. Low interest rates had added to pressures on the Japanese yen, which has fallen lately to about 160 yen to the US dollar.

title: SC seeks responses from Centre, States on plea to use Aadhaar only as identity proof
summary: The Supreme Court today sought the response of the Centre and States on a plea alleging misuse of the Unique Identification Authority of India-issued Aadhaar cards as proof of citizenship, domicile and residential address. The plea sought directions to restric...
body: The Supreme Court today sought the response of the Centre and States on a plea alleging misuse of the Unique Identification Authority of India-issued Aadhaar cards as proof of citizenship, domicile and residential address. The plea sought directions to restrict its use strictly for identity verification only. A bench of Chief Justice Surya Kant and Justice V Mohana issued notices to the Centre and all the states and Union Territories. The petition contended that the existing verification mechanism under Form-6 is inadequate and might allow individuals without proper supporting documents to be included in the electoral database. . The plea sought directions to restrict its use strictly for identity verification only. title: Sri Lanka cricket Tri series: Pacer Asho Sharma replaces injured Yudhvir Singh in India A squad
summary: Pacer Ashok Sharma has been included in the India A squad for the ongoing tri-series in Sri Lanka as a replacement for injured Yudhvir Singh. Yudhvir reported discomfort in his right shoulder while bowling and also experienced pain earlier during a fielding se...
body: Pacer Ashok Sharma has been included in the India A squad for the ongoing tri-series in Sri Lanka as a replacement for injured Yudhvir Singh. Yudhvir reported discomfort in his right shoulder while bowling and also experienced pain earlier during a fielding session. The BCCI said, the medical team has recommended that Yudhvir undergo a graduated rehabilitation programme under the supervision of the BCCI Centre of Excellence to achieve a full recovery from the right rotator cuff injury. India A have lost two games in a row in the tri-series, falling to Afghanistan A and host nation Sri Lanka A. India A will next take on Afghanistan A at Rangiri Dambulla International Stadium in Dambulla, Sri Lanka tomorrow.

title: Govt bans App Telegram till culmination of NEET UG re-exams 2026
summary: Ahead of the re-examination of the National Eligibility Cum Entrance Test (NEET) UG 2026 which is scheduled for 21st June, the government has restricted online messaging and social media app telegram till 22nd of this month. The National Testing Agency (NTA) h...
body: Ahead of the re-examination of the National Eligibility Cum Entrance Test (NEET) UG 2026 which is scheduled for 21st June, the government has restricted online messaging and social media app telegram till 22nd of this month. The National Testing Agency (NTA) has welcomed the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology’s (MEITY) directions for restricting Telegram access in India for a defined and limited period. In a statement, NTA said that the restriction will cover the day of the NEET (UG) 2026 re-examination and its immediate aftermath. The agency added that the platform has also been directed to disable the message-editing feature for already-posted messages until 30th of this month. According to the Agency, this step will address a feature that has allegedly been misused to fabricate post-facto claims of question paper leaks in national examinations. The NTA said both measures have been taken in the interest of public order and in response to the organised use of the platform by cheating rackets to defraud candidates appearing for the re-examination. NTA has expressed gratitude to the MEITY for the timely action and said the measures will help ensure the safe and secure conduct of the examination in the interest of students.

title: Oman: 21 rescued Indian crew members of vessel MT Settebello returned safely home
summary: Twenty-one Indian crew members of the vessel MT Settebello have safely returned home following a tragic maritime incident off the coast of Oman. The Indian Embassy in Muscat, Oman today shared that the Ambassador interacted with the rescued crew members before...
body: Twenty-one Indian crew members of the vessel MT Settebello have safely returned home following a tragic maritime incident off the coast of Oman. The Indian Embassy in Muscat, Oman today shared that the Ambassador interacted with the rescued crew members before their departure, conveyed his best wishes, and appreciated their resilience during the difficult ordeal. The Embassy reiterated its commitment to the welfare and safety of Indian nationals abroad. The Palau-flagged MT Settebello was about 30 nautical miles off the coast of Sohar, Oman, when it came under attack. Following the incident, the Oman Maritime Security Centre was immediately informed, prompting Omani authorities to launch a Search and Rescue operation. While 21 Indian crew members were successfully rescued, three seafarers lost their lives in the tragic incident at sea.

title: India cautions UNSC reforms fail if expansion limited to only non-permanent category of membership
summary: India has cautioned that efforts to reform the United Nations Security Council could fail if the expansion is limited to only non-permanent category of membership. India’s Permanent Representative to the UN, Ambassador Harish Parvathaneni, made these remarks w...
body: India has cautioned that efforts to reform the United Nations Security Council could fail if the expansion is limited to only non-permanent category of membership. India’s Permanent Representative to the UN, Ambassador Harish Parvathaneni, made these remarks while addressing the Inter-Governmental Negotiations meeting on Security Council reforms. Mr. Parvathaneni said, UNSC reform would be grossly inadequate, bordering on failure, if expansion is limited only to the non-permanent category, as it would fundamentally not change the decision-making power structure of the permanent five members. Mr. Parvathaneni stressed that by advocating expansion of the permanent category, India’s consistent effort has been to bring in a greater sense of balance and equity in the Security Council and change the decision-making parameters of the five veto-wielding permanent members – China, France, Russia, UK and the US. India also underscored the need for text-based negotiations to ensure that discussions are more purposeful and outcome-oriented.

title: India joins G7 efforts in fight against Ebola outbreak, Cancer
summary: From left, European Council President Antonio Costa, Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni, South Korea's President Lee Jae Myung, Egyptian President Abdel-Fattah el Sissi, British Prime Minister Keir Starmer, U.S. President Donald Trump, Kenya's President Wil...
body: From left, European Council President Antonio Costa, Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni, South Korea's President Lee Jae Myung, Egyptian President Abdel-Fattah el Sissi, British Prime Minister Keir Starmer, U.S. President Donald Trump, Kenya's President William Ruto, French President Emmanuel Macron Japan's Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi, India's Prime Minister Narenda Modi, Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney, German Chancellor Friedrich Merz, European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen and Brazil's President Luiz Inacio Lula da Silva pose during a group photo of G7 leaders and invited nations during the G7 summit in Evian-les-Bains, France, Tuesday, June 16, 2026. AP/PTI(AP06_16_2026_000342A) The G7 nations have announced a series of initiatives to strengthen global health and development cooperation, with several partner countries, including India, extending their support. The G7 leaders called for a coordinated international response to the re-emerging Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo and Uganda. India, Egypt, Kenya and South Korea joined the appeal for enhanced measures, including contact tracing, border surveillance and community engagement. The leaders also pledged support for the development of vaccines, diagnostics and treatments for the rare Ebola strain. The G7 further reaffirmed its commitment to accelerating the global fight against cancer. Supported by partner countries, including India and Brazil, the leaders stressed the importance of prevention, early diagnosis and access to quality healthcare, while advocating greater cooperation in cancer research and the responsible use of emerging technologies. The grouping also reiterated its commitment to international cooperation in development and investment finance to support vulnerable countries and promote sustainable and resilient growth. title: India provides military stores worth $5.5 million to Sri Lanka Army
summary: India has provided military stores worth 5.5 million US dollars to the Sri Lanka Army on a gratis basis, reaffirming the close defence partnership between the two countries. The assistance, approved by the Ministry of Defence, Government of India, was supplied...
body: India has provided military stores worth 5.5 million US dollars to the Sri Lanka Army on a gratis basis, reaffirming the close defence partnership between the two countries. The assistance, approved by the Ministry of Defence, Government of India, was supplied from the Indian Army’s operational stocks to meet an urgent requirement of the Sri Lankan Army. The military stores were transported to Colombo onboard Indian Naval Ship Sharda. The formal handover ceremony was attended by Indian High Commissioner to Sri Lanka Santosh Jha, Sri Lankan Defence Secretary Air Vice Marshal (Retd.) Sampath Thuyacontha, and Sri Lanka Army Commander Lt. Gen. Lasantha Rodrigo. Over the past two years, India has approved defence-related projects worth five billion Sri Lankan rupees for Sri Lanka. These initiatives include infrastructure development, the supply of military equipment and training assistance aimed at enhancing the capabilities of the Sri Lankan armed forces. India had also extended humanitarian assistance to Sri Lanka by deploying a field hospital and an engineering task force during Cyclone Ditwah relief operations. The assistance reflects India’s Neighbourhood First policy and its continued commitment to promoting regional security, stability and cooperation under the MAHASAGAR vision.

title: United States begins easing naval blockade of Iranian ports
summary: The United States has begun easing its naval blockade of Iranian ports, a significant step toward de-escalation following a framework agreement to end the ongoing conflict. Iranian Deputy Foreign Minister Majid Takht-Ravanchi confirmed the development today, s...
body: The United States has begun easing its naval blockade of Iranian ports, a significant step toward de-escalation following a framework agreement to end the ongoing conflict. Iranian Deputy Foreign Minister Majid Takht-Ravanchi confirmed the development today, stating that the lifting of the blockade has entered a practical phase. He said the lifting of the blockade was something we had emphasized from the outset. It has now begun, and the blockade has been lifted prior to the formal signing. Shipping activity in the region has already begun to reflect these changes. Reports indicate that at least three Iranian oil tankers and two cargo vessels successfully navigated through the Strait of Hormuz on today without obstruction. This movement serves as an early test of the maritime provisions outlined in the memorandum of understanding reached between Washington and Tehran. Delegations from the United States and Iran are set to meet in Switzerland this Friday, June 19, to formally sign the historic peace accord . The agreement is expected to stabilize regional energy flows and provide a framework for future diplomatic negotiations.

title: Indian Railways approves ₹755 crore Champa-Korba Third Line Project of SECR
summary: Indian Railways has approved the Champa-Korba Third Line Project of South East Central Railway (SECR) of 42 kilometres at an estimated cost of 755 crore rupees. Ministry of Railways said that the project involves the construction of a third railway line betwee...
body: Indian Railways has approved the Champa-Korba Third Line Project of South East Central Railway (SECR) of 42 kilometres at an estimated cost of 755 crore rupees. Ministry of Railways said that the project involves the construction of a third railway line between Champa and Korba, excluding the already sanctioned Madwarani-Saragbundia section. The corridor has been identified under Indian Railways’ Mission 3000 MT and High-Density Network (HDN) Corridor initiatives aimed at strengthening freight movement and supporting the country’s growing energy requirements. The Champa-Korba section serves as a critical rail link connecting the coalfields of South Eastern Coalfields Limited (SECL) and Mahanadi Coalfields Limited (MCL) with the national rail network and the Mumbai-Howrah High Density Corridor. The region’s combined coal production capacity of SECL and MCL is about 247 Million Tonnes Per Annum (MTPA) and is projected to rise to nearly 450 MTPA, adding around 200 MTPA of additional coal traffic in the coming years. The third line project will significantly enhance carrying capacity, improve operational flexibility and ensure faster movement of both freight and passenger trains. Upon completion, the project is expected to facilitate operation of two additional passenger trains in each direction every day and support additional freight movement of 5.95 MTPA. The third line will also reduce detention of freight trains, which currently face delays of around five minutes in each direction, resulting in operational savings estimated at 1.30 crore rupees annually.

title: FIFA WC: Cape Verde stun Spain in scoreless draw; Belgium also settle for draw with Egypt
summary: In the FIFA World Cup 2026, Cape Verde displayed an extraordinary performance to secure a 0-0 draw with global heavyweights Spain. Cape Verde’s 40 years old goalkeeper Vozinha delivered a heroic performance by saving several goals from the opponent side. Meanw...
body: In the FIFA World Cup 2026, Cape Verde displayed an extraordinary performance to secure a 0-0 draw with global heavyweights Spain. Cape Verde’s 40 years old goalkeeper Vozinha delivered a heroic performance by saving several goals from the opponent side. Meanwhile, Iran and New Zealand in their Group G opener match ended in a 2-2 draw this morning. The match was a rollercoaster thriller held at Los Angeles Stadium in California. New Zealand took the lead twice through a brace from Elijah Just, but Iran showcased phenomenal resilience to fight back and equalize on both occasions. In another match, Belgium and Egypt drew 1-1 in their Group G opening match at Seattle Stadium. Egypt came close to securing its first-ever World Cup victory but were denied by a quick response from Belgium’s substitutes. Egypt’s Emam Ashour earns the superior player of the match trophy. Saudi Arabia and Uruguay also drew 1-1 in their opening match of Group H in Miami Stadium in US this morning. Abdulelah Al-Amri scored on a rebound in the 41st minute for Saudi Arabia, and Uruguay’s Maxi Araújo responded with an equalizer in the 80th minutes.

title: UNGA interviewing candidates for next Secretary-General; Guterres’s term ends this December
summary: Berlin, Germany - November 04: Antonio Guterres, High Commissioner for Refugees of UNHCR, attends a press conference in german foreign office on November 04, 2015 in Berlin, Germany. (Photo by Michael Gottschalk/Photothek via Getty Images) The UN General Assem...
body: Berlin, Germany - November 04: Antonio Guterres, High Commissioner for Refugees of UNHCR, attends a press conference in german foreign office on November 04, 2015 in Berlin, Germany. (Photo by Michael Gottschalk/Photothek via Getty Images) The UN General Assembly (UNGA) convened its 5th interactive dialogue with a candidate for the position of next UN secretary-General. During the dialogue, Maria Fernanda Espinosa, former UNGA president and former foreign minister and defence minister of Ecuador, nominated by Antigua and Barbuda in May, presented her vision statement and answered questions from UN member states and civil society representatives. In April this year, the UNGA conducted a 2-day interactive dialogue with four candidates for the next UN chief- including Michelle Bachelet, former Chilean president and former UN high commissioner for human rights, nominated by Brazil and Mexico; Rafael Grossi, current director general of the International Atomic Energy Agency, nominated by Argentina; Macky Sall, former president of Senegal, nominated by Burundi; and Rebeca Grynspan, economist and former vice president of Costa Rica, nominated by Costa Rica. The term of Antonio Guterres, the current and 9th secretary-general of the United Nations, will expire at the end of this year. The next UN secretary-general will take over the role on 1st of January 1 next year.
