title: Govt refutes claims that PM Modi and FM Sitharaman endorsed investment platform promising easy monetary returns
summary: The government has refuted a video circulating on social media claiming that Prime Minister Narendra Modi and Union Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman have endorsed an investment platform promising easy monetary returns. The Fact Check Unit of the Press Infor...
body: The government has refuted a video circulating on social media claiming that Prime Minister Narendra Modi and Union Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman have endorsed an investment platform promising easy monetary returns. The Fact Check Unit of the Press Information Bureau (PIB) has informed that the video is fake and AI-generated. The unit added that no such schemes had been endorsed by the government. PIB said that such fabricated endorsements and promises of extraordinary returns are spread by fraudsters to deceive the public. The government has further urged people to verify such information from official sources only.

title: PM Narendra Modi and his Slovak counterpart agree to establish joint working group on counter-terrorism
summary: Bratislava: Prime Minister Narendra Modi with Prime Minister of Slovakia Robert Fico during a delegation-level meeting in Bratislava, Slovakia, on Monday, June 15, 2026. (Photo: IANS/PMO) India and Slovakia agreed to set up a joint working group for counter-te...
body: Bratislava: Prime Minister Narendra Modi with Prime Minister of Slovakia Robert Fico during a delegation-level meeting in Bratislava, Slovakia, on Monday, June 15, 2026. (Photo: IANS/PMO) India and Slovakia agreed to set up a joint working group for counter-terrorism and the establishment of consular dialogue. According to the joint statement, Prime Minister Modi and his Slovak counterpart, Robert Fico, during the delegation-level talks in Bratislava, also agreed to establish a joint working group on counter-terrorism. During the delegation-level talks, 11 agreements were signed in the fields ranging from labour migration, defence, digital technologies, higher education, audio-visual production, establishment of an AI Chair, quantum communication, cooperation in student exchange programs and tourism development. An agreement was also signed on Scientific Cooperation between the Indian National Science Academy and the Slovak Academy of Sciences.

title: India and Slovakia elevate ties to comprehensive partnership; Ink 11 pacts to boost cooperation
summary: India and Slovakia today elevated their ties to a comprehensive partnership and inked 11 agreements to expand bilateral cooperation. These agreements have been signed in a range of areas such as migration, digital technology and defence following talks between...
body: India and Slovakia today elevated their ties to a comprehensive partnership and inked 11 agreements to expand bilateral cooperation. These agreements have been signed in a range of areas such as migration, digital technology and defence following talks between Prime Minister Narendra Modi and his Slovak counterpart, Robert Fico. The two leaders also agreed to work towards the earliest implementation of the India-European Union free trade agreement, that is expected to significantly shore up the bilateral economic engagement. In the first visit to the European nation by an Indian Prime Minister, Mr Modi said elevation of relations to the status of a Comprehensive Partnership symbolises shared beliefs, priorities and future. The Prime Minister said he discussed ways to give a new direction to the ties with his Slovakian counterpart Robert Fico. Mr Modi also said that the defence cooperation between India and Slovakia is a testament to the deep mutual trust and strategic convergence between the two sides. A Letter of Intent was finalised on defence cooperation following the talks between the two leaders. The Prime Minister said it will give new momentum for cooperation between defence industries of the two sides. The agreements inked in areas of labour migration, defence, digital technologies, higher education and quantum communication. Mr. Modi and Mr. Fico expressed support for the orderly, safe and legal mobility of skilled professionals and welcomed the signing of the Memorandum of Understanding on cooperation in the field of labour migration to facilitate mobility. The two sides also agreed to work towards the early conclusion of a Social Security Agreement, which would contribute to safeguarding the welfare and social protection of working professionals engaged in mobility between India and Slovakia. Both leaders also agreed to enhance cooperation in the energy sector with a view to promoting energy security, sustainability and resilience through diversification of energy sources, including nuclear energy and geothermal power. Prime Minister Modi also conveyed his appreciation to Mr Fico for the cooperation Slovakia extended in finalising the India-EU free trade agreement. In his remarks, PM Fico hailed the India-EU FTA, describing it as one of the most ambitious trade pacts ever signed. In the meeting, the two leaders, recognising the growing geopolitical, economic and technological importance of the Indo-Pacific, acknowledged the value of a stronger partnership between India and Slovakia in support of mutually beneficial cooperation, regional connectivity, open international trade, freedom of navigation, peaceful resolution of disputes, according to a joint statement.

title: DRDO successfully conducts flight-test of indigenously developed Long Range Land Attack Cruise Missile
summary: The Defence Research and Development Organisation (DRDO) today successfully conducted the flight test of the indigenously developed Long Range Land Attack Cruise Missile (LRLACM) from Dr APJ Abdul Kalam Island, Odisha. The LRLACM is an indigenously developed m...
body: The Defence Research and Development Organisation (DRDO) today successfully conducted the flight test of the indigenously developed Long Range Land Attack Cruise Missile (LRLACM) from Dr APJ Abdul Kalam Island, Odisha. The LRLACM is an indigenously developed missile with all sub-systems developed by various DRDO laboratories and Indian Industry partners. The Defence Ministry said that all the test objectives were fully met during the flight test. It said the missile achieved all mission objectives, validating critical technologies and demonstrating the country’s growing long-range precision strike capability. It added that the successful test is another major step towards Aatmanirbhar Bharat and a stronger national defence ecosystem. Defence Minister Rajnath Singh congratulated the DRDO team and the Industry Partners on the successful flight test of LRLACM. Minister of State for External Affairs, Kirti Vardhan Singh, has congratulated DRDO, scientists, engineers and industry partners on the successful test of the Long Range Land Attack Cruise Missile. In a social media post, Mr Singh said that this landmark achievement reflects the country’s growing defence capabilities. He added that this success also reflects the vision of Prime Minister Narendra Modi of Aatmanirbhar Bharat, empowering indigenous innovation and strengthening national security.

title: Govt says all refineries operating at high capacity and maintaining sufficient stocks of petrol, diesel
summary: The government has said that all refineries are operating at high capacity with adequate crude inventories, and maintaining sufficient stocks of petrol and diesel. Addressing the Inter-Ministerial Briefing in New Delhi today on recent developments in West Asia...
body: The government has said that all refineries are operating at high capacity with adequate crude inventories, and maintaining sufficient stocks of petrol and diesel. Addressing the Inter-Ministerial Briefing in New Delhi today on recent developments in West Asia, Joint Secretary in the Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas Sujata Sharma said that over the past four days, one crore 84 lakh cylinders have been delivered. She said, during the same period, around 24,184 tonnes of commercial LPG have been sold. She added that about 2,18,005 kilogram LPG cylinders were sold, including nearly 14,500 cylinders distributed through camps. The Joint Secretary underlined that the Government has temporarily restricted institutional, industrial and commercial customers from buying petrol and diesel from retail outlets and asked them to source their requirements from their own consumer pump. Ms Sharma informed that retail outlets have been directed to sell upto 200 litres of High Speed Diesel to a customer per day to prevent bulk purchase after such outlets reported unusually high sales. She said that the shift led to supply issues at retail outlets. Joint Secretary in the Department of Fertilizers Bandana Preyashi said that the overall stock position of fertilizers in the country is normal. She said that for Kharif season 2026, the fertilizer requirement has been re-assessed by the Department of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare at 383.9 lakh metric tonnes. She added that farmers already purchased a total 102.78 lakh metric tonnes of chemical fertilizers in the on-going Kharif-2026 till today which is approximately 27 percent of the total requirement. Director in the Ministry of Ports, Shipping and Waterways Opesh Kumar Sharma said that the LNG carrier DISHA carrying 62,370 metric tonnes cargo, today safely transited the Strait of Hormuz. Mr Sharma said that the vessel is expected to reach Dahej port in Gujarat on Thursday. He said that the Directorate General of Shipping is closely monitoring the situation and is in continuous coordination with different stakeholders. He added that the Ministry has facilitated the safe repatriation of more than 3587 Indian seafarers so far, including 50 in the last 96 hours from various locations across the Gulf region.

title: ASI and National Museum of Denmark sign MoU for underwater archaeological investigation of historic Danish ship Oresun
summary: The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) and the National Museum of Denmark today signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) for the underwater archaeological investigation of the historic Danish ship Oresun. The Culture Ministry said that the project is aimed...
body: The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) and the National Museum of Denmark today signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) for the underwater archaeological investigation of the historic Danish ship Oresun. The Culture Ministry said that the project is aimed at locating and documenting the remains of the historic Danish ship Oresund, which was wrecked off the coast near Karaikal, Puducherry, in the 17th century. The Oresund occupies a significant place in maritime history as the first Danish ship known to have reached India. Shortly after its arrival in Indian waters, the vessel was wrecked near Karaikal, making it an important archaeological resource for understanding the early maritime interactions between Denmark and India. Under the terms of the MoU, the project will focus on conducting a non-invasive archaeological survey using advanced technologies and scientific methods to identify potential remains of the shipwreck. The investigations will be carried out by the Underwater Archaeology Wing of the Archaeological Survey of India in collaboration with the National Museum of Denmark.

title: ED attaches immovable properties worth over Rs 500 cr of Raheja Developers Ltd
summary: The Directorate of Enforcement (ED) has provisionally attached immovable properties worth over 500 crore rupees of Raheja Developers Limited, as well as immovable properties held in the names of its Director Navin M. Raheja and his family members. The attachme...
body: The Directorate of Enforcement (ED) has provisionally attached immovable properties worth over 500 crore rupees of Raheja Developers Limited, as well as immovable properties held in the names of its Director Navin M. Raheja and his family members. The attachments have been made under the provisions of the Prevention of Money Laundering Act (PMLA), 2002. ED is conducting the investigation based on complaints lodged by a large number of homebuyers in connection with various residential real estate projects launched by the company. The total estimated current market value of assets attached in this case so far is over 1,617 Crore rupees.

title: World leaders welcome peace deal announced between US & Iran
summary: Leaders across the World have welcomed the peace deal announced between the United States and Iran, describing it as a major diplomatic breakthrough that could help restore stability in West Asia. Qatar Prime Minister Sheikh Mohammed bin Abdulrahman bin Jassim...
body: Leaders across the World have welcomed the peace deal announced between the United States and Iran, describing it as a major diplomatic breakthrough that could help restore stability in West Asia. Qatar Prime Minister Sheikh Mohammed bin Abdulrahman bin Jassim Al Thani welcomed the agreement and praised the role played by regional and international actors in facilitating the understanding. UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer termed the agreement a hugely important step forward in ending the war, ensuring regional stability and re-opening the Strait of Hormuz. German Chancellor Friedrich Merz also welcomed the agreement, describing it as a diplomatic achievement with significant global implications. French President Emmanuel Macron called for the rapid implementation of the agreement and emphasised the importance of reopening the Strait of Hormuz without restrictions.

title: UN welcomes US-Iran agreement on peace deal
summary: UN Secretary-General Antonio Guterres welcomes the announcement that the United States and Iran have agreed on a peace deal that provides for an immediate and permanent ceasefire, reopening of Strait of Hormuz, as well as a framework for further negotiations. ...
body: UN Secretary-General Antonio Guterres welcomes the announcement that the United States and Iran have agreed on a peace deal that provides for an immediate and permanent ceasefire, reopening of Strait of Hormuz, as well as a framework for further negotiations. Secretary-General spokesperson Stephane Dujarric said that this represents a critical step towards the peaceful settlement of the conflict.

title: PM Narendra Modi expresses gratitude to Slovak PM Fico for supporting India-EU FTA talks
summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi expressed gratitude to Slovak Prime Minister Robert Fico for Slovakia’s support in advancing negotiations on the India-EU Free Trade Agreement, saying both sides would work towards its early implementation to benefit industries,...
body: Prime Minister Narendra Modi expressed gratitude to Slovak Prime Minister Robert Fico for Slovakia’s support in advancing negotiations on the India-EU Free Trade Agreement, saying both sides would work towards its early implementation to benefit industries, startups and traders in both countries. Addressing the joint press address along with the Slovak Prime Minister in Bratislava today, Prime Minister Modi said the successful conclusion of the FTA would mark a significant milestone in India-EU economic relations and open new opportunities for bilateral economic engagement between India and Slovakia. Highlighting the growing role of technology in the partnership, the Prime Minister said digital technology will be a key pillar of future cooperation between the two countries. He noted that a Memorandum of Understanding signed on digital technology would unlock new possibilities in digital public infrastructure and innovation-led collaboration. Prime Minister also underlined the deep cultural and people-to-people ties between India and Slovakia, describing them as a strong foundation for bilateral relations. He cited the translation of India’s ancient Upanishads into the Slovak language as an example of cultural closeness between the two nations. He further said that the Indian diaspora in Slovakia is contributing meaningfully to the country’s economy and society, strengthening mutual goodwill and understanding. On global issues, the Prime Minister said India and Slovakia are working in close coordination on the international stage and share the belief that all disputes and tensions should be resolved peacefully. Speaking on defence cooperation, PM Modi said the sector reflects growing mutual trust and strategic convergence between the two countries. He stated that a Letter of Intent signed in the defence sector would provide fresh momentum to joint development, joint production, and industrial collaboration. The Prime Minister also said that an India Chair on Artificial Intelligence at a Slovak university, stressing that the future of AI must be built on innovation as well as trust, responsibility, and human dignity. Calling the visit historic, Prime Minister Modi noted that this is the first visit by any Indian Prime Minister to Slovakia. On this occasion, both countries agreed to elevate their bilateral relationship to a Comprehensive Partnership, which he said reflects shared values, priorities, and a common vision for the future. Delivering the joint press statement with Prime Minister Modi, the Prime Minister of Slovakia, Robert Fico, said that India should be included in the UN Security Council as a permanent member. He pointed out that the Slovak Republic and India are countries which are connected by a number of common topics. The Slovakian Prime Minister added that Slovakia welcomes the agreement that was reached between the EU and India for free trade and called it one of the most ambitious free trade agreements ever signed. He said that Slovakia is also interested in cooperation in the field of the defence industry. Mr Fico congratulated Prime Minister Narendra Modi on becoming the longest-serving Indian Prime Minister. Prime Minister Fico also pointed out that India is faster than Western countries in digitalization and AI. summary: The US and Iran finalised a deal to end their 107-day war and open the Strait of Hormuz, the narrow waterway used to ferry one-fifth of the global oil supplies. US President Donald Trump made the announcement of Truth Social last night, saying America will end...
body: The US and Iran finalised a deal to end their 107-day war and open the Strait of Hormuz, the narrow waterway used to ferry one-fifth of the global oil supplies. title: ICC Women’s T20: India hand Pakistan a crushing defeat
summary: In ICC Women’s T20 World Cup, India defeated Pakistan by 64 runs in a Group A cricket match at Edgbaston in Birmingham. India set a target of 171 runs for arch rivals Pakistan. In reply Pakistan were all out with 106 runs in 17 overs. Opting to bat, India post...
body: In ICC Women’s T20 World Cup, India defeated Pakistan by 64 runs in a Group A cricket match at Edgbaston in Birmingham. India set a target of 171 runs for arch rivals Pakistan. In reply Pakistan were all out with 106 runs in 17 overs. Opting to bat, India posted 170 for six in their allotted 20 overs. Smriti Mandhana was the highest run scorer with 68 runs in 44 deliveries. Earlier Indian skipper Harmanpreet Kaur won the toss and elected to bat. Deepti Sharma was adjudged as the player of the match. In the other fixture of the day, Bangladesh registered a convincing six-wicket victory over the Netherlands. Opting to bat, Netherland posted 138 for eight in their allotted 20 overs. In response, Bangladesh chased down the target comfortably, scoring 141 for 4 in 19.1 overs. Juairiya Ferdous was awarded player of the match for her match winning knock of 50 runs in 33 balls. The month-long tournament is being played across England and Wales, with the final set to be held at Lord’s Cricket Ground on 5th July.

title: Remarkable transformation of India under Sabka Saath, Sabka Vikas, Sabka Vishwas and Sabka Prayas
summary: India has undergone a remarkable transformation, driven by the principles of Sabka Saath, Sabka Vikas, Sabka Vishwas and Sabka Prayas under the visionary leadership of Prime Minister Narendra Modi in the last 12 years. During this period, the Modi government c...
body: India has undergone a remarkable transformation, driven by the principles of Sabka Saath, Sabka Vikas, Sabka Vishwas and Sabka Prayas under the visionary leadership of Prime Minister Narendra Modi in the last 12 years. During this period, the Modi government carried out many flagship initiatives for the welfare and empowerment of the poor, farmers, women and youth. A strong and modern nation is built on the foundations of world-class infrastructure. Over the past twelve years, India has embarked on an unprecedented journey of transformation, making infrastructure-led development the cornerstone of its growth story. This commitment is reflected in a sixfold increase in government expenditure on infrastructure, rising from 2 lakh crore rupees in 2014-15 to 12.2 lakh crore rupees in the current financial year. In the last 12 years, the country’s urban transportation has been revolutionized. India’s metro rail network has expanded nearly fivefold, from 248 kilometres to eleven hundred kilometers today, making cities more connected, efficient, and commuter-friendly. India today has the world’s second-largest road network. The National Highway network has expanded from over 91 thousand kilometers in 2014 to over 1 lakh 46 thousand kilometers in 2026. The transformation is equally visible in the railways sector. Budgetary support for Indian Railways has increased nearly nine times, from 32 thousand crore rupees in 2014-15 to 2.78 lakh crore rupees now. This investment has powered modernization and improved passenger experience across the network. A symbol of this new era is the Vande Bharat train. With 162 services now operational, it reducing travel time up to 45 percent. Together, these achievements are creating a faster, more connected, and more prosperous India laying the foundation for a Viksit Bharat by 2047.

title: Indian diaspora accords warm welcome to PM Modi upon his arrival in Slovakia
summary: Prime Minister Modi arrived in Bratislava last night on a two-day official visit to Slovakia after completing the first leg of his visit to Nice in France. Upon his arrival in Bratislava, Mr Modi was given a warm welcome and accorded traditional Slovak hospita...
body: Prime Minister Modi arrived in Bratislava last night on a two-day official visit to Slovakia after completing the first leg of his visit to Nice in France. Upon his arrival in Bratislava, Mr Modi was given a warm welcome and accorded traditional Slovak hospitality. The Prime Minister arrived in the Slovak capital on the first-ever visit by an Indian Prime Minister to the Slovak Republic. The Prime Minister was received by Slovak Foreign Minister Juraj Blanár upon his arrival in Bratislava last night. Members of the Indian diaspora gathered in Bratislava welcomed him with slogans upon his arrival. Slovak musicians of the Lúčnica Ensemble performed Vande Mataram on the Prime Minister’s arrival. Mr. Narendra Modi will be accorded a ceremonial welcome today.

title: LS Speaker Om Birla will inaugurate Viksit Bharat Youth Parliament 2026 in New Delhi
summary: The Ministry of Youth Affairs and Sports is hosting the Viksit Bharat Youth Parliament 2026 in New Delhi. Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla will inaugurate the Viksit Bharat Youth Parliament at the National level tomorrow. The Ministry said that the initiative reimag...
body: The Ministry of Youth Affairs and Sports is hosting the Viksit Bharat Youth Parliament 2026 in New Delhi. Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla will inaugurate the Viksit Bharat Youth Parliament at the National level tomorrow. The Ministry said that the initiative reimagines youth engagement with democratic processes, offering young citizens a hands-on understanding of governance, policy deliberation, and parliamentary functioning. It said that the Viksit Bharat Youth Parliament is a movement that channels the energy, ideas, and aspirations of India’s youth into the national conversation.

title: NHRC takes Suo Motu Cognisance of Girl’s Death in Stray Dog Attack in Punjab’s Rupnagar District
summary: The National Human Rights Commission (NHRC) has taken suo-motu cognizance of a media report wherein it was reported that an 8-year-old girl was mauled to death by a pack of stray dogs in the Rupnagar district of Punjab. The girl was playing outside her house w...
body: The National Human Rights Commission (NHRC) has taken suo-motu cognizance of a media report wherein it was reported that an 8-year-old girl was mauled to death by a pack of stray dogs in the Rupnagar district of Punjab. The girl was playing outside her house when the stray dogs attacked her. Taking a serious view of the matter, NHRC issued a notice to the Punjab Chief Secretary, calling for a detailed report on the matter within two weeks. According to the media report, the local residents rescued the victim and rushed her to the PGIMER in Chandigarh, but she succumbed to the injuries on the way.

title: PM Modi holds bilateral talks with his Slovak counterpart Robert Fico in Bratislava
summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi and his Slovak counterpart, Robert Fico, held delegation-level talks in Bratislava today. Addressing a joint press conference after the talks, Prime Minister Narendra Modi said India and Slovakia have decided to elevate ties to the...
body: Prime Minister Narendra Modi and his Slovak counterpart, Robert Fico, held delegation-level talks in Bratislava today. Addressing a joint press conference after the talks, Prime Minister Narendra Modi said India and Slovakia have decided to elevate ties to the status of Comprehensive Partnership. He said that defence cooperation between the two nations is a testament to deep mutual trust and strategic convergence. He said both countries have signed a letter of intent in the field of the defence sector. Mr Modi said the future of AI should be based not only on innovation but also on trust, responsibility, and human dignity. Mr Modi said that the MOU signed today on digital technology will open new avenues of cooperation between the two countries. Speaking on this occasion, Slovak Prime Minister Robert Fico lauded India’s rapid economic growth, digital transformation and advances in artificial intelligence, saying the country has outpaced many advanced nations in key sectors. He said India must be included in the United Nations’ Security Council as a permanent member.

title: Domestic markets witnesses strong gains during afternoon trade

body: Maharashtra, Jan 18 (ANI): File photo of Bombay Stock Exchange (BSE) building as Sensex slips by 439.77 points, currently at 48,594.90. Nifty down by 152.45 points, currently at 14,281.25., in Mumbai on Monday. (ANI Photo) Benchmark domestic equity indices witnessed strong gains in the afternoon session today following the announcement of a peace deal between the United States and Iran. The Sensex climbed 922 points to 76 thousand 450, while the Nifty rose 287 points to 23 thousand 910, when reports last came in. Easing geopolitical concerns lifted investor confidence, triggering broad-based buying across the markets, with most sectoral indices trading in the green. However, the Pharma and Healthcare sectors bucked the trend and remained under pressure .

title: NDA govt privileged to fullfill aspirations of middle class: PM Modi
summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi has said that the middle class of the country have contributed to nation-building in countless ways and the NDA Government is privileged to be working towards fulfilling the aspirations of them. In a social media post, Mr Modi note...
body: Prime Minister Narendra Modi has said that the middle class of the country have contributed to nation-building in countless ways and the NDA Government is privileged to be working towards fulfilling the aspirations of them. In a social media post, Mr Modi noted that over the last decade, governance has increasingly focused on improving the quality of life of ordinary citizens. He added that the Government’s efforts focused on providing easier access to opportunities, better infrastructure, improved public services, affordable healthcare, quality education, cleaner cities and reduced burdens in everyday life. BJP President Nitin Nabin today said that over the past 12 years, the government has made comprehensive changes to empower India’s middle class with economic security, better facilities, and new opportunities. In a social media post, Mr Nabin highlighted several policies undertaken by the government including tax relief through income tax reforms and transparency in the tax system via GST. He added that initiatives such as Ujjwala Yojana, Jal Jeevan Mission, and Swachh Bharat have also made basic life facilities accessible for citizens. Mr Nabin further said the schemes like Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana have enabled people with an opportunity to realize the dream of owning a home and health security from Ayushman Bharat. The BJP President noted that through such initiatives the government has empowered middle class consumers which has become a major force in India’s economy and is playing an important role in accelerating the resolve for Atmanirbhar Bharat and Viksit Bharat.

title: Science-led development enabled technology to become integral part of major flagship programmes: Dr Jitendra Singh
summary: Union Minister for Science and Technology Dr Jitendra Singh today said science and technology have emerged as key drivers of India’s development journey over the past 12 years, transforming from a niche sector into a central pillar of governance, innovation an...
body: Union Minister for Science and Technology Dr Jitendra Singh today said science and technology have emerged as key drivers of India’s development journey over the past 12 years, transforming from a niche sector into a central pillar of governance, innovation and public welfare. Addressing a press conference in New Delhi on the theme of 12 Years of Science in Service of India, the Minister said the government’s focus on science-led development has enabled technology to become an integral part of major national missions and flagship programmes. He said the sector has witnessed a shift towards greater industry participation, innovation and public-private collaboration. The Minister also highlighted the growth of India’s biotechnology sector and said the country’s bio economy has witnessed significant expansion over the past decade. The Minister said scientific institutions have delivered several globally recognised success stories in recent years. He cited advancements in biotechnology, gene and cell therapies, the Purple Revolution in Jammu and Kashmir, hydrogen fuel cell technology and improved weather forecasting systems as examples of science transla ting into tangible benefits for citizens. Dr Singh has further highlighted reforms in the space, geospatial and nuclear sectors, saying these measures have expanded private sector participation and strengthened India’s scientific ecosystem. He said country has emerged as a leading global player in science and technology through a combination of policy reforms, innovation and institutional support.

title: India’s WPI-based inflation accelerates to 9.68 % year-on-year in May
summary: India’s Wholesale Price Index (WPI) based inflation accelerated to 9.68 per cent year-on-year in May under the newly released WPI series, compared with 8.26 per cent in April under the old series. The release marks the first major revision of India’s wholesale...
body: India’s Wholesale Price Index (WPI) based inflation accelerated to 9.68 per cent year-on-year in May under the newly released WPI series, compared with 8.26 per cent in April under the old series. The release marks the first major revision of India’s wholesale inflation series in over a decade, with the base year updated from 2012 to 2023. Data released by the Ministry of Commerce and Industry showed that the rise in inflation last month was primarily driven by higher prices of mineral oils, crude petroleum and natural gas, manufacture of chemicals and chemical products, and manufacture of basic metals. The sharp rise in WPI inflation reflects the continued impact of elevated global crude oil prices amid persistent geopolitical tensions in West Asia, increasing the risk of imported inflation spreading further through domestic supply chains. Among the major categories, the annual inflation rate in the fuel and power segment soared to 30.33 per cent in May, sharply up from 24.89 per cent in April. Inflation in primary articles also rose significantly to 5 per cent, from 3.78 per cent. Manufactured products, which carry the highest weight in the WPI basket, recorded an inflation rate of 7.48 per cent, compared with 6.68 per cent. Meanwhile, the WPI Food Index, comprising food articles and food products, edged up to 4.49 per cent from 3.11 per cent, during the same period.

title: UK announces social media ban for children under-16
summary: In a landmark move to protect children online, the UK government bans teenagers under 16 from accessing high-risk social media platforms. Prime Minister Keir Starmer described the move as a “big moment” that would bring “real change” for children and their fut...
body: In a landmark move to protect children online, the UK government bans teenagers under 16 from accessing high-risk social media platforms. Prime Minister Keir Starmer described the move as a “big moment” that would bring “real change” for children and their future, saying the measures are aimed at giving young people their childhood back. The government says it will go further than a blanket ban on social media by blocking “harmful functions such as livestreaming and stranger communication with children for under-16s”. According to the official statements, these restrictions will apply to a wider range of online services, including gaming sites as well.

title: India welcomes peace deal between Iran and the United States
summary: India has welcomed the understanding reached between the United States and Iran on ending the conflict in West Asia, which has caused serious economic disruption across the world and led to loss of life in many countries. In a social media post, Prime Minister...
body: India has welcomed the understanding reached between the United States and Iran on ending the conflict in West Asia, which has caused serious economic disruption across the world and led to loss of life in many countries. In a social media post, Prime Minister Narendra Modi said, India hopes that the implementation of this understanding will help restore peace and stability in the region and ensure the freedom of navigation and commerce. The Prime Minister further added that India looks forward to deliberating on the remaining issues reaching a sustainable final agreement.

title: Raja Festival begins across Odisha
summary: The second day of the traditional Raja festival, Raja Sankranti, is being celebrated with great enthusiasm across Odisha today. The festival, which honours womanhood and Mother Earth, has transformed both rural and urban landscapes into vibrant centres of cult...
body: The second day of the traditional Raja festival, Raja Sankranti, is being celebrated with great enthusiasm across Odisha today. The festival, which honours womanhood and Mother Earth, has transformed both rural and urban landscapes into vibrant centres of cultural festivities. Our correspondent reports that people of all age groups are participating in the celebrations with immense joy and devotion. Festivities have scaled up across Odisha on the auspicious occasion of Raja Sankranti, marking the second day of the three-day Raja festival. A festive atmosphere prevails across the state, with homes, villages and public parks resonating with joy and celebration. People of all age groups are participating enthusiastically by wearing new clothes, enjoying traditional delicacies and taking part in various cultural activities. Traditional swings remain a major attraction, drawing large crowds in both rural and urban areas. Special Raja delicacies, including Poda Pitha, Arisa Pitha, Manda and Kakera, along with the customary Raja Paan, continue to be an integral part of the celebrations. Cultural and social organisations are also organising special programmes to mark the occasion. Raja holds deep cultural significance in Odisha as it celebrates womanhood and pays tribute to Mother Earth. It is believed that Mother Earth undergoes her annual menstrual cycle during this period and is therefore accorded rest and rejuvenation. Agricultural activities remain suspended as a mark of respect for nature, reflecting Odisha’s enduring cultural heritage and its close bond with agriculture.

title: Theme of IYD 2026 is Yoga for Healthy Ageing; PM Modi will lead main event in Kolkata
summary: Union AYUSH Minister Prataprao Jadhav today said that the main national event of International Day of Yoga 2026 will be held in Kolkata on June 21, with Prime Minister Narendra Modi leading the celebrations. Addressing the curtain-raiser press conference for I...
body: Union AYUSH Minister Prataprao Jadhav today said that the main national event of International Day of Yoga 2026 will be held in Kolkata on June 21, with Prime Minister Narendra Modi leading the celebrations. Addressing the curtain-raiser press conference for International Day of Yoga (IDY) 2026 in New Delhi, the Minister noted that the theme for International Day of Yoga 2026 is “Yoga for Healthy Ageing”, which addresses one of the most significant challenges of the modern era. Mr Jadhav added that yoga plays a vital role in helping people lead healthy, active, self-reliant, and mentally resilient lives as they age. The Minister underlined that yoga has evolved into a global mass movement under the visionary leadership of Prime Minister Narendra Modi, following the endorsement of India’s proposal by the United Nations General Assembly in 2014. The minister said millions of people across the world have now adopted yoga as an integral part of their daily lives. Further, Mr Jadhav mentioned that this year’s International Yoga Day has received unprecedented support globally. He added that coordinated by the Indian Council for Cultural Relations (ICCR), more than 210 Indian missions abroad are organizing Yoga Day programmes at nearly 2 thousand 5 hundred locations worldwide, demonstrating how yoga has transcended India’s cultural heritage to become a shared global movement for health and wellness. The minister further announced that a special yoga programme was held yesterday, ahead of the main celebrations and it witnessed the participation of over 4 lakh people in a live yoga session, setting a new Guinness World Record. Mr Jadhav said that Ministry of Culture is organizing special IDY 2026 events at hundred iconic and heritage sites across the country. He added that the initiative seeks to connect India’s cultural legacy, historical monuments, and yoga traditions, giving the observance the character of a nationwide festival.

title: FM Sitharaman assures no shortage of foodgrains despite impact of El Nino conditions
summary: Union Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman has said that the country has adequate buffer stock of foodgrains and there will be no food shortage despite the impact of El Nino conditions. Speaking at the Mindmine Summit in New Delhi today, the Finance Minister st...
body: Union Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman has said that the country has adequate buffer stock of foodgrains and there will be no food shortage despite the impact of El Nino conditions. Speaking at the Mindmine Summit in New Delhi today, the Finance Minister stated that the Southwest Monsoon rainfall this year is expected to be below normal. She said, sufficient quantity of fertilizers is available for the Kharif crop season. Ms Sitharaman further informed that the supply of fertilizers for the upcoming Rabi season will commence from November this year.

title: PM Narendra Modi warmly received by Slovakian President Peter Pellegrini at Presidential Palace in Bratislava
summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi was warmly received by Slovakian President Peter Pellegrini at the Presidential Palace in Bratislava. ​During the visit, both leaders watched a special yoga demonstration right in the palace grounds before touring the Panchatantra ...
body: Prime Minister Narendra Modi was warmly received by Slovakian President Peter Pellegrini at the Presidential Palace in Bratislava. ​During the visit, both leaders watched a special yoga demonstration right in the palace grounds before touring the Panchatantra photo exhibition. Outside, a large crowd from the Indian diaspora had gathered near the Presidential Palace, eagerly waiting to catch a glimpse of the Prime Minister Narendra Modi.

title: PM Modi with his Slovak counterpart Robert Fico lay wreath at Tomb of the Unknown Soldier
summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi, along with his Slovak counterpart, Robert Fico, laid a wreath at the Tomb of the Unknown Soldier today. In a social media post, Mr Modi said that this is a tribute to the valour, sacrifice and unwavering spirit of those who gave t...
body: Prime Minister Narendra Modi, along with his Slovak counterpart, Robert Fico, laid a wreath at the Tomb of the Unknown Soldier today. In a social media post, Mr Modi said that this is a tribute to the valour, sacrifice and unwavering spirit of those who gave their lives for Slovakia’s freedom.

title: High Commission of India in Colombo organises roundtable titled “Rupee to Rupee: Strengthening the India-Sri Lanka Commercial Corridor”
summary: The High Commission of India in Colombo today organised a roundtable titled “Rupee to Rupee: Strengthening the India-Sri Lanka Commercial Corridor”, bringing together policymakers, regulators, bankers and business leaders from both countries. The event was att...
body: The High Commission of India in Colombo today organised a roundtable titled “Rupee to Rupee: Strengthening the India-Sri Lanka Commercial Corridor”, bringing together policymakers, regulators, bankers and business leaders from both countries. The event was attended by Indian High Commissioner Santosh Jha, Central Bank of Sri Lanka Governor Dr Nandalal Weerasinghe, senior government and Treasury officials, and representatives of leading banks and businesses. High Commissioner Jha highlighted the strong bilateral trade relationship and urged stakeholders to expand trade settlement in local currencies. Governor Weerasinghe outlined factors that have strengthened economic ties between the two countries and recalled India’s assistance during Sri Lanka’s economic crisis. Discussions focused on INR-LKR trade settlement and recent policy measures allowing authorised dealer banks in Sri Lanka to disburse INR-denominated loans. Industry experts, banking representatives and Treasury officials shared their experiences and views on increasing the use of local currencies in bilateral trade.

title: Vice President releases book named Agatthiyar- The Unifier at Uprashtrapati Bhavan in New Delhi
summary: Vice President CP Radhakrishnan has said that the country’s unity is not a contemporary construct but an ancient civilisational reality nurtured over thousands of years by sages and enlightened thinkers. Releasing a book named Agatthiyar – The Unifier at Upras...
body: Vice President CP Radhakrishnan has said that the country’s unity is not a contemporary construct but an ancient civilisational reality nurtured over thousands of years by sages and enlightened thinkers. Releasing a book named Agatthiyar – The Unifier at Uprashtrapati Bhavan in New Delhi, the Vice President said the publication highlights the enduring legacy of Sage Agatthiyar and the deep-rooted cultural unity that has bound Bharat together for millennia. Speaking on the civilisational unity of Bharat, Mr Radhakrishnan observed that while kings and political institutions are often remembered in discussions on national integration, the true architects of the nation’s unity were its sages and rishis. He said, Agatthiyar stands out as one of the greatest symbols of the country’s cultural and spiritual unity. The Vice President also praised Kalaimagal magazine for bringing out the book. He noted that the magazine has been serving with distinction for more than 95 years, preserving and promoting Tamil literature, culture and heritage for future generations. He recalled that several Tamil scholars, historians, scientists and national leaders had enriched the quality and credibility of the magazine through their writings.

title: DoT, AIIMS Delhi Organise Blood Donation Camp in New Delhi on World Blood Donor Day
summary: Department of Telecommunications, in collaboration with the All-India Institute of Medical Sciences (AIIMS), Delhi, organised a blood donation camp in New Delhi today, to mark World Blood Donor Day. The camp was inaugurated by Principal Controller of Communica...
body: Department of Telecommunications, in collaboration with the All-India Institute of Medical Sciences (AIIMS), Delhi, organised a blood donation camp in New Delhi today, to mark World Blood Donor Day. The camp was inaugurated by Principal Controller of Communication Accounts (Pr. CCA), Delhi, Asha Rungta. Under the slogan One drop of humanity, Give Blood, Save Lives, officers and officials of the Pr. CCA enthusiastically contributed to support voluntary blood donation and augment hospital blood reserves. The AIIMS Blood Bank team managed on-site blood collection, health screening, and donor counselling, adhering to all clinical and biosafety protocols. Donors were provided post-donation refreshments and were observed in a dedicated recovery area to ensure their well-being. The event concluded with a formal presentation by the AIIMS Blood Bank unit, which awarded a certificate of successful completion to the Office of the Principal Controller of Communication Accounts honoring their vital contribution to the noble cause of blood donation.

title: Earthquake of magnitude 3.8 strikes Myanmar
summary: An earthquake of magnitude 3.8 struck Myanmar last night. The National Centre of Seismology (NCS), said the quake occurred at 11:31 pm Indian Standard Time, with its epicentre located at a depth of 10 kilometres. A week earlier, an earthquake of magnitude 3.5 ...
body: An earthquake of magnitude 3.8 struck Myanmar last night. The National Centre of Seismology (NCS), said the quake occurred at 11:31 pm Indian Standard Time, with its epicentre located at a depth of 10 kilometres. A week earlier, an earthquake of magnitude 3.5 hit Myanmar. Myanmar is vulnerable to hazards from moderate and large magnitude earthquakes and tsunamis along its long coastline. Myanmar is wedged between four tectonic plates -the Indian, Eurasian, Sunda, and Burma plates that interact in active geological processes. A 1,400-kilometre transform fault runs through Myanmar and connects the Andaman spreading centre to a collision zone in the north called the Sagaing Fault.

title: FM Nirmala Sitharaman Highlights 12 Years of Economic Transformation Through UPI and Infrastructure Growth
summary: Union Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman has said that initiatives such as UPI, rapid infrastructure development, improved rail and air connectivity, and industrial corridors are transforming India’s economy. Addressing the media in Devanahalli near Bengaluru...
body: Union Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman has said that initiatives such as UPI, rapid infrastructure development, improved rail and air connectivity, and industrial corridors are transforming India’s economy. Addressing the media in Devanahalli near Bengaluru, Ms Sitharaman highlighted NDA government’s achievements of the past 12 years, including railway electrification, the introduction of Vande Bharat trains, and the establishment of new AIIMS and IITs across the country. She added that the income tax exemption limit has been raised from 2.5 lakh to 12.75 lakh rupees to benefit the middle class. The Finance Minister said fluctuations in the rupee are influenced by imports of crude oil, fertilisers and gold, and noted that several global currencies have also faced pressure. She said the Prime Minister has urged citizens to consider reducing gold purchases and fuel consumption and to adopt natural farming to help conserve foreign exchange. She added that Prime Minister Narendra Modi has become the longest-serving elected Prime Minister of India, under whose leadership the country is moving towards the goal of Viksit Bharat by 2047.

title: India, France Hold Talks to Expand Strategic Partnership Across Defence, Technology and Trade
summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi and French President Emmanuel Macron held delegation-level talks at historic Villa Kérylos at Nice in France. The discussions between two sides focused on expanding the Special Global Strategic Partnership between two countries,...
body: Prime Minister Narendra Modi and French President Emmanuel Macron held delegation-level talks at historic Villa Kérylos at Nice in France. The discussions between two sides focused on expanding the Special Global Strategic Partnership between two countries, across key sectors including defense, technology, and trade. Prime Minister Modi was accompanied by the National Security Advisor (NSA) Ajit Doval, External Affairs Minister S Jaishankar, Foreign Secretary Vikram Misri and senior officials. President Macron was accompanied by French Minister for Europe and Foreign Affairs Jean-Noël Barrot and other senior officials. The two leaders also interacted during a walk and talk session at Villa Kerlyos. Earlier in the day, Prime Minister Modi and French President Macron jointly inaugurated Bharat Innovates event at Palais des Expositions in Nice. The three-day landmark event, brings together Indian deep-tech start-ups, innovators, researchers and investors with global innovation funds and footprint. It showcases India’s premier deep technology at the global stage, presenting 120 pathbreaking start-ups and over 20 Institutes of Excellence, across 13 critical technology pillars of global importance. Over 350 top investors and venture capitalists from across the world are also participating in the event. Speaking on the occasion, Prime Minister Modi said Bharat Innovates is emerging as a bridge between India’s talent and European capital, creating new opportunities for young innovators to connect with global expertise. Highlighting India’s startup revolution, Mr Modi said, the country’s youth are developing solutions for global challenges in areas ranging from artificial intelligence and space technology to green energy, advanced manufacturing and defence innovation. He noted that India today has over two lakh startups and is promoting a model of human-centric innovation guided by the principle of Technology for Humanity. Highlighting the strength of India-France ties, Prime Minister Modi said, while many nations engage in trade and strategic cooperation, only a few relationships are guided by a shared vision as well as common interests. He described the India-France partnership as one such enduring relationship, rooted in mutual trust, shared values and a common outlook for the future. Addressing the gathering, French President Macron praised India’s emergence as a global innovation powerhouse and said the question today is no longer whether India is innovating, but who will innovate with India. He highlighted India’s vast pool of engineering talent and lauded country’s achievements such as the Chandrayaan mission as examples of India’s technological excellence. President Macron said Bharat Innovates creates a strong bridge between India’s innovation ecosystem and France’s thriving technology landscape. He described it as an honour for France to host the first edition of the initiative outside India. He also congratulated Prime Minister Modi on becoming the longest-serving elected Prime Minister of India since Independence. External Affairs Minister Dr. S. Jaishankar, Commerce and Industry Minister Piyush Goyal, National Security Advisor Ajit Doval, Principal Scientific Adviser Ajay Kumar Sood, Infosys founder Narayana Murthy and several prominent business leaders were also present on the occasion. Speaking at the event, Union Commerce and Industry Minister Piyush Goyal said, said India is emerging as a trusted and reliable partner at a time of global geopolitical and technological transformation. Highlighting India’s youthful demographic, vast talent pool and thriving innovation ecosystem, he noted that the country now has over 2.3 lakh startups under the leadership of Prime Minister Narendra Modi. Mr Goyal said the 120 innovators participating in the event represent only a fraction of India’s capabilities and invited French businesses to strengthen partnerships by investing, designing, innovating and manufacturing in India for both the domestic and global markets. The Minister stated that India and France have elevated their ties to a Special Global Strategic Partnership, highlighting growing cooperation in innovation and artificial intelligence. Mr Goyal added that the celebration of 2026 as the India-France Year of Innovation provides an ideal backdrop for the initiative. Following the inauguration, Prime Minister Modi and President Macron visited the exhibition and interacted with startup founders, researchers and investors participating in the event. Prime Minister Modi reached French city of Nice last night, marking the beginning of his six-day visit to France and Slovakia. On the second leg of his two-nation tour, Mr Modi will travel to Slovakia tonight.
