title: NTA Releases Admit Cards for NEET UG Re-Examination
summary: The National Testing Agency (NTA) has released the admit cards for the NEET UG re-examination for admission to undergraduate medical education. Candidates can download their admit cards from NTA’s official website, neet.nta.nic.in . The Re-examination is sched...
body: The National Testing Agency (NTA) has released the admit cards for the NEET UG re-examination for admission to undergraduate medical education. Candidates can download their admit cards from NTA’s official website, neet.nta.nic.in . The Re-examination is scheduled to be held on 21st of this month, from 2 PM to 05:15 PM. NTA has advised the Candidates that they should verify their Bank Account details for refund before downloading the Admit Card. It also advised the candidates to go through the instructions contained therein as well as in the Information Bulletin.

title: IMD Forecasts Heavy Rain in Northeast and West Bengal
summary: India Meteorological Department (IMD) has forecast heavy rainfall over parts of North East India and West Bengal tomorrow. IMD said that conditions are favourable for further advance of southwest monsoon into more parts of Maharashtra, some parts of Karnataka,...
body: India Meteorological Department (IMD) has forecast heavy rainfall over parts of North East India and West Bengal tomorrow. IMD said that conditions are favourable for further advance of southwest monsoon into more parts of Maharashtra, some parts of Karnataka, Telangana, Andhra Pradesh, West Bengal, Odisha, Jharkhand, Bihar and South Chhattisgarh during next four to five days. The Weather Agency has issued an orange colour warning for thundersquall conditions over Rajasthan, East Uttar Pradesh, Odisha and West Madhya Pradesh tomorrow. The Met Department further predicted thunderstorm accompanied by lightning and gusty winds over Delhi, Haryana, Chandigarh, Punjab, Himachal Pradeh and Uttarakhand tomorrow. Similar weather conditions are expected over Bihar, Jammu and Kashmir, Jharkhand, Madhya Maharashtra, Tamil Nadu Puducherry, Karaikal, and Andaman and Nicobar Islands. While Heat Wave conditions are expected over Marathwada, hot and humid weather conditions are expected over Konkan and Goa during the next two days. For Delhi, IMD has predicted a partly cloudy sky tomorrow. According to the Weather Agency, the maximum temperature will be around 38 to 40 degrees Celsius in the national capital while the minimum temperature will hover around 25 to 27 degrees Celsius tomorrow.

title: Rebel TMC MPs Meet Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla in Delhi Today
summary: Rebel TMC MPs met Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla at his residence in Delhi today amid ongoing tussle in the Trinamool Congress. The TMC leaders include Kakoli Ghosh, Saayoni Ghosh, Mala Roy and Satabdi Roy. Earlier, they had met BJP leader and Union Minister Bhupe...
body: Rebel TMC MPs met Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla at his residence in Delhi today amid ongoing tussle in the Trinamool Congress. The TMC leaders include Kakoli Ghosh, Saayoni Ghosh, Mala Roy and Satabdi Roy. Earlier, they had met BJP leader and Union Minister Bhupender Yadav. Meanwhile, TMC MPs Kirti Azad and Sagarika Ghose met the Lok Sabha Speaker at his residence and submitted a letter to him. Later, talking to media, Mr Azad said, he told the Speaker that the rebel MPs have no legitimacy and the manner in which they are acting is unconstitutional. TMC leader Abhishek Banerjee has written to the Lok Sabha Speaker urging him to treat Trinamool Congress as a single political party represented in the House solely through its duly authorised Leader and Whip. He urged the Speaker to decline to accord any recognition, status, or facility to any purported separate group or faction of the party.

title: PM Modi thanks Serbian President Aleksandar Vučić for greetings on becoming India’s longest-serving Prime Minister
summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi has expressed gratitude over the greetings of Serbian President Aleksandar Vučić to him over becoming India’s longest-serving Prime Minister. In a social media post, Mr Modi thanked President Vučić for his heartfelt greetings. He a...
body: Prime Minister Narendra Modi has expressed gratitude over the greetings of Serbian President Aleksandar Vučić to him over becoming India’s longest-serving Prime Minister. In a social media post, Mr Modi thanked President Vučić for his heartfelt greetings. He also fondly recalled the Serbian President’s recent visit to India and the productive discussions held during the visit. Mr Modi said India deeply values its friendship with Serbia and expressed confidence that the partnership between the two countries would continue to grow stronger in the years ahead. Earlier, in a social media post, President Aleksandar Vučić extended his greetings to Prime Minister Modi on becoming the longest-serving elected Prime Minister in India’s history. The Serbian President said the milestone reflects the trust reposed by the people in Mr Modi’s leadership and public service. He also described Mr Modi as a respected global leader and expressed confidence that India would continue to achieve new milestones under his leadership.

title: India’s space economy poised to grow from 9 billion dollars to 45 billion dollars in next decade
summary: Union Minister Dr Jitendra Singh today said India’s space economy is poised to grow from the current 8 to 9 billion dollars to nearly 40 to 45 billion dollars over the next decade. Addressing a press conference in New Delhi, the Minister said, this growth is d...
body: Union Minister Dr Jitendra Singh today said India’s space economy is poised to grow from the current 8 to 9 billion dollars to nearly 40 to 45 billion dollars over the next decade. Addressing a press conference in New Delhi, the Minister said, this growth is driven by policy reforms, growing private participation and a rapidly expanding innovation ecosystem. Dr Singh said the transformation of India’s space sector reflects a larger change taking place across the country, where science and technology have moved beyond laboratories to become part of the national consciousness. He said one of the most significant achievements of recent years has been the growing connect between science and society. The Minister said the growing prominence of science and technology in public discourse reflects the vision of Prime Minister Narendra Modi, who has consistently brought science-driven initiatives into the national mainstream. Programmes such as Swachh Bharat, Digital India, Digital Health, Deep Ocean Mission and Gaganyaan have helped position science and innovation at the centre of India’s development journey. Missions such as Chandrayaan-3 have transformed space science into a subject of widespread public interest, creating unprecedented awareness and engagement among citizens across the country. India’s growing capabilities in frontier sectors such as space, atomic energy and advanced technologies have strengthened the country’s global standing. The confidence generated by these achievements has enhanced the credibility of indigenous technologies and reinforced India’s position as a trusted technology partner.

title: All 14 Indian crew members rescued from a vessel off Oman coast after engine failure
summary: Indian Embassy in Oman has said that all 14 Indian crew members from the Indian-flagged mechanised sailing vessel, Virat 1 have been rescued. The vessel had suffered an engine failure off the coast of Oman. In a social media post, the Embassy said that the cre...
body: Indian Embassy in Oman has said that all 14 Indian crew members from the Indian-flagged mechanised sailing vessel, Virat 1 have been rescued. The vessel had suffered an engine failure off the coast of Oman. In a social media post, the Embassy said that the crew members are safe and in good health, and are onboard another vessel, MV Jabal Ali 9. The Embassy added that the crew members are being headed to Mumbai.

title: PM Modi to Begin Two-Day Historic Visit to Slovakia
summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi will arrive in Slovakia later tonight, marking the first-ever visit by an Indian Prime Minister to the country. During his two-day visit, Mr Modi will meet Slovakian President Peter Pelligrini and hold talks with Slovakian Prime Mi...
body: Prime Minister Narendra Modi will arrive in Slovakia later tonight, marking the first-ever visit by an Indian Prime Minister to the country. During his two-day visit, Mr Modi will meet Slovakian President Peter Pelligrini and hold talks with Slovakian Prime Minister Robert Fico in Bratislava. Prime Minister Narendra Modi will arrive in Slovakia tonight after completing the first leg of his visit to France. A ceremonial welcome will be accorded to the Prime Minister on Monday morning. Shri Narendra Modi will meet President Pellegrini and Prime Minister Fico during his visit. The Indian diaspora in the Slovak Republic is highly excited about the maiden visit of an Indian Prime Minister to the country.

title: Union Minister Pralhad Joshi Urges Youth to Drive Atmanirbhar Bharat Vision for Developed India by 2047
summary: Union Minister for Consumer Affairs, Food and Public Distribution Pralhad Joshi has called upon the youth of the country to actively participate in advancing Atmanirbhar Bharat to realise the vision of a developed India by 2047. The Minister made the remarks w...
body: Union Minister for Consumer Affairs, Food and Public Distribution Pralhad Joshi has called upon the youth of the country to actively participate in advancing Atmanirbhar Bharat to realise the vision of a developed India by 2047. The Minister made the remarks while delivering the valedictory address at the CSIR RISE Conclave in Bengaluru. Mr Joshi said Indians have the capability to create world-class products that are globally recognised, citing examples such as UPI, DigiYatra, Akash missile system, the GalaxEye Drishti satellite mission and Zoho applications. The Minister said India should not depend on other countries for critical technologies, as support may be withdrawn at crucial times. He recalled that during the Kargil conflict, GPS access was denied to India by US, which led to the development of the NAVIC satellite navigation system. He added that NAVIC will also help in providing India Standard Time in the future. Mr Joshi said indigenous innovation helps reduce costs and save foreign exchange, and urged the youth to develop India-specific solutions and reduce dependence on imports. The Minister also spoke about the importance of sovereign AI and strengthening manufacturing capabilities. During the event, the Tirumala Tirupati Devasthanams signed an agreement with the Central Food Technological Research Institute (CFTRI) for technology support in quality control and assurance of its prasadams.

title: PM Modi Says Unprecedented Work Done for Farmers’ Welfare and Empowerment Over Past 12 Years
summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi today said, unprecedented work has been done in the last 12 years towards the welfare and empowerment of farmers in the country. Mr Modi said this while sharing an article written by Union Agriculture and Farmers’ Welfare Minister,...
body: Prime Minister Narendra Modi today said, unprecedented work has been done in the last 12 years towards the welfare and empowerment of farmers in the country. Mr Modi said this while sharing an article written by Union Agriculture and Farmers’ Welfare Minister, Shivraj Singh Chouhan. In a social media post, Mr Modi said, the government’s schemes for the farmers have brought significant positive changes in their lives. The Prime Minister added that Mr. Chouhan has elaborated on the transformative journey of India’s agriculture sector in detail in his article. In the article Mr Chouhan emphasized that under the leadership of Prime Minister Modi, agriculture has become an integrated foundation for farmers’ prosperity, risk protection, nutritional security, green technology, and rural development.

title: PM Modi Shares Ayush Minister Prataprao Jadhav’s Article on Benefits of Yoga for Healthy Living
summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi has shared an article written by Ayush Minister Prataprao Jadhav highlighting the importance of Yoga in leading a healthy life. In a social media post, the Prime Minister said the article stresses the need for citizens to remain he...
body: Prime Minister Narendra Modi has shared an article written by Ayush Minister Prataprao Jadhav highlighting the importance of Yoga in leading a healthy life. In a social media post, the Prime Minister said the article stresses the need for citizens to remain healthy, active and productive at all stages of life to build a stronger and more developed nation. He added that the Minister in the article highlighted that Yoga rooted in India’s ancient knowledge, offers a holistic approach to health, serving as a lifelong companion that supports healthy ageing. In his article, Mr Jadhav described Yoga as a scientific approach to maintaining physical and mental fitness and urged people to make it a part of their daily routine for a healthier society.

title: Union Minister Sarbananda Sonowal Highlights ‘Ek Ped Maa Ke Naam’ Campaign
summary: Union Minister for Ports, Shipping and Waterways Sarbananda Sonowal today said, crores of trees are being planted across the country in response to Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s call for environmental balance. Mr Sonowal was speaking at a tree plantation prog...
body: Union Minister for Ports, Shipping and Waterways Sarbananda Sonowal today said, crores of trees are being planted across the country in response to Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s call for environmental balance. Mr Sonowal was speaking at a tree plantation programme at Natun Nagar Girls’ Higher Secondary School in Agartala, Tripura. He stressed the importance of large-scale afforestation in combating pollution. Mr Sonowal said the ‘Ek Ped Maa Ke Naam’ campaign, which encourages citizens to plant a tree in honour of their mothers, is key to building a cleaner and greener India. He also planted a sapling at the school and urged people from all sections of society to actively participate in plantation drives.

title: ISRO, Department of Atomic Energy collaborate on advanced lunar lander technology
summary: Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) Chairman V Narayanan has informed that they have collaborated with the Department of Atomic Energy to develop an advanced artificial heating system that can protect lunar lander from the freezing weather of the Moon. A...
body: Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) Chairman V Narayanan has informed that they have collaborated with the Department of Atomic Energy to develop an advanced artificial heating system that can protect lunar lander from the freezing weather of the Moon. Addressing the CSIR-RISE Conclave in Bengaluru, ISRO chief explained that Chandrayaan 3 lander Vikram fuelled by solar energy, operated only for 14 days on Moon. ISRO will now work with the Atomic Energy Department to develop a lunar lander that can survive on the Moon for up to 200 days. This lander with artificial heaters can survive harsh weather conditions on the Moon. He added that this will help long-duration lunar exploration in future missions. The lunar days and nights last about 14 Earth days each, and heat reaches 121 celsius and temperature dips to minus 129 celsius at night. Speaking further about research partnerships, V Narayanan disclosed that ISRO and the Council of Scientific and Industrial Research, CSIR have identified 40 areas of technological cooperation and 17 among them have already been approved for implementation in the first phase. Similar research partnerships are also forged with the Department of Science and Technology and others in the private sector to develop space medicines for the Gaganyaan human spaceflight mission. ISRO also worked with the Department of Biotechnology on microgravity experiments that were carried out by Group Captain Subhanshu Shukla during the Axiom 4 mission to the International Space Station.

title: US President Trump says deal to end Iran war to be signed today; Strait of Hormuz to be opened
summary: US President Donald Trump has said a deal to end the war with Iran will be signed today and that the strategic Strait of Hormuz will be opened to all immediately afterwards. Trump made this announcement on a social media post. At the same time, Trump kept the ...
body: US President Donald Trump has said a deal to end the war with Iran will be signed today and that the strategic Strait of Hormuz will be opened to all immediately afterwards. Trump made this announcement on a social media post. At the same time, Trump kept the threat of fresh attacks dangling if the deal failed to pan out as expected. He said, hopefully, this process will work out, if it doesn’t, they have the ultimate alternative. The US president also contrasted the proposed agreement with the 2015 Joint Comprehensive Plan of Action. Trump said, his agreement with Iran is a wall to no nuclear weapon. Trump further claimed that Iran’s nuclear programme has been effectively neutralised and said the remaining nuclear material will be disposed of at a later stage. Meanwhile, Iran’s Foreign Ministry spokesperson Esmail Baghaei has said the signing of any agreement is unlikely to take place immediately, stating that it will not happen today and may take place in the coming days. According to Iran’s state broadcaster, Islamic Republic of Iran Broadcasting, Baghaei urged caution over the ongoing process due to inconsistencies from the other side.

title: President Droupadi Murmu extends greetings on harvest festival Raja
summary: President Droupadi Murmu has extended her greetings to the people of the country, particularly the people of Odisha, on the occasion of the harvest festival Raja. In a social media post, the President described Raja as a welcoming festival of the monsoon seaso...
body: President Droupadi Murmu has extended her greetings to the people of the country, particularly the people of Odisha, on the occasion of the harvest festival Raja. In a social media post, the President described Raja as a welcoming festival of the monsoon season that is celebrated to honour the earth, motherhood and the clouds. She said the festival reflects the deep bond between nature and society and is marked by traditional festivities, including pithas, betel leaves and swing games. The President further expressed hope that the spirit of the festival would inspire people to live in harmony with nature and dedicate themselves to nation-building. She also wished happiness, peace and prosperity for the people of Odisha as well as the entire country.

title: Switzerland to vote in referendum on capping population at 10 million
summary: Switzerland is set to vote in a referendum today on limiting its population size. Swiss voters will decide whether to cap the country’s population at 10 million. If the proposal passes, Switzerland would become the first European country to set a limit on its ...
body: Switzerland is set to vote in a referendum today on limiting its population size. Swiss voters will decide whether to cap the country’s population at 10 million. If the proposal passes, Switzerland would become the first European country to set a limit on its population. The current population stands at just over 9 million, up from 8.3 million a decade ago. More than a quarter of its residents are foreign-born. The proposal for a cap was put forward by the country’s largest political grouping, Swiss People’s Party. The proposal could have far-reaching consequences for the economy and Bern’s relations with the European Union.

title: Iran announces schedule for farewell & burial ceremonies of late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei
summary: Iranian authorities have announced the schedule for the farewell, procession and burial ceremonies of the late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. According to Iran’s News Agency, farewell ceremonies for the late Khamenei will be held on July 4 and 5 at the...
body: Iranian authorities have announced the schedule for the farewell, procession and burial ceremonies of the late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. According to Iran’s News Agency, farewell ceremonies for the late Khamenei will be held on July 4 and 5 at the Imam Khomeini Prayer Grounds in Tehran. A procession ceremony is scheduled in Tehran on 6th of next month, followed by ceremonies in the holy city of Qom on 7th of next month and in Mashhad on July 9. He will be buried at the holy shrine of Imam Reza in Mashhad on July 9. Meanwhile, marking one year since the start of the conflict, Iran’s top negotiator and Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf accused the United States and Israel of targeting civilians and children during the war.

title: MoS Kirti Vardhan Singh highlights yoga’s role in holistic health in Tajikistan ahead of International Day of Yoga
summary: External Affairs Minister of State Kirti Vardhan Singh has highlighted yoga’s role in promoting holistic health, mental well-being and harmony with nature ahead of the International Day of Yoga. Participating in a yoga session in Dushanbe, Tajikistan, he said,...
body: External Affairs Minister of State Kirti Vardhan Singh has highlighted yoga’s role in promoting holistic health, mental well-being and harmony with nature ahead of the International Day of Yoga. Participating in a yoga session in Dushanbe, Tajikistan, he said, Yoga reflects the timeless values that continue to inspire millions across the world. Earlier on Friday, Mr Singh met with Sirojiddin Muhriddin, the Minister of Foreign Affairs of Tajikistan, to discuss the deepening of bilateral ties between the two nations. During the meeting, the two leaders conducted a comprehensive review of the entire spectrum of India-Tajikistan bilateral relations. The discussions focused on identifying and expanding avenues for cooperation in areas of mutual interest, further solidifying the longstanding friendship and strategic partnership between both the nations. Mr. Singh extended an invitation to Tajikistan for high-level participation in the inaugural Shanghai Cooperation Organisation (SCO) Civilizational Dialogue. The event is scheduled to be hosted in Kolkata next month, serving as a platform to highlight shared civilizational linkages among SCO member states.

title: MoS Prataprao Jadhav calls for voluntary blood donation on World Blood Donor Day
summary: Minister of State for Health and Family Welfare Prataprao Jadhav has called upon people across the country to support voluntary blood donation on the occasion of World Blood Donor Day today. In a social media post, Mr Jadhav said that every single drop of dona...
body: Minister of State for Health and Family Welfare Prataprao Jadhav has called upon people across the country to support voluntary blood donation on the occasion of World Blood Donor Day today. In a social media post, Mr Jadhav said that every single drop of donated blood has the power to give a new lease of life to someone in need and bring happiness back to a family. The Minister has also urged people to step forward, donate blood responsibly, and play their part in saving precious lives.

title: IMD forecasts heavy rainfall over Northeast, Andaman and Nicobar Islands, Kerala and other regions
summary: India Meteorological Department (IMD) has forecast heavy rainfall over parts of the North Eastern region, Andaman and Nicobar Islands, Kerala, Mahe, Odisha, Tamil Nadu, Puducherry and Karaikal today. According to the IMD, conditions are favourable for the furt...
body: India Meteorological Department (IMD) has forecast heavy rainfall over parts of the North Eastern region, Andaman and Nicobar Islands, Kerala, Mahe, Odisha, Tamil Nadu, Puducherry and Karaikal today. According to the IMD, conditions are favourable for the further advance of the Southwest Monsoon into some more parts of Karnataka, Telangana, Andhra Pradesh, additional parts of Odisha and parts of South Chhattisgarh during the next two to three days. Met Department has also predicted thundersquall activity with wind speeds reaching 60 to 70 kilometres per hour and gusting up to 80 kilometres per hour at isolated places over East Rajasthan today. Meanwhile, heat wave conditions are likely to prevail over parts of Maharashtra today. Hot and humid weather conditions are also expected over Coastal Andhra Pradesh, Konkan, Goa and Odisha till tomorrow. Further, squally weather with wind speeds of 40 to 50 kilometres per hour, gusting up to 60 kilometres per hour, is likely to prevail over the Gulf of Mannar, Comorin area, along and off the Sri Lanka coast, the southwest Bay of Bengal adjoining the southeast and parts of the central Bay of Bengal, along and off the Odisha coast, and over the Andaman Sea today.

title: Brazil open FIFA World Cup 2026 campaign with 1-1 draw against Morocco in Group C
summary: In FIFA World Cup 2026, Brazil opened their campaign with a 1-1 draw against Morocco in a Group C match at MetLife Stadium, New Jersey, US. For Brazil, Vinícius Júnior scored an equalising goal in the 32nd minute. Number six Brazil and seventh-ranked Morocco a...
body: In FIFA World Cup 2026, Brazil opened their campaign with a 1-1 draw against Morocco in a Group C match at MetLife Stadium, New Jersey, US. For Brazil, Vinícius Júnior scored an equalising goal in the 32nd minute. Number six Brazil and seventh-ranked Morocco are the only top 10 teams to meet in the first round of the expanded 48-nation World Cup tournament. Earlier, the Group B clash between Qatar and Switzerland at the San Francisco Bay Area Stadium in California, United States, ended in a 1-1 draw. Switzerland took the lead with the opening goal, but Qatar struck back late in the match to level the score and earn a share of the points. Meanwhile, the match between Haiti and Scotland is currently underway. In other fixtures today, Australia will take on Turkiye in Group D at 9:30 AM IST, while Germany will face Curacao in Group E at 10:30 PM IST.

title: Indian Embassy in Oman Coordinates Repatriation of Indian Sailor’s Mortal Remains
summary: Indian Embassy in Muscat, Oman today said it remains in active coordination with local Omani authorities, port officials and the shipping company regarding Indian national Nishanth Uirthanathan, who died onboard MT Celestial due to medical conditions. The Emba...
body: Indian Embassy in Muscat, Oman today said it remains in active coordination with local Omani authorities, port officials and the shipping company regarding Indian national Nishanth Uirthanathan, who died onboard MT Celestial due to medical conditions. The Embassy said in a social media post that the vessel is expected to berth soon at Duqm port. It added that necessary arrangements have been made for prompt recovery of the mortal remains from the vessel. The Mission is in touch with the family and facilitating all formalities for the earliest repatriation of the mortal remains to India.

title: India to begin ICC Women’s T20 World Cup campaign against Pakistan at Edgbaston today
summary: In the ICC Women’s T20 World Cup, India will begin their campaign against arch-rivals Pakistan in a Group A fixture at Edgbaston in Birmingham today. The match will start at 7 PM Indian Standard Time. India holds a strong record against Pakistan in Women’s T20...
body: In the ICC Women’s T20 World Cup, India will begin their campaign against arch-rivals Pakistan in a Group A fixture at Edgbaston in Birmingham today. The match will start at 7 PM Indian Standard Time. India holds a strong record against Pakistan in Women’s T20 Internationals, winning 13 of their 16 encounters. In Another Clash today, debutants Netherlands will take on Bangladesh. The match will start at 10:30 AM Indian Standard Time. Earlier, West Indies beat defending champions New Zealand by 7 wickets in a Group B clash in Southampton, England today. New Zealand posted 162 for the loss of 6 wickets. Chasing the target, West Indies reached their target with a ball to spare as Shemaine Campbelle finished unbeaten on 90. In another match, Australia began their T20 World Cup campaign, registering a 65-run victory over South Africa in group A clash at Old Trafford, in Manchester yesterday. Australia battled to put up 172 for the loss of 8 wickets. Chasing the target, the Proteas posted 107 in 16.4 overs. Georgia Wareham was judged Player of the Match for her all-round display, striking 32 off 22 balls. In Group B action, Scotland defeated Ireland by 40 runs at Old Trafford in Manchester yesterday. Batting first, Scotland scored 161 for 5 in 20 overs. Ireland were then bowled out for 121 in 19.1 overs. Scotland captain Kathryn Bryce was named Player of the Match for her all-round performance, scoring 60 runs and taking two wickets. Earlier in the tournament opener, hosts England beat Sri Lanka by 87 runs at Edgbaston on Friday. The month-long competition is being held across England and Wales, with the final scheduled at Lord’s Cricket Ground on the 5th of next month.

title: Israel Attacks Hezbollah Targets in Beirut
summary: The Israeli military today announced that it launched strike on Lebanese capital targeting infrastructure of Iran-backed armed group Hezbollah. Smoke could be seen rising over the capital city Beirut. The office of Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu sai...
body: The Israeli military today announced that it launched strike on Lebanese capital targeting infrastructure of Iran-backed armed group Hezbollah. Smoke could be seen rising over the capital city Beirut. The office of Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said the strikes were in response to Hezbollah attacks in northern Israel. When Israel last struck the Beirut suburbs a week ago, Iran responded with strikes on Israel. Tehran, which is Hezbollah’s main backer, has insisted that any US-Iran ceasefire deal must also include an end to Israeli strikes in Lebanon.

title: India defeat Afghanistan by seven wickets in rain-affected first ODI in Dharamshala
summary: In Men’s Cricket, India defeated Afghanistan by seven wickets in a rain-affected first One Day International of the three-match series in Dharamshala, Himachal Pradesh, last night. Chasing 195 runs, India overhauled the target in 22.5 overs. Indian Skipper Shu...
body: In Men’s Cricket, India defeated Afghanistan by seven wickets in a rain-affected first One Day International of the three-match series in Dharamshala, Himachal Pradesh, last night. Chasing 195 runs, India overhauled the target in 22.5 overs. Indian Skipper Shubman Gill scored unbeaten 84 off 66 balls. Earlier, put into bat, Afghanistan were bowled out for 194 in 24.5 overs. Shubman Gill was adjudged player of the match. For Afghanistan, Rahmanullah Gurbaz smashed a brilliant century, scoring 102 off 51 balls. For India, debutant Harsh Dubey and Gurnoor Brar clinched three wickets each. The match was reduced to 25 overs-per-side after relentless rains delayed the start. India won the toss and elected to field first. The 2nd ODI between India and Afghanistan is scheduled for 17th June at the Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium in Lucknow.

title: Union Minister Jyotiraditya Scindia announces launch of drone-based mail transmission service in Himachal Pradesh
summary: Union Minister of Communications Jyotiraditya Scindia has announced the launch of drone-based mail transmission service by Department of Posts in Himachal Pradesh. The service has been started between Mandi Head Post Office and Rehardhar Branch Post Office in ...
body: Union Minister of Communications Jyotiraditya Scindia has announced the launch of drone-based mail transmission service by Department of Posts in Himachal Pradesh. The service has been started between Mandi Head Post Office and Rehardhar Branch Post Office in the State. The initiative builds upon feedback received during the interactions of Union Minister with Gramin Dak Sevaks and Postal Assistants, where the need for faster and more efficient connectivity in remote and difficult terrains was highlighted. The Ministry of Communications has said that the Department of Posts has planned deployment of drone-based mail transmission across around 150 identified routes in Himachal Pradesh and Assam over the next two to three months. The Ministry added that Rehardhar Branch Post Office, located about 12 kilometres from Mandi Head Post Office, currently takes over two hours to connect through conventional arrangements. It added that with drone-based transmission, the transit time has been reduced to just about seven minutes.

title: Government Debunks Fake WhatsApp Message Claiming Free Laptops Under National Student Laptop Scheme
summary: The Government has debunked a WhatsApp message circulating online that falsely claims applications are being accepted for free laptops under the National Student Laptop Scheme 2026. The Fact Check Unit of the Press Information Bureau (PIB) has clarified that t...
body: The Government has debunked a WhatsApp message circulating online that falsely claims applications are being accepted for free laptops under the National Student Laptop Scheme 2026. The Fact Check Unit of the Press Information Bureau (PIB) has clarified that this claim is fake and that the Government has not announced any such scheme. PIB cautioned that such messages are often designed to collect personal or financial information from unsuspecting individuals. It advised citizens not to click on unknown links and urged them to verify information through official sources before sharing or forwarding such messages.

title: INS Sharda Successfully Concludes Visit to Colombo, Sri Lanka
summary: INS Sharda has successfully concluded its visit to Colombo Sri Lanka. The Ministry of Defence said, the visit further strengthened maritime cooperation and friendship between India and Sri Lanka. The visit featured a series of professional, training, and sport...
body: INS Sharda has successfully concluded its visit to Colombo Sri Lanka. The Ministry of Defence said, the visit further strengthened maritime cooperation and friendship between India and Sri Lanka. The visit featured a series of professional, training, and sporting engagements with the Sri Lankan Navy. During the visit, Commander Western Naval Area, Sri Lanka Navy, RAdm Jagath Kumara visited the ship and interacted with the crew, reaffirming the strong maritime partnership between the two nations. The Ministry informed that as part of capacity-building initiatives, Sri Lankan Navy personnel participated in small arms handling, basic firefighting, first aid, and rescue procedures. It added that friendly sports fixtures, professional exchanges, and cross-deck visits further enhanced interoperability and strengthened bonds between the two services. During the port call, the Commanding Officer of INS Sharda paid a courtesy call on High Commissioner of India to Sri Lanka Santosh Jha. The Ministry further said that the visit underscored the enduring maritime partnership between India and Sri Lanka and reaffirmed their shared commitment to regional maritime cooperation and security in line with the vision of MAHASAGAR.

title: PM Modi’s 12 years transform Ease of Living through reforms & digital empowerment
summary: India has undergone a remarkable transformation, driven by the principles of Sabka Saath, Sabka Vikas, Sabka Vishwas and Sabka Prayas under the visionary leadership of Prime Minister Narendra Modi in the last 12 years. During this period, the Modi government c...
body: India has undergone a remarkable transformation, driven by the principles of Sabka Saath, Sabka Vikas, Sabka Vishwas and Sabka Prayas under the visionary leadership of Prime Minister Narendra Modi in the last 12 years. During this period, the Modi government carried out many flagship initiatives for the welfare and empowerment of the poor, farmers, women and youth. Akashvani News brings you a special feature, 12 Saal– Vishwas Ke, Vikas Ke, Jankalyan ke. Today, we focus on the steps taken by the government to bring Ease of Living in the lives of the country’s citizens. Twelve years of reforms have become twelve years of trust. Whether it is GST, faceless tax, or Digital India, every step has made citizens feel that they too are nation-builders. Comprehensive revisions to income, tax slabs, and rates in the Union Budget 25-26 have been designed to benefit all taxpayers. No income tax is payable under the new tax regime on an annual income of up to 12 lakh rupees. 88% of taxpayers earn up to 12 lakh rupees and are receiving direct benefits. The number of income tax filers rose from 6.9 crores in the financial year 2022 to 9.2 crores in Financial Year 2025. Next generation GST has simplified taxation and made life easier for citizens. The tax structure is now primarily based on 5% and 18% slabs, resulting in a broader taxpayer base. GST collections reached 2.42 lakh crore rupees in April this year, the highest level ever recorded. Talking to Akashvani News, Film Producer and Distributor, Sameer Dixit said GST reforms are one of the biggest policy decisions. Narendra Modi government has embraced digital technology and has leveraged this technology within both the realms of politics and policy making. 5G Services have reached 99.6% of the country’s districts in just 22 months. The cost of internet data has dropped by 97.07%, making internet more affordable and accessible. 7.22 lakh kilometres of optical fibre cable have been laid under BharatNet scheme. India is poised to become a global leader in 6G technology. The goal is to contribute approximately 10% to global patents related to 6G. Talking to Akashvani News, Mahendra Tiwari, CEO of Ethernet Xpress said Digital India is helping people living in remote areas and people who are at the bottom of the pyramid. Over the past twelve years, NDA government has envisioned the nation’s dreams on a grand scale and worked to ensure saturation of programmes designed to enhance public amenities. Ease of living is no longer just a phrase, it has become the government’s guiding mantra.

title: Embassy of India in Abu Dhabi announces change in outsourced service provider for passport & visa services in UAE
summary: The Embassy of India in Abu Dhabi has announced a change in the outsourced service provider handling Indian passport, visa and consular services across the United Arab Emirates. According to an official statement, the current service providers, BLS Internation...
body: The Embassy of India in Abu Dhabi has announced a change in the outsourced service provider handling Indian passport, visa and consular services across the United Arab Emirates. According to an official statement, the current service providers, BLS International Services Limited and SGIVS Global, will continue to accept and process applications until June 30, 2026. Following the completion of a tendering and selection process, Al Hind Tours and Travels LLC has been appointed as the new outsourced service provider for Indian passport, visa and consular services in the UAE. The transition will take effect from July 1, 2026. From that date, all applications for Indian passport, visa and consular services will be accepted through designated centres operated by Al Hind Tours and Travels. Applicants have been advised to continue using existing BLS International and SGIVS Global centres for all applications submitted before July 1 and to follow official announcements for updates regarding the transition. The Embassy also urged members of the Indian community and other applicants to rely only on official websites and verified social media platforms for accurate and up-to-date information. It said the Embassy and the Consulate remain committed to ensuring a smooth transition while maintaining efficient, accessible and high-quality consular services throughout the UAE.

title: Gold and Silver Prices Remain Under Pressure Amid Inflation Concerns, ECB Rate Hike
summary: In the bullion market, gold and silver prices faced sustained pressure during the week, weighed down by hotter-than-expected US inflation data and the European Central Bank’s decision to raise interest rates, which reinforced expectations of a prolonged rate-h...
body: In the bullion market, gold and silver prices faced sustained pressure during the week, weighed down by hotter-than-expected US inflation data and the European Central Bank’s decision to raise interest rates, which reinforced expectations of a prolonged rate-hike cycle. However, on the final trading day of the week, precious metals rebounded after US President Donald Trump called off planned airstrikes on Iran. Optimism surrounding a possible peace deal between Washington and Tehran sparked a strong relief rally. Despite the sharp gains on the last trading day, gold and silver prices were unable to offset their earlier losses and still ended the week in negative territory. At the Multi Commodity Exchange, Gold futures for the August contract shed 3.2 per cent during the week to settle at 1,50,528 rupees per 10 grams. Meanwhile, Silver futures for the July contract dropped nearly one per cent over the week to close at 2,46,186 rupees per kilogram. At the Commodity Exchange (COMEX), Gold futures for the August contract slipped 2.9 per cent during the week to settle at 4,239 dollars per troy ounce. Silver futures for the July contract lost 1.6 per cent over the week to end at 67.97 dollars per troy ounce.

title: PM Modi Leading Nation Towards Viksit Bharat Vision: MoS Shobha Karandlaje
summary: Union Minister of State for Labour and Employment, Shobha Karandlaje, has said that Prime Minister Narendra Modi is leading the country towards the goal of a Viksit Bharat. The Minister was speaking after offering prayers at Tirumala Sri Venkateswara Temple in...
body: Union Minister of State for Labour and Employment, Shobha Karandlaje, has said that Prime Minister Narendra Modi is leading the country towards the goal of a Viksit Bharat. The Minister was speaking after offering prayers at Tirumala Sri Venkateswara Temple in Andhra Pradesh along with her family. Speaking to the media after her visit to the temple, Ms. Karandlaje said, the visit coincided with Prime Minister Narendra Modi completing 12 years in office and becoming the longest consecutively serving elected Prime Minister of the country. The Minister praised Mr Modi’s leadership and said she prayed for the continued welfare of the country and for his guidance in the years ahead.

title: PM Modi to hold talks with French President Emmanuel Macron and jointly inaugurate ‘Bharat Innovates’ event in Nice today
summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi arrived in Nice, France, last night on the first leg of his two-nation visit to France and Slovakia. During his stay in France, he will join French President Emmanuel Macron in jointly inaugurating Bharat Innovates and hold bilater...
body: Prime Minister Narendra Modi arrived in Nice, France, last night on the first leg of his two-nation visit to France and Slovakia. During his stay in France, he will join French President Emmanuel Macron in jointly inaugurating Bharat Innovates and hold bilateral talks with him on key areas of cooperation and global issues ahead of the upcoming G7 Summit. The Prime Minister will subsequently travel to Slovakia for bilateral engagements with the Slovak leadership. At a community reception held at the iconic Hotel Negresco yesterday, a large gathering of the Indian diaspora accorded a warm and enthusiastic welcome to the Prime Minister. Prime Minister Modi interacted warmly with members of the community and witnessed colourful cultural performances featuring classical Indian dance forms, including Kathak, Odissi and Bharatanatyam, presented by French and Italian artists. The vibrant reception reflected the strong cultural and people-to-people ties between India and France. Prime Minister Narendra Modi will today join French President Emmanuel Macron for the inauguration of Bharat Innovates at the Palais des Expositions in Nice. The event will showcase India’s startup and innovation ecosystem, with around 120 startups presenting innovations to global investors and technology leaders. Following the event, Prime Minister Modi and President Macron will hold bilateral talks at Villa Kerylos. The meeting will be the first since India and France elevated their ties to a Special Global Strategic Partnership earlier this year. The two leaders are expected to discuss cooperation in defence, technology, trade and innovation, besides exchanging views on key regional and global developments. The Prime Minister will later depart for Slovakia for the second leg of his visit.

title: IMD forecasts further advancement of southwest monsoon into Karnataka, Telangana, Andhra Pradesh and Odisha
summary: The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has forecast favourable conditions for further advancement of southwest monsoon into remaining parts of Karnataka, Telangana, Andhra Pradesh, some more parts of Odisha and some parts of South Chhattisgarh during next 2...
body: The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has forecast favourable conditions for further advancement of southwest monsoon into remaining parts of Karnataka, Telangana, Andhra Pradesh, some more parts of Odisha and some parts of South Chhattisgarh during next 2-3 days. IMD predicted heavy rainfall over parts of the North Eastern region, Andaman and Nicobar Islands, Kerala, Mahe, Odisha, Tamil Nadu, Puducherry and Karaikal today. The weather agency said that thundersquall conditions are likely to prevail over Madhya Pradesh, Telangana and West Rajasthan till tomorrow. It added that Andhra Pradesh, Bihar, Chhattisgarh, Himachal Pradesh, Jammu and Kashmir and Ladakh, Jharkhand, Maharashtra, Uttarakhand, Interior Karnataka and West Bengal will witness thunderstorms accompanied with lightning and gusty winds today. Meanwhile, heat wave conditions are likely to prevail over parts of Maharashtra today. It added that Hot and humid weather conditions are also expected over Coastal Andhra Pradesh, Konkan and Goa till tomorrow.

title: US President Trump says Iran deal to be signed today; Strait of Hormuz to be opened
summary: US President Donald Trump has said a deal aimed at ending the conflict with Iran is scheduled to be signed today, adding that the strategic Strait of Hormuz would be open to all immediately afterwards. His comments came hours after Pakistan indicated that the ...
body: US President Donald Trump has said a deal aimed at ending the conflict with Iran is scheduled to be signed today, adding that the strategic Strait of Hormuz would be open to all immediately afterwards. His comments came hours after Pakistan indicated that the United States and Iran were in the final stage of negotiations and that an electronic signing ceremony was expected to take place today. In a post on Truth Social, Trump said the agreement would mark a major step forward, claiming that Iran no longer wants a Nuclear Weapon and suggesting that the United States would work with Tehran to remove enriched uranium at an appropriate time. He also said relations between the two countries would become different and better if the process moved ahead smoothly. However, there has been no immediate confirmation from Iran on the reported timeline. Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesperson Esmaeil Baqaei reportedly denied that a memorandum of understanding between Tehran and Washington would be signed today, citing what he described as the other side’s hesitation. Baqaei also stressed that any such memorandum would only provide a framework for further discussions and should not be viewed as a final agreement. Meanwhile, protesters gathered in several Iranian cities yesterday to oppose a proposed peace agreement between Iran and the United States. The demonstrations were largely directed at Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi and Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf, with critics accusing them of making excessive concessions during negotiations with Washington. According to reports, dozens of protesters assembled outside a Foreign Ministry office in Mashhad shortly after Araghchi discussed the proposed agreement in a televised interview. Similar demonstrations were also reported in Tehran and other parts of the country, reflecting growing opposition among hardline groups to the ongoing diplomatic efforts.
